revision.text:
{{日本の古墳 |名称=梅山古墳 |画像=[[画像:UmeyamaKohun-01.jpg|250px]]
南西方向より見た梅山古墳 |所在地=奈良県明日香村平田 |位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|28|7|N|135|48|3|E|scale:20000}} |形状=[[前方後円墳]] |規模=全長140m |埋葬施設= |築造年代=古墳時代後期 |被葬者= |出土品= |史跡指定= |特記事項=欽明天皇陵（宮内庁指定） }} '''梅山古墳'''（うめやまこふん）は[[奈良県]][[高市郡]][[明日香村]]にある[[古墳]]。[[宮内庁]]によって[[欽明天皇]]陵（檜隈坂合陵）に指定されている。 == 概要 == 墳丘は前方部を西に向け東西に正確に主軸をとった[[前方後円墳]]で、全長約140m 後円部径72mで、明日香村内では最大の古墳である。非常に多くの[[葺石]]があることでも知られている。現在は回りに水を湛えた周濠を持っているが、これは文久の修復で大幅に改築されたものであり元は田であった。なお、この修復の際に双円墳から前方後円墳に改造されたとする考えもある[[森浩一]]による説。 同古墳と[[平田岩屋古墳]]、[[鬼の爼・鬼の雪隠|鬼の俎・雪隠]]古墳、[[野口王墓]]（天武・持統合葬陵）は同じ丘陵の南側の地形を利用して造られており、4つの古墳が東西に並んでいる。 == 築造時期 == [[古墳時代]]後期の築造とみられているが、発掘調査が行われていないため正確な築造時期を推定することは難しい。 == 埋葬者 == 宮内庁によって欽明天皇陵とされているが、梅山古墳から北西に約800m行った所にある奈良県下最大の古墳の[[見瀬丸山古墳]]を欽明天皇陵とする学説も多い。梅山古墳の埋葬者については[[蘇我稲目]]とする考えもある。 == 猿石 == 隣接する吉備姫王墓に[[猿石]]と呼ばれる石像物が置かれているが、これは江戸時代に梅山古墳のすぐ南の田（小字池田）で掘り出されて古墳のかたわらに置かれていたもので、明治初期に現在の場所に移された。 == 関連項目 == *[[日本の古墳一覧]] *[[天皇陵]] == 脚注 ==
{{DEFAULTSORT:うめやまこふん}} [[Category:奈良県の古墳]] [[Category:奈良県の歴史]] [[Category:日本の陵墓]] [[Category:明日香村]]

{{日本の古墳 |名称=梅山古墳 |画像=[[画像:UmeyamaKohun-01.jpg|250px]]

{{日本の古墳 |名称=梅山古墳 |画像=[[画像:UmeyamaKohun-01.jpg|250px]]
南西方向より見た梅山古墳 |所在地=奈良県明日香村平田 |位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|28|7|N|135|48|3|E|scale:20000}} |形状=[[前方後円墳]] |規模=全長140m |埋葬施設= |築造年代=古墳時代後期 |被葬者=[[欽明天皇]]（[[宮内庁]]治定） |出土品= |史跡指定=宮内庁治定（檜隈坂合陵） |特記事項= }} '''梅山古墳'''（うめやまこふん）は[[奈良県]][[高市郡]][[明日香村]]にある[[古墳]]。[[宮内庁]]によって[[欽明天皇]]陵（檜隈坂合陵）に指定されている。 == 概要 == [[ファイル:Umeyama kohun aerial.jpg|thumb|320px|左下に吉備姫皇女王墓・猿石（{{国土航空写真}}）]] 墳丘は前方部を西に向け東西に正確に主軸をとった[[前方後円墳]]で、全長約140m 後円部径72mで、明日香村内では最大の古墳である。非常に多くの[[葺石]]があることでも知られている。現在は回りに水を湛えた周濠を持っているが、これは文久の修復で大幅に改築されたものであり元は田であった。なお、この修復の際に双円墳から前方後円墳に改造されたとする考えもある[[森浩一]]による説。 同古墳と[[平田岩屋古墳]]、[[鬼の爼・鬼の雪隠|鬼の俎・雪隠]]古墳、[[野口王墓]]（天武・持統合葬陵）は同じ丘陵の南側の地形を利用して造られており、4つの古墳が東西に並んでいる。 == 築造時期 == [[古墳時代]]後期の築造とみられているが、発掘調査が行われていないため正確な築造時期を推定することは難しい。 == 埋葬者 == 宮内庁によって欽明天皇陵とされているが、梅山古墳から北西に約800m行った所にある奈良県下最大の古墳の[[見瀬丸山古墳]]を欽明天皇陵とする学説も多い。梅山古墳の埋葬者については[[蘇我稲目]]とする考えもある。 == 猿石 == 隣接する[[吉備姫王]]墓に[[猿石]]と呼ばれる石像物が置かれているが、これは江戸時代に梅山古墳のすぐ南の田（小字池田）で掘り出されて古墳のかたわらに置かれていたもので、明治初期に現在の場所に移された。 == 関連項目 == *[[日本の古墳一覧]] *[[天皇陵]] == 脚注 ==

== 外部リンク == *[http://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/guide/029/index.html 檜隈坂合陵] - 宮内庁 *[http://goryou.fc2web.com/goryou03/029.html 欽明天皇陵参拝案内] {{DEFAULTSORT:うめやまこふん}} [[Category:奈良県の古墳]] [[Category:奈良県の歴史]] [[Category:日本の陵墓]] [[Category:明日香村]]

{{日本の古墳 |名称=梅山古墳 |画像=[[画像:UmeyamaKohun-01.jpg|250px]]
南西方向より見た梅山古墳 |所在地=奈良県明日香村平田 |位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|28|7|N|135|48|3|E|scale:20000}} |形状=[[前方後円墳]] |規模=墳丘長140m |埋葬施設= |築造年代=古墳時代後期 |被葬者=第29代[[欽明天皇]]（[[宮内庁]]治定） |出土品= |史跡指定=[[宮内庁]]治定（檜隈坂合陵） |特記事項= }} '''梅山古墳'''（うめやまこふん）は、[[奈良県]][[高市郡]][[明日香村]]にある[[前方後円墳]]。 [[宮内庁]]により「'''檜隈坂合陵'''（ひのくまのさかあいのみささぎ）」として第29代[[欽明天皇]]の[[天皇陵|陵]]に治定されている。 == 概要 == [[ファイル:Umeyama kohun aerial.jpg|thumb|320px|左下に吉備姫皇女王墓・猿石（{{国土航空写真}}）]] 墳丘は前方部を西に向け東西に正確に主軸をとった[[前方後円墳]]で、全長約140m 後円部径72mで、明日香村内では最大の古墳である。非常に多くの[[葺石]]があることでも知られている。現在は回りに水を湛えた周濠を持っているが、これは文久の修復で大幅に改築されたものであり元は田であった。なお、この修復の際に双円墳から前方後円墳に改造されたとする考えもある[[森浩一]]による説。 同古墳と[[平田岩屋古墳]]、[[鬼の爼・鬼の雪隠|鬼の俎・雪隠]]古墳、[[野口王墓]]（天武・持統合葬陵）は同じ丘陵の南側の地形を利用して造られており、4つの古墳が東西に並んでいる。 == 築造時期 == [[古墳時代]]後期の築造とみられているが、発掘調査が行われていないため正確な築造時期を推定することは難しい。 == 埋葬者 == 宮内庁によって欽明天皇陵とされているが、梅山古墳から北西に約800m行った所にある奈良県下最大の古墳の[[見瀬丸山古墳]]を欽明天皇陵とする学説も多い。梅山古墳の埋葬者については[[蘇我稲目]]とする考えもある。 == 猿石 == 隣接する[[吉備姫王]]墓に[[猿石]]と呼ばれる石像物が置かれているが、これは江戸時代に梅山古墳のすぐ南の田（小字池田）で掘り出されて古墳のかたわらに置かれていたもので、明治初期に現在の場所に移された。 == 関連項目 == *[[日本の古墳一覧]] *[[天皇陵]] == 脚注 ==
== 外部リンク == *[http://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/guide/029/index.html 檜隈坂合陵] - 宮内庁 *[http://goryou.fc2web.com/goryou03/029.html 欽明天皇陵参拝案内] {{DEFAULTSORT:うめやまこふん}} [[Category:奈良県の古墳]] [[Category:奈良県の歴史]] [[Category:日本の陵墓]] [[Category:明日香村]]

revision.comment: 陵墓に係る自身の編集を修正、および陵墓項の序文表記統一。
revision.text:
{{日本の古墳 |名称=梅山古墳 |画像=[[画像:UmeyamaKohun-01.jpg|250px]]
南西方向より見た梅山古墳 |所在地=[[奈良県]][[高市郡]][[明日香村]]平田 |位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|28|7.38|N|135|48|2.80|E|region:JP-29_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=梅山古墳（伝欽明天皇陵）}} |形状=[[前方後円墳]] |規模=墳丘長140m |埋葬施設= |築造年代=[[古墳時代]]後期 |被葬者=（[[宮内庁]]治定）第29代[[欽明天皇]] |出土品= |史跡指定=[[宮内庁]]治定「檜隈坂合陵」 |特記事項= }} '''梅山古墳'''（うめやまこふん）は、[[奈良県]][[高市郡]][[明日香村]]にある[[古墳]]。形状は[[前方後円墳]]。 実際の被葬者は明らかでないが、[[宮内庁]]により「'''檜隈坂合陵'''（ひのくまのさかあいのみささぎ）」として第29代[[欽明天皇]]の[[天皇陵|陵]]に治定されている。 == 概要 == [[ファイル:Umeyama kohun aerial.jpg|thumb|320px|左下に吉備姫皇女王墓・猿石（{{国土航空写真}}）]] 墳丘は前方部を西に向け東西に正確に主軸をとった[[前方後円墳]]で、全長約140m 後円部径72mで、明日香村内では最大の古墳である。非常に多くの[[葺石]]があることでも知られている。現在は回りに水を湛えた周濠を持っているが、これは文久の修復で大幅に改築されたものであり元は田であった。なお、この修復の際に双円墳から前方後円墳に改造されたとする考えもある[[森浩一]]による説。 同古墳と[[平田岩屋古墳]]、[[鬼の爼・鬼の雪隠|鬼の俎・雪隠]]古墳、[[野口王墓]]（天武・持統合葬陵）は同じ丘陵の南側の地形を利用して造られており、4つの古墳が東西に並んでいる。 == 築造時期 == [[古墳時代]]後期の築造とみられているが、発掘調査が行われていないため正確な築造時期を推定することは難しい。 == 埋葬者 == 宮内庁によって欽明天皇陵とされているが、梅山古墳から北西に約800m行った所にある奈良県下最大の古墳の[[見瀬丸山古墳]]を欽明天皇陵とする学説も多い。梅山古墳の埋葬者については[[蘇我稲目]]とする考えもある。 == 猿石 == 隣接する[[吉備姫王]]墓に[[猿石]]と呼ばれる石像物が置かれているが、これは江戸時代に梅山古墳のすぐ南の田（小字池田）で掘り出されて古墳のかたわらに置かれていたもので、明治初期に現在の場所に移された。 == 脚注 ==

墳丘全景（左に前方部、右奥に後円部） |所在地=[[奈良県]][[高市郡]][[明日香村]]平田 |位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|28|7.38|N|135|48|2.80|E|region:JP-29_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=梅山古墳（伝欽明天皇陵）}} |形状=[[前方後円墳]] |規模=墳丘長140m |埋葬施設= |築造年代=[[古墳時代]]後期 |被葬者=（[[宮内庁]]治定）第29代[[欽明天皇]] |出土品= |史跡指定=[[宮内庁]]治定「檜隈坂合陵」 |特記事項= }} [[ファイル:Umeyama kohun aerial.jpg|thumb|220px|左下に吉備姫皇女王墓・猿石（{{国土航空写真}}）]] [[File:Umeyama Kofun, haisho.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|[[欽明天皇]]檜隈坂合陵 拝所}}]] '''梅山古墳'''（うめやまこふん）は、[[奈良県]][[高市郡]][[明日香村]]にある[[古墳]]。形状は[[前方後円墳]]。 実際の被葬者は明らかでないが、[[宮内庁]]により「'''檜隈坂合陵'''（ひのくまのさかあいのみささぎ）」として第29代[[欽明天皇]]の[[天皇陵|陵]]に治定されている。 == 概要 == 墳丘は前方部を西に向け東西に正確に主軸をとった[[前方後円墳]]で、全長約140m 後円部径72mで、明日香村内では最大の古墳である。非常に多くの[[葺石]]があることでも知られている。現在は回りに水を湛えた周濠を持っているが、これは文久の修復で大幅に改築されたものであり元は田であった。なお、この修復の際に双円墳から前方後円墳に改造されたとする考えもある[[森浩一]]による説。 同古墳と[[平田岩屋古墳]]、[[鬼の爼・鬼の雪隠|鬼の俎・雪隠]]古墳、[[野口王墓]]（天武・持統合葬陵）は同じ丘陵の南側の地形を利用して造られており、4つの古墳が東西に並んでいる。 == 築造時期 == [[古墳時代]]後期の築造とみられているが、発掘調査が行われていないため正確な築造時期を推定することは難しい。 == 埋葬者 == 宮内庁によって欽明天皇陵とされているが、梅山古墳から北西に約800m行った所にある奈良県下最大の古墳の[[見瀬丸山古墳]]を欽明天皇陵とする学説も多い。梅山古墳の埋葬者については[[蘇我稲目]]とする考えもある。 == 猿石 == 隣接する[[吉備姫王]]墓に[[猿石]]と呼ばれる石像物が置かれているが、これは江戸時代に梅山古墳のすぐ南の田（小字池田）で掘り出されて古墳のかたわらに置かれていたもので、明治初期に現在の場所に移された。 == 脚注 ==
== 外部リンク == {{Commonscat|Umeyama Kofun}} *[http://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/guide/029/index.html 檜隈坂合陵] - 宮内庁 {{DEFAULTSORT:うめやまこふん}} [[Category:奈良県の古墳]] [[Category:明日香村の歴史]] [[Category:前方後円墳]] [[Category:日本の陵墓]]

== 外部リンク == {{Commonscat|Umeyama Kofun}} *[http://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/guide/029/index.html 檜隈坂合陵] - 宮内庁 {{DEFAULTSORT:うめやまこふん}} [[Category:奈良県の古墳]] [[Category:明日香村の歴史]] [[Category:前方後円墳]] [[Category:日本の陵墓]]

{{日本の古墳 |名称=梅山古墳 |画像=[[File:Umeyama Kofun, zenkei-2.jpg|280px]]
墳丘と周濠
（右に後円部、左奥に前方部） |所在地=[[奈良県]][[高市郡]][[明日香村]]平田 |位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|28|7.38|N|135|48|2.80|E|region:JP-29_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=梅山古墳（伝欽明天皇陵）}} |形状={{前方後円墳}} |規模=墳丘長140[[メートル|m]] |埋葬施設= |築造年代=[[古墳時代後期]] |被葬者=（[[宮内庁]]治定）第29代[[欽明天皇]] |出土品= |陵墓=[[宮内庁]]治定「檜隈坂合陵」 |特記事項= }} [[ファイル:Umeyama kohun aerial.jpg|thumb|220px|左下に吉備姫皇女王墓・猿石（{{国土航空写真}}）]] [[File:Umeyama Kofun, haisho.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|[[欽明天皇]]檜隈坂合陵 拝所}}]] '''梅山古墳'''（うめやまこふん）は、[[奈良県]][[高市郡]][[明日香村]]にある[[古墳]]。形状は[[前方後円墳]]。 実際の被葬者は明らかでないが、[[宮内庁]]により「'''檜隈坂合陵'''（ひのくまのさかあいのみささぎ）」として第29代[[欽明天皇]]の[[天皇陵|陵]]に治定されている。 == 概要 == 墳丘は前方部を西に向け東西に正確に主軸をとった[[前方後円墳]]で、全長約140[[メートル|m]] 後円部径72mで、明日香村内では最大の古墳である。非常に多くの[[葺石]]があることでも知られている。現在は回りに水を湛えた周濠を持っているが、これは[[文久]]の修復で大幅に改築されたものであり元は[[田]]であった。なお、この修復の際に双円墳から前方後円墳に改造されたとする考えもある[[森浩一]]による説。 同古墳と[[平田岩屋古墳]]、[[鬼の爼・鬼の雪隠|鬼の俎・雪隠]]古墳、[[野口王墓]]（天武・持統合葬陵）は同じ丘陵の南側の地形を利用して造られており、4つの古墳が東西に並んでいる。 == 築造時期 == [[古墳時代後期]]の築造とみられているが、[[発掘調査]]が行われていないため正確な築造時期を推定することは難しい。 == 埋葬者 == 宮内庁によって欽明天皇陵とされているが、梅山古墳から北西に約800m行った所にある奈良県下最大の古墳の[[見瀬丸山古墳]]を欽明天皇陵とする学説も多い。梅山古墳の埋葬者については[[蘇我稲目]]とする考えもある。 == 猿石 == 隣接する[[吉備姫王]]墓に[[猿石]]と呼ばれる石像物が置かれているが、これは江戸時代に梅山古墳のすぐ南の田（小字池田）で掘り出されて古墳のかたわらに置かれていたもので、[[明治]]初期に現在の場所に移された。 == 脚注 == {{Reflist}} == 外部リンク == {{Commonscat|Umeyama Kofun}} *[http://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/guide/029/index.html 檜隈坂合陵] - 宮内庁 {{デフォルトソート:うめやまこふん}} [[Category:奈良県の古墳]] [[Category:明日香村の歴史]] [[Category:前方後円墳]] [[Category:日本の陵墓]]

== 概要 == 墳丘は前方部を西に向け東西に正確に主軸をとった[[前方後円墳]]で、全長約140[[メートル|m]] 後円部径72mで、明日香村内では最大の古墳である。非常に多くの[[葺石]]があることでも知られている。現在は回りに水を湛えた周濠を持っているが、これは[[文久]]の修復で大幅に改築されたものであり元は[[田]]であった。なお、この修復の際に双円墳から前方後円墳に改造されたとする考えもある<ref>[[森浩一]]による説</ref>。
同古墳と[[平田岩屋古墳]]、[[鬼の爼・鬼の雪隠|鬼の俎・雪隠]]古墳、[[野口王墓]]（天武・持統合葬陵）は同じ丘陵の南側の地形を利用して造られており、4つの古墳が東西に並んでいる。
== 築造時期 == [[古墳時代後期]]の築造とみられているが、[[発掘調査]]が行われていないため正確な築造時期を推定することは難しい。
== 埋葬者 == 宮内庁によって欽明天皇陵とされているが、梅山古墳から北西に約800m行った所にある奈良県下最大の古墳の[[見瀬丸山古墳]]を欽明天皇陵とする学説も多い。梅山古墳の埋葬者については[[蘇我稲目]]とする考えもある。
== 猿石 == 隣接する[[吉備姫王]]墓に[[猿石]]と呼ばれる石像物が置かれているが、これは江戸時代に梅山古墳のすぐ南の田（小字池田）で掘り出されて古墳のかたわらに置かれていたもので、[[明治]]初期に現在の場所に移された。
== 脚注 == {{Reflist}}
== 外部リンク == {{Commonscat|Umeyama Kofun}} *[http://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/guide/029/index.html 檜隈坂合陵] - 宮内庁

{{日本の古墳 |名称=梅山古墳 |画像=[[File:Umeyama Kofun, zenkei-2.jpg|280px]]
墳丘と周濠
（右に後円部、左奥に前方部） |所在地=[[奈良県]][[高市郡]][[明日香村]]平田 |位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|28|7.38|N|135|48|2.80|E|region:JP-29_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=梅山古墳（伝欽明天皇陵）}} |形状=[[前方後円墳]] |規模=墳丘長140[[メートル|m]] |埋葬施設= |築造年代=[[古墳時代後期]] |被葬者=（[[宮内庁]]治定）第29代[[欽明天皇]] |出土品= |陵墓=[[宮内庁]]治定「檜隈坂合陵」 |特記事項= }} [[ファイル:Umeyama kohun aerial.jpg|thumb|220px|左下に吉備姫皇女王墓・猿石（{{国土航空写真}}）]] [[File:Umeyama Kofun, haisho.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|[[欽明天皇]]檜隈坂合陵 拝所}}]] '''梅山古墳'''（うめやまこふん）は、[[奈良県]][[高市郡]][[明日香村]]にある[[古墳]]。形状は[[前方後円墳]]。 実際の被葬者は明らかでないが、[[宮内庁]]により「'''檜隈坂合陵'''（ひのくまのさかあいのみささぎ）」として第29代[[欽明天皇]]の[[天皇陵|陵]]に治定されている。 == 概要 == 墳丘は前方部を西に向け東西に正確に主軸をとった[[前方後円墳]]で、全長約140[[メートル|m]] 後円部径72mで、明日香村内では最大の古墳である。非常に多くの[[葺石]]があることでも知られている。現在は回りに水を湛えた周濠を持っているが、これは[[文久]]の修復で大幅に改築されたものであり元は[[田]]であった。なお、この修復の際に双円墳から前方後円墳に改造されたとする考えもある[[森浩一]]による説。 同古墳と[[平田岩屋古墳]]、[[鬼の爼・鬼の雪隠|鬼の俎・雪隠]]古墳、[[野口王墓]]（天武・持統合葬陵）は同じ丘陵の南側の地形を利用して造られており、4つの古墳が東西に並んでいる。 == 築造時期 == [[古墳時代後期]]の築造とみられているが、[[発掘調査]]が行われていないため正確な築造時期を推定することは難しい。 == 埋葬者 == 宮内庁によって欽明天皇陵とされているが、梅山古墳から北西に約800m行った所にある奈良県下最大の古墳の[[見瀬丸山古墳]]を欽明天皇陵とする学説も多い。梅山古墳の埋葬者については[[蘇我稲目]]とする考えもある。 == 猿石 == 隣接する[[吉備姫王]]墓に[[猿石]]と呼ばれる石像物が置かれているが、これは江戸時代に梅山古墳のすぐ南の田（小字池田）で掘り出されて古墳のかたわらに置かれていたもので、[[明治]]初期に現在の場所に移された。 == 脚注 == {{Reflist}} == 外部リンク == {{Commonscat|Umeyama Kofun}} *[https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/guide/029/index.html 檜隈坂合陵] - 宮内庁 {{デフォルトソート:うめやまこふん}} [[Category:奈良県の古墳]] [[Category:明日香村の歴史]] [[Category:前方後円墳]] [[Category:日本の陵墓]]

{{日本の古墳 |名称=梅山古墳 |画像=[[File:Umeyama Kofun, zenkei-2.jpg|280px]]
墳丘と周濠
（右に後円部、左奥に前方部） |所在地=[[奈良県]][[高市郡]][[明日香村]]平田 |位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|28|7.38|N|135|48|2.80|E|region:JP-29_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=梅山古墳（伝欽明天皇陵）}} |形状=[[前方後円墳]] |規模=墳丘長140[[メートル|m]] |埋葬施設= |築造年代=[[古墳時代後期]] |被葬者=（[[宮内庁]]治定）第29代[[欽明天皇]] |出土品= |陵墓=[[宮内庁]]治定「檜隈坂合陵」 |特記事項= }} [[ファイル:Umeyama kohun aerial.jpg|thumb|220px|左下に吉備姫皇女王墓・猿石（{{国土航空写真}}）]] [[File:Umeyama Kofun, haisho.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|[[欽明天皇]]檜隈坂合陵 拝所}}]] '''梅山古墳'''（うめやまこふん）は、[[奈良県]][[高市郡]][[明日香村]]にある[[古墳]]。形状は[[前方後円墳]]。 実際の被葬者は明らかでないが、[[宮内庁]]により「'''檜隈坂合陵'''（ひのくまのさかあいのみささぎ）」として第29代[[欽明天皇]]の[[天皇陵|陵]]に治定されている。 == 概要 == 墳丘は前方部を西に向け東西に正確に主軸をとった[[前方後円墳]]で、全長約140[[メートル|m]] 後円部径72mで、明日香村内では最大の古墳である。非常に多くの[[葺石]]があることでも知られている。現在は回りに水を湛えた周濠を持っているが、これは[[文久]]の修復で大幅に改築されたものであり元は[[田]]であった。なお、この修復の際に双円墳から前方後円墳に改造されたとする考えもある[[森浩一]]による説。 同古墳と[[平田岩屋古墳]]、[[鬼の爼・鬼の雪隠|鬼の俎・雪隠]]古墳、[[野口王墓]]（天武・持統合葬陵）は同じ丘陵の南側の地形を利用して造られており、4つの古墳が東西に並んでいる。 == 築造時期 == [[古墳時代後期]]の築造とみられているが、[[発掘調査]]が行われていないため正確な築造時期を推定することは難しい。 == 埋葬者 == 宮内庁によって欽明天皇陵とされているが、梅山古墳から北西に約800m行った所にある奈良県下最大の古墳の[[見瀬丸山古墳]]を欽明天皇陵とする学説も多い。梅山古墳の埋葬者については[[蘇我稲目]]とする考えもある。 == 猿石 == 隣接する[[吉備姫王]]墓に[[猿石]]と呼ばれる石像物が置かれているが、これは江戸時代に梅山古墳のすぐ南の田（小字池田）で掘り出されて古墳のかたわらに置かれていたもので、[[明治]]初期に現在の場所に移された。 == 脚注 == {{Reflist}} == 関連項目 == * [[カナヅカ古墳]] == 外部リンク == {{Commonscat|Umeyama Kofun}} *[https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/guide/029/index.html 檜隈坂合陵] - 宮内庁 {{デフォルトソート:うめやまこふん}} [[Category:奈良県の古墳]] [[Category:明日香村の歴史]] [[Category:前方後円墳]] [[Category:日本の陵墓]]

[[ja:梅山古墳]]

[[ja:梅山古墳]] {{Ancient Japanese Burial Mound | Name = Umeyama Kofun | Image = [[File:Umeyama Kofun, zenkei-2.jpg|280px]]
Tumulus and moat
(Rear circular mound on the right, front rectangular mound in the left background) | Location = {{ill|Asuka, Nara|ja|明日香村}}, Nara Prefecture | Coordinates = {{Coord|34|28|7.38|N|135|48|2.80|E|region:JP-29_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | Shape = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳}} | Dimensions = Total length: 140m | Burial facility = Unknown | Construction era = Late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} | Interred individual = ({{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designation) {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇}} | Excavated items = Unknown | Mausoleum = Designated by the Imperial Household Agency as "Hinokuma Sakaai no misasagi" (檜隈坂合陵) | Map = {{Location map|Japan Nara|lat_deg=34|lat_min=28|lat_sec=7.38|lon_deg=135|lon_min=48|lon_sec=2.8|label=Umeyama Kofun|mark=前方後円墳-西.svg|marksize=23|caption=Location in Nara Prefecture|position=bottom|width=200|float=center|border=none}} }} [[File:Umeyama kohun aerial.jpg|thumb|220px|Bottom left: Kibi-hime Royal Tomb and Saruishi statues ({{National Land Aerial Photography}})]] [[File:Umeyama Kofun, haisho.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Mausoleum of {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇}}}}]] '''Umeyama Kofun''' (梅山古墳) is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳}} located in {{ill|Asuka, Nara|ja|明日香村}}, Nara Prefecture. The actual interred individual is unknown, but it has been designated by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} as the "Hinokuma Sakaai no misasagi" (檜隈坂合陵), attributed to {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇}}. == Overview == The tumulus features a keyhole shape, with the front mound oriented to the west along an east-west axis. Measuring approximately 140 meters in total length and 72 meters in diameter for the rear mound, it is the largest tumulus in Asuka Village. The mound is notable for its extensive use of {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}} (covering stones). Surrounding the tumulus is a water-filled moat, which was extensively remodeled during the {{ill|Bunkyū era|ja|文久}} restoration. Originally, the surrounding area was agricultural land. Some theories suggest that the restoration converted the tumulus from a double circular mound to its current keyhole shape.Theory proposed by {{ill|Kōichi Mori|ja|森浩一}} The tumulus is part of a group of burial mounds, including {{ill|Hirata Iwaya Kofun|ja|平田岩屋古墳}}, {{ill|Oni no Manaita and Oni no Senkan|ja|鬼の俎・鬼の雪隠}}, and {{ill|Noguchi Royal Tomb|ja|野口王墓}} (the joint tomb of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|天武天皇}} and {{ill|Empress Jitō|ja|持統天皇}}), all situated along the southern slope of the same hill. == Construction Period == While believed to have been built during the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, the exact construction date is uncertain due to the lack of archaeological excavations. == Interred Individual == Although designated by the Imperial Household Agency as the tomb of {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇}}, many scholars suggest that the nearby {{ill|Mise Maruyama Kofun|ja|見瀬丸山古墳}}, located approximately 800 meters northwest and the largest tumulus in Nara Prefecture, might be a more likely candidate. Other theories propose that {{ill|Soga no Iname|ja|蘇我稲目}} may be the interred individual. == Saruishi Statues == Adjacent to the {{ill|Tomb of Princess Kibi|ja|吉備姫王墓}}, stone statues known as {{ill|Saruishi|ja|猿石}} ("monkey stones") are placed nearby. These statues were excavated during the Edo period from a field just south of Umeyama Kofun and relocated to their current location in the early {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治時代}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Kanazuka Kofun|ja|カナヅカ古墳}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Umeyama Kofun}} * [https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/guide/029/index.html Hinokuma Sakaai no misasagi] - Imperial Household Agency [[Category:Ancient tumuli in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Asuka, Nara]] [[Category:Keyhole-shaped tumuli]] [[Category:Imperial mausolea in Japan]] [[Category:Emperor Kinmei]] [[ja:梅山古墳]]

[[category:new pages]][[ja:梅山古墳]] {{Ancient Japanese Burial Mound | Name = Umeyama Kofun | Image = [[File:Umeyama Kofun, zenkei-2.jpg|280px]]
Tumulus and moat
(Rear circular mound on the right, front rectangular mound in the left background) | Location = {{ill|Asuka, Nara|ja|明日香村}}, Nara Prefecture | Coordinates = {{Coord|34|28|7.38|N|135|48|2.80|E|region:JP-29_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | Shape = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳}} | Dimensions = Total length: 140m | Burial facility = Unknown | Construction era = Late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} | Interred individual = ({{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designation) {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇}} | Excavated items = Unknown | Mausoleum = Designated by the Imperial Household Agency as "Hinokuma Sakaai no misasagi" (檜隈坂合陵) | Map = {{Location map|Japan Nara|lat_deg=34|lat_min=28|lat_sec=7.38|lon_deg=135|lon_min=48|lon_sec=2.8|label=Umeyama Kofun|mark=前方後円墳-西.svg|marksize=23|caption=Location in Nara Prefecture|position=bottom|width=200|float=center|border=none}} }} [[File:Umeyama kohun aerial.jpg|thumb|220px|Bottom left: Kibi-hime Royal Tomb and Saruishi statues ({{National Land Aerial Photography}})]] [[File:Umeyama Kofun, haisho.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Mausoleum of {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇}}}}]] '''Umeyama Kofun''' (梅山古墳) is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳}} located in {{ill|Asuka, Nara|ja|明日香村}}, Nara Prefecture. The actual interred individual is unknown, but it has been designated by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} as the "Hinokuma Sakaai no misasagi" (檜隈坂合陵), attributed to {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇}}. == Overview == The tumulus features a keyhole shape, with the front mound oriented to the west along an east-west axis. Measuring approximately 140 meters in total length and 72 meters in diameter for the rear mound, it is the largest tumulus in Asuka Village. The mound is notable for its extensive use of {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}} (covering stones). Surrounding the tumulus is a water-filled moat, which was extensively remodeled during the {{ill|Bunkyū era|ja|文久}} restoration. Originally, the surrounding area was agricultural land. Some theories suggest that the restoration converted the tumulus from a double circular mound to its current keyhole shape.Theory proposed by {{ill|Kōichi Mori|ja|森浩一}} The tumulus is part of a group of burial mounds, including {{ill|Hirata Iwaya Kofun|ja|平田岩屋古墳}}, {{ill|Oni no Manaita and Oni no Senkan|ja|鬼の俎・鬼の雪隠}}, and {{ill|Noguchi Royal Tomb|ja|野口王墓}} (the joint tomb of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|天武天皇}} and {{ill|Empress Jitō|ja|持統天皇}}), all situated along the southern slope of the same hill. == Construction Period == While believed to have been built during the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, the exact construction date is uncertain due to the lack of archaeological excavations. == Interred Individual == Although designated by the Imperial Household Agency as the tomb of {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇}}, many scholars suggest that the nearby {{ill|Mise Maruyama Kofun|ja|見瀬丸山古墳}}, located approximately 800 meters northwest and the largest tumulus in Nara Prefecture, might be a more likely candidate. Other theories propose that {{ill|Soga no Iname|ja|蘇我稲目}} may be the interred individual. == Saruishi Statues == Adjacent to the {{ill|Tomb of Princess Kibi|ja|吉備姫王墓}}, stone statues known as {{ill|Saruishi|ja|猿石}} ("monkey stones") are placed nearby. These statues were excavated during the Edo period from a field just south of Umeyama Kofun and relocated to their current location in the early {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治時代}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Kanazuka Kofun|ja|カナヅカ古墳}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Umeyama Kofun}} * [https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/guide/029/index.html Hinokuma Sakaai no misasagi] - Imperial Household Agency [[Category:Ancient tumuli in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Asuka, Nara]] [[Category:Keyhole-shaped tumuli]] [[Category:Imperial mausolea in Japan]] [[Category:Emperor Kinmei]] [[category:new pages]][[ja:梅山古墳]]

Tumulus and moat

The actual interred individual is unknown, but it has been designated by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} as the "Hinokuma Sakaai no misasagi" (檜隈坂合陵), attributed to {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇}}. == Overview == The tumulus features a keyhole shape, with the front mound oriented to the west along an east-west axis. Some theories suggest that the restoration converted the tumulus from a double circular mound to its current keyhole shape.Theory proposed by {{ill|Kōichi Mori|ja|森浩一}} The tumulus is part of a group of burial mounds, including {{ill|Hirata Iwaya Kofun|ja|平田岩屋古墳}}, {{ill|Oni no Manaita and Oni no Senkan|ja|鬼の俎・鬼の雪隠}}, and {{ill|Noguchi Royal Tomb|ja|野口王墓}} (the joint tomb of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|天武天皇}} and {{ill|Empress Jitō|ja|持統天皇}}), all situated along the southern slope of the same hill. == Construction Period == While believed to have been built during the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, the exact construction date is uncertain due to the lack of archaeological excavations. == Interred Individual == Although designated by the Imperial Household Agency as the tomb of {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇}}, many scholars suggest that the nearby {{ill|Mise Maruyama Kofun|ja|見瀬丸山古墳}}, located approximately 800 meters northwest and the largest tumulus in Nara Prefecture, might be a more likely candidate. Other theories propose that {{ill|Soga no Iname|ja|蘇我稲目}} may be the interred individual. == Saruishi Statues == Adjacent to the {{ill|Tomb of Princess Kibi|ja|吉備姫王墓}}, stone statues known as {{ill|Saruishi|ja|猿石}} ("monkey stones") are placed nearby. These statues were excavated during the Edo period from a field just south of Umeyama Kofun and relocated to their current location in the early {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治時代}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Kanazuka Kofun|ja|カナヅカ古墳}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Umeyama Kofun}} * [https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/guide/029/index.html Hinokuma Sakaai no misasagi] - Imperial Household Agency [[Category:Ancient tumuli in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Asuka, Nara]] [[Category:Keyhole-shaped tumuli]] [[Category:Imperial mausolea in Japan]] [[Category:Emperor Kinmei]] [[category:new pages]][[category:new pages]][[ja:梅山古墳]]

revision.comment: 推古天皇による堅塩媛の改葬について
revision.text:
{{日本の古墳 |名称=梅山古墳 |画像=[[File:Umeyama Kofun, zenkei-2.jpg|280px]]
墳丘と周濠
（右に後円部、左奥に前方部） |所在地=[[奈良県]][[高市郡]][[明日香村]]平田 |位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|28|7.38|N|135|48|2.80|E|region:JP-29_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=梅山古墳（伝欽明天皇陵）}} |形状=[[前方後円墳]] |規模=墳丘長140[[メートル|m]] |埋葬施設= |築造年代=[[古墳時代後期]] |被葬者=（[[宮内庁]]治定）第29代[[欽明天皇]] |出土品= |陵墓=[[宮内庁]]治定「檜隈坂合陵」 |特記事項= |地図2= {{Location map|Japan Nara|lat_deg=34|lat_min=28|lat_sec=7.38|lon_deg=135|lon_min=48|lon_sec=2.8|label=梅山古墳|mark=前方後円墳-西.svg|marksize=23|caption=奈良県内の位置|position=bottom|width=200|float=center|border=none}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|28|7.38|N|135|48|2.8|E}}}} }} [[ファイル:Umeyama kohun aerial.jpg|thumb|220px|左下に吉備姫皇女王墓・猿石（{{国土航空写真}}）]] [[File:Umeyama Kofun, haisho.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|[[欽明天皇]]檜隈坂合陵 拝所}}]] '''梅山古墳'''（うめやまこふん）は、[[奈良県]][[高市郡]][[明日香村]]にある[[古墳]]。形状は[[前方後円墳]]。 実際の被葬者は明らかでないが、[[宮内庁]]により「'''檜隈坂合陵'''（ひのくまのさかあいのみささぎ）」として第29代[[欽明天皇]]の[[天皇陵|陵]]に治定されている。檜隈坂合陵には[[推古天皇]]20年（612年）2月に[[蘇我堅塩媛|堅塩媛]]も[[改葬]]されている。『日本書記』、巻第二十二 == 概要 == 墳丘は前方部を西に向け東西に正確に主軸をとった[[前方後円墳]]で、全長約140[[メートル|m]] 後円部径72mで、明日香村内では最大の古墳である。非常に多くの[[葺石]]があることでも知られている。現在は回りに水を湛えた周濠を持っているが、これは[[文久]]の修復で大幅に改築されたものであり元は[[田]]であった。なお、この修復の際に双円墳から前方後円墳に改造されたとする考えもある[[森浩一]]による説。他にも[[外池昇]]『検証 天皇陵』p.97でも、『文久山陵図』の荒無図の檜隈坂合陵は双円墳のようと評している。。 同古墳と[[平田岩屋古墳]]、[[鬼の爼・鬼の雪隠|鬼の俎・雪隠]]古墳、[[野口王墓]]（天武・持統合葬陵）は同じ丘陵の南側の地形を利用して造られており、4つの古墳が東西に並んでいる。 == 築造時期 == [[古墳時代後期]]の築造とみられているが、[[発掘調査]]が行われていないため正確な築造時期を推定することは難しい。 == 埋葬者 == 宮内庁によって欽明天皇陵とされているが、梅山古墳から北西に約800m行った所にある奈良県下最大の古墳の[[見瀬丸山古墳]]を欽明天皇陵とする学説も多い。梅山古墳の埋葬者については[[蘇我稲目]]とする考えもある。 == 猿石 == 隣接する[[吉備姫王]]墓に[[猿石]]と呼ばれる石像物が置かれているが、これは江戸時代に梅山古墳のすぐ南の田（小字池田）で掘り出されて古墳のかたわらに置かれていたもので、[[明治]]初期に現在の場所に移された。 == 脚注 == {{Reflist}} == 関連項目 == * [[カナヅカ古墳]] == 外部リンク == {{Commonscat|Umeyama Kofun}} *[https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/guide/029/index.html 檜隈坂合陵] - 宮内庁 {{デフォルトソート:うめやまこふん}} [[Category:奈良県の古墳]] [[Category:明日香村の歴史]] [[Category:前方後円墳]] [[Category:日本の陵墓]] [[Category:欽明天皇]]

Other theories propose that {{ill|Soga no Iname|ja|蘇我稲目}} may be the interred individual. == Saruishi Statues == Adjacent to the {{ill|Tomb of Princess Kibi|ja|吉備姫王墓}}, stone statues known as {{ill|Saruishi|ja|猿石}} ("monkey stones") are placed nearby. These statues were excavated during the Edo period from a field just south of Umeyama Kofun and relocated to their current location in the early {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治時代}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Kanazuka Kofun|ja|カナヅカ古墳}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Umeyama Kofun}} * [https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/guide/029/index.html Hinokuma Sakaai no misasagi] - Imperial Household Agency [[Category:Ancient tumuli in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Asuka, Nara]] [[Category:Keyhole-shaped tumuli]] [[Category:Imperial mausolea in Japan]] [[Category:Emperor Kinmei]] [[category:new pages]][[category:new pages]][[ja:梅山古墳]]

[[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]][[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]][[ja:梅山古墳]] {{Infobox ancient site | Name = Umeyama Kofun | image = [[File:Umeyama Kofun, zenkei-2.jpg|280px]]
Tumulus and moat
(Rear circular mound on the right, front rectangular mound in the left background) | location = {{ill|Asuka, Nara|ja|明日香村}}, Nara Prefecture | coordinates = {{Coord|34|28|7.38|N|135|48|2.80|E|region:JP-29_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳}} | Dimensions = Total length: 140m | Burial facility = Unknown | Construction era = Late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} | Interred individual = ({{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designation) {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇}} | Excavated items = Unknown | Mausoleum = Designated by the Imperial Household Agency as "Hinokuma Sakaai no misasagi" (檜隈坂合陵) | Map = {{location map|Japan Nara|lat_deg=34|lat_min=28|lat_sec=7.38|lon_deg=135|lon_min=48|lon_sec=2.8|label=Umeyama Kofun|mark=前方後円墳-西.svg|marksize=23|caption=location in Nara Prefecture|position=bottom|width=200|float=center|border=none}} }} [[File:Umeyama kohun aerial.jpg|thumb|220px|Bottom left: Kibi-hime Royal Tomb and Saruishi statues ({{National Land Aerial Photography}})]] [[File:Umeyama Kofun, haisho.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Mausoleum of {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇}}}}]] '''Umeyama Kofun''' (梅山古墳) is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳}} located in {{ill|Asuka, Nara|ja|明日香村}}, Nara Prefecture. The actual interred individual is unknown, but it has been designated by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} as the "Hinokuma Sakaai no misasagi" (檜隈坂合陵), attributed to {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇}}. == Overview == The tumulus features a keyhole shape, with the front mound oriented to the west along an east-west axis. Measuring approximately 140 meters in total length and 72 meters in diameter for the rear mound, it is the largest tumulus in Asuka Village. The mound is notable for its extensive use of {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}} (covering stones). Surrounding the tumulus is a water-filled moat, which was extensively remodeled during the {{ill|Bunkyū era|ja|文久}} restoration. Originally, the surrounding area was agricultural land. Some theories suggest that the restoration converted the tumulus from a double circular mound to its current keyhole shape.Theory proposed by {{ill|Kōichi Mori|ja|森浩一}} The tumulus is part of a group of burial mounds, including {{ill|Hirata Iwaya Kofun|ja|平田岩屋古墳}}, {{ill|Oni no Manaita and Oni no Senkan|ja|鬼の俎・鬼の雪隠}}, and {{ill|Noguchi Royal Tomb|ja|野口王墓}} (the joint tomb of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|天武天皇}} and {{ill|Empress Jitō|ja|持統天皇}}), all situated along the southern slope of the same hill. == Construction Period == While believed to have been built during the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, the exact construction date is uncertain due to the lack of archaeological excavations. == Interred Individual == Although designated by the Imperial Household Agency as the tomb of {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇}}, many scholars suggest that the nearby {{ill|Mise Maruyama Kofun|ja|見瀬丸山古墳}}, located approximately 800 meters northwest and the largest tumulus in Nara Prefecture, might be a more likely candidate. Other theories propose that {{ill|Soga no Iname|ja|蘇我稲目}} may be the interred individual. == Saruishi Statues == Adjacent to the {{ill|Tomb of Princess Kibi|ja|吉備姫王墓}}, stone statues known as {{ill|Saruishi|ja|猿石}} ("monkey stones") are placed nearby. These statues were excavated during the Edo period from a field just south of Umeyama Kofun and relocated to their current location in the early {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治時代}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Kanazuka Kofun|ja|カナヅカ古墳}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Umeyama Kofun}} * [https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/guide/029/index.html Hinokuma Sakaai no misasagi] - Imperial Household Agency [[Category:Ancient tumuli in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Asuka, Nara]] [[Category:Keyhole-shaped tumuli]] [[Category:Imperial mausolea in Japan]] [[Category:Emperor Kinmei]] [[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]][[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]][[ja:梅山古墳]]

Other theories propose that {{ill|Soga no Iname|ja|蘇我稲目}} may be the interred individual. == Saruishi Statues == Adjacent to the {{ill|Tomb of Princess Kibi|ja|吉備姫王墓}}, stone statues known as {{ill|Saruishi|ja|猿石}} ("monkey stones") are placed nearby. These statues were excavated during the Edo period from a field just south of Umeyama Kofun and relocated to their current location in the early {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治時代}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Kanazuka Kofun|ja|カナヅカ古墳}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Umeyama Kofun}} * [https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/guide/029/index.html Hinokuma Sakaai no misasagi] - Imperial Household Agency [[Category:Ancient tumuli in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Asuka, Nara]] [[Category:Keyhole-shaped tumuli]] [[Category:Imperial mausolea in Japan]] [[Category:Emperor Kinmei]] [[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]][[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]] [[Category:pages to try to fix ills]] [[ja:梅山古墳]]

Tumulus and moat
(Rear circular mound on the right, front rectangular mound in the left background) | location = {{ill|Asuka, Nara|ja|明日香村}}, Nara Prefecture | coordinates = {{Coord|34|28|7.38|N|135|48|2.80|E|region:JP-29_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳}} | Dimensions = Total length: 140m | Burial facility = Unknown | Construction era = Late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} | Interred individual = ({{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designation) {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇}} | Excavated items = Unknown | Mausoleum = Designated by the Imperial Household Agency as "Hinokuma Sakaai no misasagi" (檜隈坂合陵) | Map = {{location map|Japan Nara|lat_deg=34|lat_min=28|lat_sec=7.38|lon_deg=135|lon_min=48|lon_sec=2.8|label=Umeyama Kofun|mark=前方後円墳-西.svg|marksize=23|caption=location in Nara Prefecture|position=bottom|width=200|float=center|border=none}} }} [[File:Umeyama kohun aerial.jpg|thumb|220px|Bottom left: Kibi-hime Royal Tomb and Saruishi statues ({{National Land Aerial Photography}})]] [[File:Umeyama Kofun, haisho.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Mausoleum of {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇}}}} '''Umeyama Kofun''' (梅山古墳) is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳}} located in {{ill|Asuka, Nara|ja|明日香村}}, Nara Prefecture. The actual interred individual is unknown, but it has been designated by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} as the "Hinokuma Sakaai no misasagi" (檜隈坂合陵), attributed to {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇}}. == Overview == The tumulus features a keyhole shape, with the front mound oriented to the west along an east-west axis. Measuring approximately 140 meters in total length and 72 meters in diameter for the rear mound, it is the largest tumulus in Asuka Village. The mound is notable for its extensive use of {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}} (covering stones). Surrounding the tumulus is a water-filled moat, which was extensively remodeled during the {{ill|Bunkyū era|ja|文久}} restoration. Originally, the surrounding area was agricultural land. Some theories suggest that the restoration converted the tumulus from a double circular mound to its current keyhole shape.Theory proposed by {{ill|Kōichi Mori|ja|森浩一}} The tumulus is part of a group of burial mounds, including {{ill|Hirata Iwaya Kofun|ja|平田岩屋古墳}}, {{ill|Oni no Manaita and Oni no Senkan|ja|鬼の俎・鬼の雪隠}}, and {{ill|Noguchi Royal Tomb|ja|野口王墓}} (the joint tomb of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|天武天皇}} and {{ill|Empress Jitō|ja|持統天皇}}), all situated along the southern slope of the same hill. == Construction Period == While believed to have been built during the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, the exact construction date is uncertain due to the lack of archaeological excavations. == Interred Individual == Although designated by the Imperial Household Agency as the tomb of {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇}}, many scholars suggest that the nearby {{ill|Mise Maruyama Kofun|ja|見瀬丸山古墳}}, located approximately 800 meters northwest and the largest tumulus in Nara Prefecture, might be a more likely candidate. Other theories propose that {{ill|Soga no Iname|ja|蘇我稲目}} may be the interred individual. == Saruishi Statues == Adjacent to the {{ill|Tomb of Princess Kibi|ja|吉備姫王墓}}, stone statues known as {{ill|Saruishi|ja|猿石}} ("monkey stones") are placed nearby. These statues were excavated during the Edo period from a field just south of Umeyama Kofun and relocated to their current location in the early {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治時代}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Kanazuka Kofun|ja|カナヅカ古墳}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Umeyama Kofun}} * [https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/guide/029/index.html Hinokuma Sakaai no misasagi] - Imperial Household Agency [[Category:Ancient tumuli in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Asuka, Nara]] [[Category:Keyhole-shaped tumuli]] [[Category:Imperial mausolea in Japan]] [[Category:Emperor Kinmei]] [[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]] [[Category:original pages]] [[Category:kkk]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Umeyama Kofun}}[[category:kk]] [[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]] [[Category:original pages]] [[Category:kkk]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Umeyama Kofun}}[[category:kk]] [[Category:pages to try to fix ills]] [[Category:kk]][[category:kk]] [[ja:梅山古墳]]

These statues were excavated during the Edo period from a field just south of Umeyama Kofun and relocated to their current location in the early {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治時代}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Kanazuka Kofun|ja|カナヅカ古墳}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Umeyama Kofun}} * [https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/guide/029/index.html Hinokuma Sakaai no misasagi] - Imperial Household Agency [[Category:Ancient tumuli in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Asuka, Nara]] [[Category:Keyhole-shaped tumuli]] [[Category:Imperial mausolea in Japan]] [[Category:Emperor Kinmei]] [[Category:original pages]] [[Category:original pages]] [[Category:kkk]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Umeyama Kofun}}[[category:kk]] [[Category:original pages]] [[Category:original pages]] [[Category:kkk]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Umeyama Kofun}}[[category:kk]] [[Category:kk]][[category:kk]] [[ja:梅山古墳]]

(Rear circular mound on the right, front rectangular mound in the left background) | location = {{ill|Asuka, Nara|ja|明日香村}}, Nara Prefecture | coordinates = {{Coord|34|28|7.38|N|135|48|2.80|E|region:JP-29_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳}} | Dimensions = Total length: 140m | Burial facility = Unknown | Construction era = Late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} | Interred individual = ({{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designation) {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇}} | Excavated items = Unknown | Mausoleum = Designated by the Imperial Household Agency as "Hinokuma Sakaai no misasagi" (檜隈坂合陵) | Map = {{location map|Japan Nara|lat_deg=34|lat_min=28|lat_sec=7.38|lon_deg=135|lon_min=48|lon_sec=2.8|label=Umeyama Kofun|mark=前方後円墳-西.svg|marksize=23|caption=location in Nara Prefecture|position=bottom|width=200|float=center|border=none}} }} [[File:Umeyama kohun aerial.jpg|thumb|220px|Bottom left: Kibi-hime Royal Tomb and Saruishi statues ({{National Land Aerial Photography}})]] [[File:Umeyama Kofun, haisho.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Mausoleum of {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇}}}} '''Umeyama Kofun''' (梅山古墳) is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳}} located in {{ill|Asuka, Nara|ja|明日香村}}, Nara Prefecture. The actual interred individual is unknown, but it has been designated by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} as the "Hinokuma Sakaai no misasagi" (檜隈坂合陵), attributed to {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇}}. == Overview == The tumulus features a keyhole shape, with the front mound oriented to the west along an east-west axis. Measuring approximately 140 meters in total length and 72 meters in diameter for the rear mound, it is the largest tumulus in Asuka Village. The mound is notable for its extensive use of {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}} (covering stones). Surrounding the tumulus is a water-filled moat, which was extensively remodeled during the {{ill|Bunkyū era|ja|文久}} restoration. Originally, the surrounding area was agricultural land. Some theories suggest that the restoration converted the tumulus from a double circular mound to its current keyhole shape.Theory proposed by {{ill|Kōichi Mori|ja|森浩一}} The tumulus is part of a group of burial mounds, including {{ill|Hirata Iwaya Kofun|ja|平田岩屋古墳}}, {{ill|Oni no Manaita and Oni no Senkan|ja|鬼の俎・鬼の雪隠}}, and {{ill|Noguchi Royal Tomb|ja|野口王墓}} (the joint tomb of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|天武天皇}} and {{ill|Empress Jitō|ja|持統天皇}}), all situated along the southern slope of the same hill. == Construction Period == While believed to have been built during the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, the exact construction date is uncertain due to the lack of archaeological excavations. == Interred Individual == Although designated by the Imperial Household Agency as the tomb of {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇}}, many scholars suggest that the nearby {{ill|Mise Maruyama Kofun|ja|見瀬丸山古墳}}, located approximately 800 meters northwest and the largest tumulus in Nara Prefecture, might be a more likely candidate. Other theories propose that {{ill|Soga no Iname|ja|蘇我稲目}} may be the interred individual. == Saruishi Statues == Adjacent to the {{ill|Tomb of Princess Kibi|ja|吉備姫王墓}}, stone statues known as {{ill|Saruishi|ja|猿石}} ("monkey stones") are placed nearby. These statues were excavated during the Edo period from a field just south of Umeyama Kofun and relocated to their current location in the early {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治時代}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Kanazuka Kofun|ja|カナヅカ古墳}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Umeyama Kofun}} * [https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/guide/029/index.html Hinokuma Sakaai no misasagi] - Imperial Household Agency [[Category:Ancient tumuli in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Asuka, Nara]] [[Category:Keyhole-shaped tumuli]] [[Category:Imperial mausolea in Japan]] [[Category:Emperor Kinmei]] [[Category:original pages]] [[Category:original pages]] [[Category:kkk]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Umeyama Kofun}}[[category:kk]] [[Category:original pages]] [[Category:original pages]] [[Category:kkk]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Umeyama Kofun}}[[category:kk]] [[Category:kk]][[category:kk]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Umeyama Kofun]][[Category:kk]] [[ja:梅山古墳]]

Some theories suggest that the restoration converted the tumulus from a double circular mound to its current keyhole shape.Theory proposed by {{ill|Kōichi Mori|ja|森浩一}} The tumulus is part of a group of burial mounds, including {{ill|Hirata Iwaya Kofun|ja|平田岩屋古墳}}, {{ill|Oni no Manaita and Oni no Senkan|ja|鬼の俎・鬼の雪隠}}, and {{ill|Noguchi Royal Tomb|ja|野口王墓}} (the joint tomb of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|天武天皇}} and {{ill|Empress Jitō|ja|持統天皇}}), all situated along the southern slope of the same hill. == Construction Period == While believed to have been built during the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, the exact construction date is uncertain due to the lack of archaeological excavations. == Interred Individual == Although designated by the Imperial Household Agency as the tomb of {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇}}, many scholars suggest that the nearby {{ill|Mise Maruyama Kofun|ja|見瀬丸山古墳}}, located approximately 800 meters northwest and the largest tumulus in Nara Prefecture, might be a more likely candidate. Other theories propose that {{ill|Soga no Iname|ja|蘇我稲目}} may be the interred individual. == Saruishi Statues == Adjacent to the {{ill|Tomb of Princess Kibi|ja|吉備姫王墓}}, stone statues known as {{ill|Saruishi|ja|猿石}} ("monkey stones") are placed nearby. These statues were excavated during the Edo period from a field just south of Umeyama Kofun and relocated to their current location in the early {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治時代}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Kanazuka Kofun|ja|カナヅカ古墳}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Umeyama Kofun}} * [https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/guide/029/index.html Hinokuma Sakaai no misasagi] - Imperial Household Agency [[Category:Ancient tumuli in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Asuka, Nara]] [[Category:Keyhole-shaped tumuli]] [[Category:Imperial mausolea in Japan]] [[Category:Emperor Kinmei]] [[Category:unsorted link categories]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:unsorted link categories]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Umeyama Kofun}}[[category:kk]] [[Category:unsorted link categories]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:unsorted link categories]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Umeyama Kofun}}[[category:kk]] [[Category:kk]][[category:kk]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Umeyama Kofun]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:mass bump]] [[ja:梅山古墳]]

Tumulus and moat
(Rear circular mound on the right, front rectangular mound in the left background) | location = {{ill|Asuka, Nara|ja|明日香村}}, Nara Prefecture | coordinates = {{Coord|34|28|7.38|N|135|48|2.80|E|region:JP-29_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳}} | Dimensions = Total length: 140m | Burial facility = Unknown | Construction era = Late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} | Interred individual = ({{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designation) {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇}} | Excavated items = Unknown | Mausoleum = Designated by the Imperial Household Agency as "Hinokuma Sakaai no misasagi" (檜隈坂合陵) | Map = {{location map|Japan Nara|lat_deg=34|lat_min=28|lat_sec=7.38|lon_deg=135|lon_min=48|lon_sec=2.8|label=Umeyama Kofun|mark=前方後円墳-西.svg|marksize=23|caption=location in Nara Prefecture|position=bottom|width=200|float=center|border=none}} }} [[File:Umeyama kohun aerial.jpg|thumb|220px|Bottom left: Kibi-hime Royal Tomb and Saruishi statues ({{National Land Aerial Photography}})]] [[File:Umeyama Kofun, haisho.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Mausoleum of {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇}}}} '''Umeyama Kofun''' (梅山古墳) is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳}} located in {{ill|Asuka, Nara|ja|明日香村}}, Nara Prefecture. The actual interred individual is unknown, but it has been designated by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} as the "Hinokuma Sakaai no misasagi" (檜隈坂合陵), attributed to {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇}}. == Overview == The tumulus features a keyhole shape, with the front mound oriented to the west along an east-west axis. Measuring approximately 140 meters in total length and 72 meters in diameter for the rear mound, it is the largest tumulus in Asuka Village. The mound is notable for its extensive use of {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}} (covering stones). Surrounding the tumulus is a water-filled moat, which was extensively remodeled during the {{ill|Bunkyū era|ja|文久}} restoration. Originally, the surrounding area was agricultural land. Some theories suggest that the restoration converted the tumulus from a double circular mound to its current keyhole shape.Theory proposed by {{ill|Kōichi Mori|ja|森浩一}} The tumulus is part of a group of burial mounds, including {{ill|Hirata Iwaya Kofun|ja|平田岩屋古墳}}, {{ill|Oni no Manaita and Oni no Senkan|ja|鬼の俎・鬼の雪隠}}, and {{ill|Noguchi Royal Tomb|ja|野口王墓}} (the joint tomb of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|天武天皇}} and {{ill|Empress Jitō|ja|持統天皇}}), all situated along the southern slope of the same hill. == Construction Period == While believed to have been built during the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, the exact construction date is uncertain due to the lack of archaeological excavations. == Interred Individual == Although designated by the Imperial Household Agency as the tomb of {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇}}, many scholars suggest that the nearby {{ill|Mise Maruyama Kofun|ja|見瀬丸山古墳}}, located approximately 800 meters northwest and the largest tumulus in Nara Prefecture, might be a more likely candidate. Other theories propose that {{ill|Soga no Iname|ja|蘇我稲目}} may be the interred individual. == Saruishi Statues == Adjacent to the {{ill|Tomb of Princess Kibi|ja|吉備姫王墓}}, stone statues known as {{ill|Saruishi|ja|猿石}} ("monkey stones") are placed nearby. These statues were excavated during the Edo period from a field just south of Umeyama Kofun and relocated to their current location in the early {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治時代}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Kanazuka Kofun|ja|カナヅカ古墳}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Umeyama Kofun}} * [https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/guide/029/index.html Hinokuma Sakaai no misasagi] - Imperial Household Agency [[Category:Ancient tumuli in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Asuka, Nara]] [[Category:Keyhole-shaped tumuli]] [[Category:Imperial mausolea in Japan]] [[Category:Emperor Kinmei]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:fix the O]]{{DEFAULTSORTUmeyama Kofun}}[[category:fix the O]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:fix the O]]{{DEFAULTSORTUmeyama Kofun}}[[category:fix the O]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Umeyama Kofun}}[[category:kk]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:fix the O]]{{DEFAULTSORTUmeyama Kofun}}[[category:fix the O]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:fix the O]]{{DEFAULTSORTUmeyama Kofun}}[[category:fix the O]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Umeyama Kofun}}[[category:kk]] [[Category:kk]][[category:kk]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Umeyama Kofun]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:mass bump]] [[Category:bump 1-20 (2)]] [[ja:梅山古墳]]

[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:defautsort]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Umeyama Kofun}}[[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:defautsort]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Umeyama Kofun}}[[category:unsorted completion categories]] {{DEFAULTSORT::Umeyama Kofun}}[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:defautsort]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Umeyama Kofun}}[[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:defautsort]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Umeyama Kofun}}[[category:unsorted completion categories]] {{DEFAULTSORT::Umeyama Kofun}}[[ja:梅山古墳]] {{Infobox ancient site | name = Umeyama Kofun | image = [[File:Umeyama Kofun, zenkei-2.jpg|280px]]
Tumulus and moat
(Rear circular mound on the right, front rectangular mound in the left background) | location = {{ill|Asuka, Nara|ja|明日香村}}, Nara Prefecture | coordinates = {{Coord|34|28|7.38|N|135|48|2.80|E|region:JP-29_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳}} | Dimensions = Total length: 140m | Burial facility = Unknown | Construction era = Late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} | Interred individual = ({{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designation) {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇}} | Excavated items = Unknown | Mausoleum = Designated by the Imperial Household Agency as "Hinokuma Sakaai no misasagi" (檜隈坂合陵) | Map = {{location map|Japan Nara|lat_deg=34|lat_min=28|lat_sec=7.38|lon_deg=135|lon_min=48|lon_sec=2.8|label=Umeyama Kofun|mark=前方後円墳-西.svg|marksize=23|caption=location in Nara Prefecture|position=bottom|width=200|float=center|border=none}} }} [[File:Umeyama kohun aerial.jpg|thumb|220px|Bottom left: Kibi-hime Royal Tomb and Saruishi statues ({{National Land Aerial Photography}})]] [[File:Umeyama Kofun, haisho.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Mausoleum of {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇}}}} '''Umeyama Kofun''' (梅山古墳) is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳}} located in {{ill|Asuka, Nara|ja|明日香村}}, Nara Prefecture. The actual interred individual is unknown, but it has been designated by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} as the "Hinokuma Sakaai no misasagi" (檜隈坂合陵), attributed to {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇}}. == Overview == The tumulus features a keyhole shape, with the front mound oriented to the west along an east-west axis. Measuring approximately 140 meters in total length and 72 meters in diameter for the rear mound, it is the largest tumulus in Asuka Village. The mound is notable for its extensive use of {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}} (covering stones). Surrounding the tumulus is a water-filled moat, which was extensively remodeled during the {{ill|Bunkyū era|ja|文久}} restoration. Originally, the surrounding area was agricultural land. Some theories suggest that the restoration converted the tumulus from a double circular mound to its current keyhole shape.Theory proposed by {{ill|Kōichi Mori|ja|森浩一}} The tumulus is part of a group of burial mounds, including {{ill|Hirata Iwaya Kofun|ja|平田岩屋古墳}}, {{ill|Oni no Manaita and Oni no Senkan|ja|鬼の俎・鬼の雪隠}}, and {{ill|Noguchi Royal Tomb|ja|野口王墓}} (the joint tomb of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|天武天皇}} and {{ill|Empress Jitō|ja|持統天皇}}), all situated along the southern slope of the same hill. == Construction Period == While believed to have been built during the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, the exact construction date is uncertain due to the lack of archaeological excavations. == Interred Individual == Although designated by the Imperial Household Agency as the tomb of {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇}}, many scholars suggest that the nearby {{ill|Mise Maruyama Kofun|ja|見瀬丸山古墳}}, located approximately 800 meters northwest and the largest tumulus in Nara Prefecture, might be a more likely candidate. Other theories propose that {{ill|Soga no Iname|ja|蘇我稲目}} may be the interred individual. == Saruishi Statues == Adjacent to the {{ill|Tomb of Princess Kibi|ja|吉備姫王墓}}, stone statues known as {{ill|Saruishi|ja|猿石}} ("monkey stones") are placed nearby. These statues were excavated during the Edo period from a field just south of Umeyama Kofun and relocated to their current location in the early {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治時代}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Kanazuka Kofun|ja|カナヅカ古墳}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Umeyama Kofun}} * [https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/guide/029/index.html Hinokuma Sakaai no misasagi] - Imperial Household Agency [[Category:Ancient tumuli in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Asuka, Nara]] [[Category:Keyhole-shaped tumuli]] [[Category:Imperial mausolea in Japan]] [[Category:Emperor Kinmei]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:defautsort]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Umeyama Kofun}}[[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:defautsort]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Umeyama Kofun}}[[category:unsorted completion categories]] {{DEFAULTSORT::Umeyama Kofun}}[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:defautsort]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Umeyama Kofun}}[[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:defautsort]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Umeyama Kofun}}[[category:unsorted completion categories]] {{DEFAULTSORT::Umeyama Kofun}}[[Category:Umeyama Kofun]][[Category:mass bump]] [[Category:bump 1-20 (2)]] [[ja:梅山古墳]]

The mound is notable for its extensive use of {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}} (covering stones). Surrounding the tumulus is a water-filled moat, which was extensively remodeled during the {{ill|Bunkyū era|ja|文久}} restoration. Originally, the surrounding area was agricultural land. Some theories suggest that the restoration converted the tumulus from a double circular mound to its current keyhole shape.Theory proposed by {{ill|Kōichi Mori|ja|森浩一}} The tumulus is part of a group of burial mounds, including {{ill|Hirata Iwaya Kofun|ja|平田岩屋古墳}}, {{ill|Oni no Manaita and Oni no Senkan|ja|鬼の俎・鬼の雪隠}}, and {{ill|Noguchi Royal Tomb|ja|野口王墓}} (the joint tomb of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|天武天皇}} and {{ill|Empress Jitō|ja|持統天皇}}), all situated along the southern slope of the same hill. == Construction Period == While believed to have been built during the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, the exact construction date is uncertain due to the lack of archaeological excavations. == Interred Individual == Although designated by the Imperial Household Agency as the tomb of {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇}}, many scholars suggest that the nearby {{ill|Mise Maruyama Kofun|ja|見瀬丸山古墳}}, located approximately 800 meters northwest and the largest tumulus in Nara Prefecture, might be a more likely candidate. Other theories propose that {{ill|Soga no Iname|ja|蘇我稲目}} may be the interred individual. == Saruishi Statues == Adjacent to the {{ill|Tomb of Princess Kibi|ja|吉備姫王墓}}, stone statues known as {{ill|Saruishi|ja|猿石}} ("monkey stones") are placed nearby. These statues were excavated during the Edo period from a field just south of Umeyama Kofun and relocated to their current location in the early {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治時代}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Kanazuka Kofun|ja|カナヅカ古墳}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Umeyama Kofun}} * [https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/guide/029/index.html Hinokuma Sakaai no misasagi] - Imperial Household Agency [[Category:Ancient tumuli in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Asuka, Nara]] [[Category:Keyhole-shaped tumuli]] [[Category:Imperial mausolea in Japan]] [[Category:Emperor Kinmei]] [[Category:defautsort]] {{citation needed|Umeyama Kofun}}[[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:defautsort]] {{citation needed|Umeyama Kofun}}[[category:unsorted completion categories]] {{citation needed|Umeyama Kofun}} [[Category:defautsort]] {{citation needed|Umeyama Kofun}}[[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:defautsort]] {{citation needed|Umeyama Kofun}}[[category:unsorted completion categories]] {{citation needed|Umeyama Kofun}}[[Category:Umeyama Kofun]][[Category:mass bump]] [[Category:bump 1-20 (2)]] [[ja:梅山古墳]]

(Rear circular mound on the right, front rectangular mound in the left background) | location = {{ill|Asuka, Nara|ja|明日香村}}, Nara Prefecture | coordinates = {{Coord|34|28|7.38|N|135|48|2.80|E|region:JP-29_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳}} | Dimensions = Total length: 140m | Burial facility = Unknown | Construction era = Late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} | Interred individual = ({{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designation) {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇}} | Excavated items = Unknown | Mausoleum = Designated by the Imperial Household Agency as "Hinokuma Sakaai no misasagi" (檜隈坂合陵) | Map = {{location map|Japan Nara|lat_deg=34|lat_min=28|lat_sec=7.38|lon_deg=135|lon_min=48|lon_sec=2.8|label=Umeyama Kofun|mark=前方後円墳-西.svg|marksize=23|caption=location in Nara Prefecture|position=bottom|width=200|float=center|border=none}} }} [[File:Umeyama kohun aerial.jpg|thumb|220px|Bottom left: Kibi-hime Royal Tomb and Saruishi statues ({{National Land Aerial Photography}})]] [[File:Umeyama Kofun, haisho.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Mausoleum of {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇}}]] '''Umeyama Kofun''' (梅山古墳) is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳}} located in {{ill|Asuka, Nara|ja|明日香村}}, Nara Prefecture. The actual interred individual is unknown, but it has been designated by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} as the "Hinokuma Sakaai no misasagi" (檜隈坂合陵), attributed to {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇}}. == Overview == The tumulus features a keyhole shape, with the front mound oriented to the west along an east-west axis. Measuring approximately 140 meters in total length and 72 meters in diameter for the rear mound, it is the largest tumulus in Asuka Village. The mound is notable for its extensive use of {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}} (covering stones). Surrounding the tumulus is a water-filled moat, which was extensively remodeled during the {{ill|Bunkyū era|ja|文久}} restoration. Originally, the surrounding area was agricultural land. Some theories suggest that the restoration converted the tumulus from a double circular mound to its current keyhole shape.Theory proposed by {{ill|Kōichi Mori|ja|森浩一}} The tumulus is part of a group of burial mounds, including {{ill|Hirata Iwaya Kofun|ja|平田岩屋古墳}}, {{ill|Oni no Manaita and Oni no Senkan|ja|鬼の俎・鬼の雪隠}}, and {{ill|Noguchi Royal Tomb|ja|野口王墓}} (the joint tomb of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|天武天皇}} and {{ill|Empress Jitō|ja|持統天皇}}), all situated along the southern slope of the same hill. == Construction Period == While believed to have been built during the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, the exact construction date is uncertain due to the lack of archaeological excavations. == Interred Individual == Although designated by the Imperial Household Agency as the tomb of {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇}}, many scholars suggest that the nearby {{ill|Mise Maruyama Kofun|ja|見瀬丸山古墳}}, located approximately 800 meters northwest and the largest tumulus in Nara Prefecture, might be a more likely candidate. Other theories propose that {{ill|Soga no Iname|ja|蘇我稲目}} may be the interred individual. == Saruishi Statues == Adjacent to the {{ill|Tomb of Princess Kibi|ja|吉備姫王墓}}, stone statues known as {{ill|Saruishi|ja|猿石}} ("monkey stones") are placed nearby. These statues were excavated during the Edo period from a field just south of Umeyama Kofun and relocated to their current location in the early {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治時代}}. == References == (Rear circular mound on the right, front rectangular mound in the left background) | location = {{ill|Asuka, Nara|ja|明日香村}}, Nara Prefecture | coordinates = {{Coord|34|28|7.38|N|135|48|2.80|E|region:JP-29_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳}} | Dimensions = Total length: 140m | Burial facility = Unknown | Construction era = Late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} | Interred individual = ({{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designation) {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇}} | Excavated items = Unknown | Mausoleum = Designated by the Imperial Household Agency as "Hinokuma Sakaai no misasagi" (檜隈坂合陵) | Map = {{location map|Japan Nara|lat_deg=34|lat_min=28|lat_sec=7.38|lon_deg=135|lon_min=48|lon_sec=2.8|label=Umeyama Kofun|mark=前方後円墳-西.svg|marksize=23|caption=location in Nara Prefecture|position=bottom|width=200|float=center|border=none}} }} [[File:Umeyama kohun aerial.jpg|thumb|220px|Bottom left: Kibi-hime Royal Tomb and Saruishi statues ({{National Land Aerial Photography}})]] [[File:Umeyama Kofun, haisho.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Mausoleum of {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇}}]] '''Umeyama Kofun''' (梅山古墳) is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳}} located in {{ill|Asuka, Nara|ja|明日香村}}, Nara Prefecture. The actual interred individual is unknown, but it has been designated by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} as the "Hinokuma Sakaai no misasagi" (檜隈坂合陵), attributed to {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇}}. == Overview == The tumulus features a keyhole shape, with the front mound oriented to the west along an east-west axis. Measuring approximately 140 meters in total length and 72 meters in diameter for the rear mound, it is the largest tumulus in Asuka Village. The mound is notable for its extensive use of {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}} (covering stones). Surrounding the tumulus is a water-filled moat, which was extensively remodeled during the {{ill|Bunkyū era|ja|文久}} restoration. Originally, the surrounding area was agricultural land. Some theories suggest that the restoration converted the tumulus from a double circular mound to its current keyhole shape.Theory proposed by {{ill|Kōichi Mori|ja|森浩一}} The tumulus is part of a group of burial mounds, including {{ill|Hirata Iwaya Kofun|ja|平田岩屋古墳}}, {{ill|Oni no Manaita and Oni no Senkan|ja|鬼の俎・鬼の雪隠}}, and {{ill|Noguchi Royal Tomb|ja|野口王墓}} (the joint tomb of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|天武天皇}} and {{ill|Empress Jitō|ja|持統天皇}}), all situated along the southern slope of the same hill. == Construction Period == While believed to have been built during the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, the exact construction date is uncertain due to the lack of archaeological excavations. == Interred Individual == Although designated by the Imperial Household Agency as the tomb of {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇}}, many scholars suggest that the nearby {{ill|Mise Maruyama Kofun|ja|見瀬丸山古墳}}, located approximately 800 meters northwest and the largest tumulus in Nara Prefecture, might be a more likely candidate. Other theories propose that {{ill|Soga no Iname|ja|蘇我稲目}} may be the interred individual. == Saruishi Statues == Adjacent to the {{ill|Tomb of Princess Kibi|ja|吉備姫王墓}}, stone statues known as {{ill|Saruishi|ja|猿石}} ("monkey stones") are placed nearby. These statues were excavated during the Edo period from a field just south of Umeyama Kofun and relocated to their current location in the early {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治時代}}. == References == The actual interred individual is unknown, but it has been designated by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} as the "Hinokuma Sakaai no misasagi" (檜隈坂合陵), attributed to {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇}}. == Overview == The tumulus features a keyhole shape, with the front mound oriented to the west along an east-west axis. Measuring approximately 140 meters in total length and 72 meters in diameter for the rear mound, it is the largest tumulus in Asuka Village. The mound is notable for its extensive use of {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}} (covering stones). Surrounding the tumulus is a water-filled moat, which was extensively remodeled during the {{ill|Bunkyū era|ja|文久}} restoration. Originally, the surrounding area was agricultural land. Some theories suggest that the restoration converted the tumulus from a double circular mound to its current keyhole shape.Theory proposed by {{ill|Kōichi Mori|ja|森浩一}} The tumulus is part of a group of burial mounds, including {{ill|Hirata Iwaya Kofun|ja|平田岩屋古墳}}, {{ill|Oni no Manaita and Oni no Senkan|ja|鬼の俎・鬼の雪隠}}, and {{ill|Noguchi Royal Tomb|ja|野口王墓}} (the joint tomb of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|天武天皇}} and {{ill|Empress Jitō|ja|持統天皇}}), all situated along the southern slope of the same hill. == Construction Period == While believed to have been built during the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, the exact construction date is uncertain due to the lack of archaeological excavations. == Interred Individual == Although designated by the Imperial Household Agency as the tomb of {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇}}, many scholars suggest that the nearby {{ill|Mise Maruyama Kofun|ja|見瀬丸山古墳}}, located approximately 800 meters northwest and the largest tumulus in Nara Prefecture, might be a more likely candidate. Other theories propose that {{ill|Soga no Iname|ja|蘇我稲目}} may be the interred individual. == Saruishi Statues == Adjacent to the {{ill|Tomb of Princess Kibi|ja|吉備姫王墓}}, stone statues known as {{ill|Saruishi|ja|猿石}} ("monkey stones") are placed nearby. These statues were excavated during the Edo period from a field just south of Umeyama Kofun and relocated to their current location in the early {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治時代}}. == References == Kofun]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:pages needing attribution]] {{citation needed|Umeyama Kofun}}[[ja:梅山古墳]] {{Infobox ancient site | name = Umeyama Kofun | image = [[File:Umeyama Kofun, zenkei-2.jpg|280px]]
Tumulus and moat
(Rear circular mound on the right, front rectangular mound in the left background) | location = {{ill|Asuka, Nara|ja|明日香村}}, Nara Prefecture | coordinates = {{Coord|34|28|7.38|N|135|48|2.80|E|region:JP-29_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳}} | Dimensions = Total length: 140m | Burial facility = Unknown | Construction era = Late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} | Interred individual = ({{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designation) {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇}} | Excavated items = Unknown | Mausoleum = Designated by the Imperial Household Agency as "Hinokuma Sakaai no misasagi" (檜隈坂合陵) | Map = {{location map|Japan Nara|lat_deg=34|lat_min=28|lat_sec=7.38|lon_deg=135|lon_min=48|lon_sec=2.8|label=Umeyama Kofun|mark=前方後円墳-西.svg|marksize=23|caption=location in Nara Prefecture|position=bottom|width=200|float=center|border=none}} }} [[File:Umeyama kohun aerial.jpg|thumb|220px|Bottom left: Kibi-hime Royal Tomb and Saruishi statues ({{National Land Aerial Photography}})]] [[File:Umeyama Kofun, haisho.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Mausoleum of {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇}}]] '''Umeyama Kofun''' (梅山古墳) is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳}} located in {{ill|Asuka, Nara|ja|明日香村}}, Nara Prefecture. The actual interred individual is unknown, but it has been designated by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} as the "Hinokuma Sakaai no misasagi" (檜隈坂合陵), attributed to {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇}}. == Overview == The tumulus features a keyhole shape, with the front mound oriented to the west along an east-west axis. Measuring approximately 140 meters in total length and 72 meters in diameter for the rear mound, it is the largest tumulus in Asuka Village. The mound is notable for its extensive use of {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}} (covering stones). Surrounding the tumulus is a water-filled moat, which was extensively remodeled during the {{ill|Bunkyū era|ja|文久}} restoration. Originally, the surrounding area was agricultural land. Some theories suggest that the restoration converted the tumulus from a double circular mound to its current keyhole shape.Theory proposed by {{ill|Kōichi Mori|ja|森浩一}} The tumulus is part of a group of burial mounds, including {{ill|Hirata Iwaya Kofun|ja|平田岩屋古墳}}, {{ill|Oni no Manaita and Oni no Senkan|ja|鬼の俎・鬼の雪隠}}, and {{ill|Noguchi Royal Tomb|ja|野口王墓}} (the joint tomb of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|天武天皇}} and {{ill|Empress Jitō|ja|持統天皇}}), all situated along the southern slope of the same hill. == Construction Period == While believed to have been built during the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, the exact construction date is uncertain due to the lack of archaeological excavations. == Interred Individual == Although designated by the Imperial Household Agency as the tomb of {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇}}, many scholars suggest that the nearby {{ill|Mise Maruyama Kofun|ja|見瀬丸山古墳}}, located approximately 800 meters northwest and the largest tumulus in Nara Prefecture, might be a more likely candidate. Other theories propose that {{ill|Soga no Iname|ja|蘇我稲目}} may be the interred individual. == Saruishi Statues == Adjacent to the {{ill|Tomb of Princess Kibi|ja|吉備姫王墓}}, stone statues known as {{ill|Saruishi|ja|猿石}} ("monkey stones") are placed nearby. These statues were excavated during the Edo period from a field just south of Umeyama Kofun and relocated to their current location in the early {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治時代}}. == References == Kofun]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:pages needing attribution]] {{citation needed|Umeyama Kofun}}[[ja:梅山古墳]] {{Infobox ancient site | name = Umeyama Kofun | image = [[File:Umeyama Kofun, zenkei-2.jpg|280px]]
Tumulus and moat
(Rear circular mound on the right, front rectangular mound in the left background) | location = {{ill|Asuka, Nara|ja|明日香村}}, Nara Prefecture | coordinates = {{Coord|34|28|7.38|N|135|48|2.80|E|region:JP-29_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳}} | Dimensions = Total length: 140m | Burial facility = Unknown | Construction era = Late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} | Interred individual = ({{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designation) {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇}} | Excavated items = Unknown | Mausoleum = Designated by the Imperial Household Agency as "Hinokuma Sakaai no misasagi" (檜隈坂合陵) | Map = {{location map|Japan Nara|lat_deg=34|lat_min=28|lat_sec=7.38|lon_deg=135|lon_min=48|lon_sec=2.8|label=Umeyama Kofun|mark=前方後円墳-西.svg|marksize=23|caption=location in Nara Prefecture|position=bottom|width=200|float=center|border=none}} }} [[File:Umeyama kohun aerial.jpg|thumb|220px|Bottom left: Kibi-hime Royal Tomb and Saruishi statues ({{National Land Aerial Photography}})]] [[File:Umeyama Kofun, haisho.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Mausoleum of {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇}}]] '''Umeyama Kofun''' (梅山古墳) is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳}} located in {{ill|Asuka, Nara|ja|明日香村}}, Nara Prefecture. The actual interred individual is unknown, but it has been designated by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} as the "Hinokuma Sakaai no misasagi" (檜隈坂合陵), attributed to {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇}}. == Overview == The tumulus features a keyhole shape, with the front mound oriented to the west along an east-west axis. Measuring approximately 140 meters in total length and 72 meters in diameter for the rear mound, it is the largest tumulus in Asuka Village. The mound is notable for its extensive use of {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}} (covering stones). Surrounding the tumulus is a water-filled moat, which was extensively remodeled during the {{ill|Bunkyū era|ja|文久}} restoration. Originally, the surrounding area was agricultural land. Some theories suggest that the restoration converted the tumulus from a double circular mound to its current keyhole shape.Theory proposed by {{ill|Kōichi Mori|ja|森浩一}} The tumulus is part of a group of burial mounds, including {{ill|Hirata Iwaya Kofun|ja|平田岩屋古墳}}, {{ill|Oni no Manaita and Oni no Senkan|ja|鬼の俎・鬼の雪隠}}, and {{ill|Noguchi Royal Tomb|ja|野口王墓}} (the joint tomb of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|天武天皇}} and {{ill|Empress Jitō|ja|持統天皇}}), all situated along the southern slope of the same hill. == Construction Period == While believed to have been built during the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, the exact construction date is uncertain due to the lack of archaeological excavations. == Interred Individual == Although designated by the Imperial Household Agency as the tomb of {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇}}, many scholars suggest that the nearby {{ill|Mise Maruyama Kofun|ja|見瀬丸山古墳}}, located approximately 800 meters northwest and the largest tumulus in Nara Prefecture, might be a more likely candidate. Other theories propose that {{ill|Soga no Iname|ja|蘇我稲目}} may be the interred individual. == Saruishi Statues == Adjacent to the {{ill|Tomb of Princess Kibi|ja|吉備姫王墓}}, stone statues known as {{ill|Saruishi|ja|猿石}} ("monkey stones") are placed nearby. These statues were excavated during the Edo period from a field just south of Umeyama Kofun and relocated to their current location in the early {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治時代}}. == References == Kofun]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]]{{citation needed|Umeyama Kofun}}[[ja:梅山古墳]] {{Infobox ancient site | name = Umeyama Kofun | image = [[File:Umeyama Kofun, zenkei-2.jpg|280px]]
Tumulus and moat
(Rear circular mound on the right, front rectangular mound in the left background) | location = {{ill|Asuka, Nara|ja|明日香村}}, Nara Prefecture | coordinates = {{Coord|34|28|7.38|N|135|48|2.80|E|region:JP-29_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳}} | Dimensions = Total length: 140m | Burial facility = Unknown | Construction era = Late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} | Interred individual = ({{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designation) {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇}} | Excavated items = Unknown | Mausoleum = Designated by the Imperial Household Agency as "Hinokuma Sakaai no misasagi" (檜隈坂合陵) | Map = {{location map|Japan Nara|lat_deg=34|lat_min=28|lat_sec=7.38|lon_deg=135|lon_min=48|lon_sec=2.8|label=Umeyama Kofun|mark=前方後円墳-西.svg|marksize=23|caption=location in Nara Prefecture|position=bottom|width=200|float=center|border=none}} }} [[File:Umeyama kohun aerial.jpg|thumb|220px|Bottom left: Kibi-hime Royal Tomb and Saruishi statues ({{National Land Aerial Photography}})]] [[File:Umeyama Kofun, haisho.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Mausoleum of {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇}}]] '''Umeyama Kofun''' (梅山古墳) is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳}} located in {{ill|Asuka, Nara|ja|明日香村}}, Nara Prefecture. The actual interred individual is unknown, but it has been designated by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} as the "Hinokuma Sakaai no misasagi" (檜隈坂合陵), attributed to {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇}}. == Overview == The tumulus features a keyhole shape, with the front mound oriented to the west along an east-west axis. Measuring approximately 140 meters in total length and 72 meters in diameter for the rear mound, it is the largest tumulus in Asuka Village. The mound is notable for its extensive use of {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}} (covering stones). Surrounding the tumulus is a water-filled moat, which was extensively remodeled during the {{ill|Bunkyū era|ja|文久}} restoration. Originally, the surrounding area was agricultural land. Some theories suggest that the restoration converted the tumulus from a double circular mound to its current keyhole shape.Theory proposed by {{ill|Kōichi Mori|ja|森浩一}} The tumulus is part of a group of burial mounds, including {{ill|Hirata Iwaya Kofun|ja|平田岩屋古墳}}, {{ill|Oni no Manaita and Oni no Senkan|ja|鬼の俎・鬼の雪隠}}, and {{ill|Noguchi Royal Tomb|ja|野口王墓}} (the joint tomb of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|天武天皇}} and {{ill|Empress Jitō|ja|持統天皇}}), all situated along the southern slope of the same hill. == Construction Period == While believed to have been built during the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, the exact construction date is uncertain due to the lack of archaeological excavations. == Interred Individual == Although designated by the Imperial Household Agency as the tomb of {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇}}, many scholars suggest that the nearby {{ill|Mise Maruyama Kofun|ja|見瀬丸山古墳}}, located approximately 800 meters northwest and the largest tumulus in Nara Prefecture, might be a more likely candidate. Other theories propose that {{ill|Soga no Iname|ja|蘇我稲目}} may be the interred individual. == Saruishi Statues == Adjacent to the {{ill|Tomb of Princess Kibi|ja|吉備姫王墓}}, stone statues known as {{ill|Saruishi|ja|猿石}} ("monkey stones") are placed nearby. These statues were excavated during the Edo period from a field just south of Umeyama Kofun and relocated to their current location in the early {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治時代}}. == References == Tumulus and moat
(Rear circular mound on the right, front rectangular mound in the left background) | location = {{ill|Asuka, Nara|ja|明日香村}}, Nara Prefecture | coordinates = {{Coord|34|28|7.38|N|135|48|2.80|E|region:JP-29_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳}} | Dimensions = Total length: 140m | Burial facility = Unknown | Construction era = Late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} | Interred individual = ({{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designation) {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇}} | Excavated items = Unknown | Mausoleum = Designated by the Imperial Household Agency as "Hinokuma Sakaai no misasagi" (檜隈坂合陵) | Map = {{location map|Japan Nara|lat_deg=34|lat_min=28|lat_sec=7.38|lon_deg=135|lon_min=48|lon_sec=2.8|label=Umeyama Kofun|mark=前方後円墳-西.svg|marksize=23|caption=location in Nara Prefecture|position=bottom|width=200|float=center|border=none}} }} [[File:Umeyama kohun aerial.jpg|thumb|220px|Bottom left: Kibi-hime Royal Tomb and Saruishi statues ({{National Land Aerial Photography}})]] [[File:Umeyama Kofun, haisho.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Mausoleum of {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇}}]] '''Umeyama Kofun''' (梅山古墳) is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳}} located in {{ill|Asuka, Nara|ja|明日香村}}, Nara Prefecture. The actual interred individual is unknown, but it has been designated by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} as the "Hinokuma Sakaai no misasagi" (檜隈坂合陵), attributed to {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇}}. == Overview == The tumulus features a keyhole shape, with the front mound oriented to the west along an east-west axis. Measuring approximately 140 meters in total length and 72 meters in diameter for the rear mound, it is the largest tumulus in Asuka Village. The mound is notable for its extensive use of {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}} (covering stones). Surrounding the tumulus is a water-filled moat, which was extensively remodeled during the {{ill|Bunkyū era|ja|文久}} restoration. Originally, the surrounding area was agricultural land. Some theories suggest that the restoration converted the tumulus from a double circular mound to its current keyhole shape.Theory proposed by {{ill|Kōichi Mori|ja|森浩一}} The tumulus is part of a group of burial mounds, including {{ill|Hirata Iwaya Kofun|ja|平田岩屋古墳}}, {{ill|Oni no Manaita and Oni no Senkan|ja|鬼の俎・鬼の雪隠}}, and {{ill|Noguchi Royal Tomb|ja|野口王墓}} (the joint tomb of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|天武天皇}} and {{ill|Empress Jitō|ja|持統天皇}}), all situated along the southern slope of the same hill. == Construction Period == While believed to have been built during the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, the exact construction date is uncertain due to the lack of archaeological excavations. == Interred Individual == Although designated by the Imperial Household Agency as the tomb of {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇}}, many scholars suggest that the nearby {{ill|Mise Maruyama Kofun|ja|見瀬丸山古墳}}, located approximately 800 meters northwest and the largest tumulus in Nara Prefecture, might be a more likely candidate. Other theories propose that {{ill|Soga no Iname|ja|蘇我稲目}} may be the interred individual. == Saruishi Statues == Adjacent to the {{ill|Tomb of Princess Kibi|ja|吉備姫王墓}}, stone statues known as {{ill|Saruishi|ja|猿石}} ("monkey stones") are placed nearby. These statues were excavated during the Edo period from a field just south of Umeyama Kofun and relocated to their current location in the early {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治時代}}. == References ==
