Tumulus and moat
(Rear circular mound on the right, front rectangular mound in the left background) | location = {{ill|Asuka, Nara|ja|明日香村}}, Nara Prefecture | coordinates = {{Coord|34|28|7.38|N|135|48|2.80|E|region:JP-29_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳}} | Dimensions = Total length: 140m | Burial facility = Unknown | Construction era = Late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} | Interred individual = ({{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designation) {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇}} | Excavated items = Unknown | Mausoleum = Designated by the Imperial Household Agency as "Hinokuma Sakaai no misasagi" (檜隈坂合陵) | Map = {{location map|Japan Nara|lat_deg=34|lat_min=28|lat_sec=7.38|lon_deg=135|lon_min=48|lon_sec=2.8|label=Umeyama Kofun|mark=前方後円墳-西.svg|marksize=23|caption=location in Nara Prefecture|position=bottom|width=200|float=center|border=none}} }} [[File:Umeyama kohun aerial.jpg|thumb|220px|Bottom left: Kibi-hime Royal Tomb and Saruishi statues ({{National Land Aerial Photography}})]] [[File:Umeyama Kofun, haisho.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Mausoleum of {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇}}]] '''Umeyama Kofun''' (梅山古墳) is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳}} located in {{ill|Asuka, Nara|ja|明日香村}}, Nara Prefecture. The actual interred individual is unknown, but it has been designated by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} as the "Hinokuma Sakaai no misasagi" (檜隈坂合陵), attributed to {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇}}. == Overview == The tumulus features a keyhole shape, with the front mound oriented to the west along an east-west axis. Measuring approximately 140 meters in total length and 72 meters in diameter for the rear mound, it is the largest tumulus in Asuka Village. The mound is notable for its extensive use of {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}} (covering stones). Surrounding the tumulus is a water-filled moat, which was extensively remodeled during the {{ill|Bunkyū era|ja|文久}} restoration. Originally, the surrounding area was agricultural land. Some theories suggest that the restoration converted the tumulus from a double circular mound to its current keyhole shape.Theory proposed by {{ill|Kōichi Mori|ja|森浩一}} The tumulus is part of a group of burial mounds, including {{ill|Hirata Iwaya Kofun|ja|平田岩屋古墳}}, {{ill|Oni no Manaita and Oni no Senkan|ja|鬼の俎・鬼の雪隠}}, and {{ill|Noguchi Royal Tomb|ja|野口王墓}} (the joint tomb of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|天武天皇}} and {{ill|Empress Jitō|ja|持統天皇}}), all situated along the southern slope of the same hill. == Construction Period == While believed to have been built during the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, the exact construction date is uncertain due to the lack of archaeological excavations. == Interred Individual == Although designated by the Imperial Household Agency as the tomb of {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇}}, many scholars suggest that the nearby {{ill|Mise Maruyama Kofun|ja|見瀬丸山古墳}}, located approximately 800 meters northwest and the largest tumulus in Nara Prefecture, might be a more likely candidate. Other theories propose that {{ill|Soga no Iname|ja|蘇我稲目}} may be the interred individual. == Saruishi Statues == Adjacent to the {{ill|Tomb of Princess Kibi|ja|吉備姫王墓}}, stone statues known as {{ill|Saruishi|ja|猿石}} ("monkey stones") are placed nearby. These statues were excavated during the Edo period from a field just south of Umeyama Kofun and relocated to their current location in the early {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治時代}}. == References == [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Umeyama Kofun]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]]{{citation needed|Umeyama Kofun}}[[ja:梅山古墳]] {{Infobox ancient site | name = Umeyama Kofun | image = [[File:Umeyama Kofun, zenkei-2.jpg|280px]]
Tumulus and moat
(Rear circular mound on the right, front rectangular mound in the left background) | location = {{ill|Asuka, Nara|ja|明日香村}}, Nara Prefecture | coordinates = {{Coord|34|28|7.38|N|135|48|2.80|E|region:JP-29_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳}} | Dimensions = Total length: 140m | Burial facility = Unknown | Construction era = Late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} | Interred individual = ({{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designation) {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇}} | Excavated items = Unknown | Mausoleum = Designated by the Imperial Household Agency as "Hinokuma Sakaai no misasagi" (檜隈坂合陵) | Map = {{location map|Japan Nara|lat_deg=34|lat_min=28|lat_sec=7.38|lon_deg=135|lon_min=48|lon_sec=2.8|label=Umeyama Kofun|mark=前方後円墳-西.svg|marksize=23|caption=location in Nara Prefecture|position=bottom|width=200|float=center|border=none}} }} [[File:Umeyama kohun aerial.jpg|thumb|220px|Bottom left: Kibi-hime Royal Tomb and Saruishi statues ({{National Land Aerial Photography}})]] [[File:Umeyama Kofun, haisho.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Mausoleum of {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇}}]] '''Umeyama Kofun''' (梅山古墳) is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳}} located in {{ill|Asuka, Nara|ja|明日香村}}, Nara Prefecture. The actual interred individual is unknown, but it has been designated by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} as the "Hinokuma Sakaai no misasagi" (檜隈坂合陵), attributed to {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇}}. == Overview == The tumulus features a keyhole shape, with the front mound oriented to the west along an east-west axis. Measuring approximately 140 meters in total length and 72 meters in diameter for the rear mound, it is the largest tumulus in Asuka Village. The mound is notable for its extensive use of {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}} (covering stones). Surrounding the tumulus is a water-filled moat, which was extensively remodeled during the {{ill|Bunkyū era|ja|文久}} restoration. Originally, the surrounding area was agricultural land. Some theories suggest that the restoration converted the tumulus from a double circular mound to its current keyhole shape.Theory proposed by {{ill|Kōichi Mori|ja|森浩一}} The tumulus is part of a group of burial mounds, including {{ill|Hirata Iwaya Kofun|ja|平田岩屋古墳}}, {{ill|Oni no Manaita and Oni no Senkan|ja|鬼の俎・鬼の雪隠}}, and {{ill|Noguchi Royal Tomb|ja|野口王墓}} (the joint tomb of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|天武天皇}} and {{ill|Empress Jitō|ja|持統天皇}}), all situated along the southern slope of the same hill. == Construction Period == While believed to have been built during the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, the exact construction date is uncertain due to the lack of archaeological excavations. == Interred Individual == Although designated by the Imperial Household Agency as the tomb of {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇}}, many scholars suggest that the nearby {{ill|Mise Maruyama Kofun|ja|見瀬丸山古墳}}, located approximately 800 meters northwest and the largest tumulus in Nara Prefecture, might be a more likely candidate. Other theories propose that {{ill|Soga no Iname|ja|蘇我稲目}} may be the interred individual. == Saruishi Statues == Adjacent to the {{ill|Tomb of Princess Kibi|ja|吉備姫王墓}}, stone statues known as {{ill|Saruishi|ja|猿石}} ("monkey stones") are placed nearby. These statues were excavated during the Edo period from a field just south of Umeyama Kofun and relocated to their current location in the early {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治時代}}. == References == These statues were excavated during the Edo period from a field just south of Umeyama Kofun and relocated to their current location in the early {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治時代}}. == References == [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Umeyama Kofun]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]]{{citation needed|Umeyama Kofun}}[[ja:梅山古墳]] {{Infobox ancient site | name = Umeyama Kofun | image = [[File:Umeyama Kofun, zenkei-2.jpg|280px]]
Tumulus and moat
(Rear circular mound on the right, front rectangular mound in the left background) | location = {{ill|Asuka, Nara|ja|明日香村}}, Nara Prefecture | coordinates = {{Coord|34|28|7.38|N|135|48|2.80|E|region:JP-29_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳}} | Dimensions = Total length: 140m | Burial facility = Unknown | Construction era = Late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} | Interred individual = ({{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designation) {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇}} | Excavated items = Unknown | Mausoleum = Designated by the Imperial Household Agency as "Hinokuma Sakaai no misasagi" (檜隈坂合陵) | Map = {{location map|Japan Nara|lat_deg=34|lat_min=28|lat_sec=7.38|lon_deg=135|lon_min=48|lon_sec=2.8|label=Umeyama Kofun|mark=前方後円墳-西.svg|marksize=23|caption=location in Nara Prefecture|position=bottom|width=200|float=center|border=none}} }} [[File:Umeyama kohun aerial.jpg|thumb|220px|Bottom left: Kibi-hime Royal Tomb and Saruishi statues ({{National Land Aerial Photography}})]] [[File:Umeyama Kofun, haisho.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Mausoleum of {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇}}]] '''Umeyama Kofun''' (梅山古墳) is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳}} located in {{ill|Asuka, Nara|ja|明日香村}}, Nara Prefecture. The actual interred individual is unknown, but it has been designated by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} as the "Hinokuma Sakaai no misasagi" (檜隈坂合陵), attributed to {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇}}. == Overview == The tumulus features a keyhole shape, with the front mound oriented to the west along an east-west axis. Measuring approximately 140 meters in total length and 72 meters in diameter for the rear mound, it is the largest tumulus in Asuka Village. The mound is notable for its extensive use of {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}} (covering stones). Surrounding the tumulus is a water-filled moat, which was extensively remodeled during the {{ill|Bunkyū era|ja|文久}} restoration. Originally, the surrounding area was agricultural land. Some theories suggest that the restoration converted the tumulus from a double circular mound to its current keyhole shape.Theory proposed by {{ill|Kōichi Mori|ja|森浩一}} The tumulus is part of a group of burial mounds, including {{ill|Hirata Iwaya Kofun|ja|平田岩屋古墳}}, {{ill|Oni no Manaita and Oni no Senkan|ja|鬼の俎・鬼の雪隠}}, and {{ill|Noguchi Royal Tomb|ja|野口王墓}} (the joint tomb of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|天武天皇}} and {{ill|Empress Jitō|ja|持統天皇}}), all situated along the southern slope of the same hill. == Construction Period == While believed to have been built during the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, the exact construction date is uncertain due to the lack of archaeological excavations. == Interred Individual == Although designated by the Imperial Household Agency as the tomb of {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇}}, many scholars suggest that the nearby {{ill|Mise Maruyama Kofun|ja|見瀬丸山古墳}}, located approximately 800 meters northwest and the largest tumulus in Nara Prefecture, might be a more likely candidate. Other theories propose that {{ill|Soga no Iname|ja|蘇我稲目}} may be the interred individual. == Saruishi Statues == Adjacent to the {{ill|Tomb of Princess Kibi|ja|吉備姫王墓}}, stone statues known as {{ill|Saruishi|ja|猿石}} ("monkey stones") are placed nearby. These statues were excavated during the Edo period from a field just south of Umeyama Kofun and relocated to their current location in the early {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治時代}}. == References == misasagi] - Imperial Household Agency [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Ancient tumuli in Nara Prefecture]] (Rear circular mound on the right, front rectangular mound in the left background) | location = {{ill|Asuka, Nara|ja|明日香村}}, Nara Prefecture | coordinates = {{Coord|34|28|7.38|N|135|48|2.80|E|region:JP-29_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳}} | Dimensions = Total length: 140m | Burial facility = Unknown | Construction era = Late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} | Interred individual = ({{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designation) {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇}} | Excavated items = Unknown | Mausoleum = Designated by the Imperial Household Agency as "Hinokuma Sakaai no misasagi" (檜隈坂合陵) | Map = {{location map|Japan Nara|lat_deg=34|lat_min=28|lat_sec=7.38|lon_deg=135|lon_min=48|lon_sec=2.8|label=Umeyama Kofun|mark=前方後円墳-西.svg|marksize=23|caption=location in Nara Prefecture|position=bottom|width=200|float=center|border=none}} }} [[File:Umeyama kohun aerial.jpg|thumb|220px|Bottom left: Kibi-hime Royal Tomb and Saruishi statues ({{National Land Aerial Photography}})]] [[File:Umeyama Kofun, haisho.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Mausoleum of {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇}}]] '''Umeyama Kofun''' (梅山古墳) is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳}} located in {{ill|Asuka, Nara|ja|明日香村}}, Nara Prefecture. The actual interred individual is unknown, but it has been designated by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} as the "Hinokuma Sakaai no misasagi" (檜隈坂合陵), attributed to {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇}}. == Overview == The tumulus features a keyhole shape, with the front mound oriented to the west along an east-west axis. Measuring approximately 140 meters in total length and 72 meters in diameter for the rear mound, it is the largest tumulus in Asuka Village. The mound is notable for its extensive use of {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}} (covering stones). Surrounding the tumulus is a water-filled moat, which was extensively remodeled during the {{ill|Bunkyū era|ja|文久}} restoration. Originally, the surrounding area was agricultural land. Some theories suggest that the restoration converted the tumulus from a double circular mound to its current keyhole shape.Theory proposed by {{ill|Kōichi Mori|ja|森浩一}} The tumulus is part of a group of burial mounds, including {{ill|Hirata Iwaya Kofun|ja|平田岩屋古墳}}, {{ill|Oni no Manaita and Oni no Senkan|ja|鬼の俎・鬼の雪隠}}, and {{ill|Noguchi Royal Tomb|ja|野口王墓}} (the joint tomb of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|天武天皇}} and {{ill|Empress Jitō|ja|持統天皇}}), all situated along the southern slope of the same hill. == Construction Period == While believed to have been built during the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, the exact construction date is uncertain due to the lack of archaeological excavations. == Interred Individual == Although designated by the Imperial Household Agency as the tomb of {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇}}, many scholars suggest that the nearby {{ill|Mise Maruyama Kofun|ja|見瀬丸山古墳}}, located approximately 800 meters northwest and the largest tumulus in Nara Prefecture, might be a more likely candidate. Other theories propose that {{ill|Soga no Iname|ja|蘇我稲目}} may be the interred individual. == Saruishi Statues == Adjacent to the {{ill|Tomb of Princess Kibi|ja|吉備姫王墓}}, stone statues known as {{ill|Saruishi|ja|猿石}} ("monkey stones") are placed nearby. These statues were excavated during the Edo period from a field just south of Umeyama Kofun and relocated to their current location in the early {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治時代}}. == References == Kofun | image = [[File:Umeyama Kofun, zenkei-2.jpg|280px]]
Tumulus and moat
(Rear circular mound on the right, front rectangular mound in the left background) | location = {{ill|Asuka, Nara|ja|明日香村}}, Nara Prefecture | coordinates = {{Coord|34|28|7.38|N|135|48|2.80|E|region:JP-29_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳}} | Dimensions = Total length: 140m | Burial facility = Unknown | Construction era = Late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} | Interred individual = ({{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designation) {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇}} | Excavated items = Unknown | Mausoleum = Designated by the Imperial Household Agency as "Hinokuma Sakaai no misasagi" (檜隈坂合陵) | Map = {{location map|Japan Nara|lat_deg=34|lat_min=28|lat_sec=7.38|lon_deg=135|lon_min=48|lon_sec=2.8|label=Umeyama Kofun|mark=前方後円墳-西.svg|marksize=23|caption=location in Nara Prefecture|position=bottom|width=200|float=center|border=none}} }} [[File:Umeyama kohun aerial.jpg|thumb|220px|Bottom left: Kibi-hime Royal Tomb and Saruishi statues ({{National Land Aerial Photography}})]] [[File:Umeyama Kofun, haisho.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Mausoleum of {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇}}]] '''Umeyama Kofun''' (梅山古墳) is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳}} located in {{ill|Asuka, Nara|ja|明日香村}}, Nara Prefecture. The actual interred individual is unknown, but it has been designated by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} as the "Hinokuma Sakaai no misasagi" (檜隈坂合陵), attributed to {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇}}. == Overview == The tumulus features a keyhole shape, with the front mound oriented to the west along an east-west axis. Measuring approximately 140 meters in total length and 72 meters in diameter for the rear mound, it is the largest tumulus in Asuka Village. The mound is notable for its extensive use of {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}} (covering stones). Surrounding the tumulus is a water-filled moat, which was extensively remodeled during the {{ill|Bunkyū era|ja|文久}} restoration. Originally, the surrounding area was agricultural land. Some theories suggest that the restoration converted the tumulus from a double circular mound to its current keyhole shape.Theory proposed by {{ill|Kōichi Mori|ja|森浩一}} The tumulus is part of a group of burial mounds, including {{ill|Hirata Iwaya Kofun|ja|平田岩屋古墳}}, {{ill|Oni no Manaita and Oni no Senkan|ja|鬼の俎・鬼の雪隠}}, and {{ill|Noguchi Royal Tomb|ja|野口王墓}} (the joint tomb of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|天武天皇}} and {{ill|Empress Jitō|ja|持統天皇}}), all situated along the southern slope of the same hill. == Construction Period == While believed to have been built during the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, the exact construction date is uncertain due to the lack of archaeological excavations. == Interred Individual == Although designated by the Imperial Household Agency as the tomb of {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇}}, many scholars suggest that the nearby {{ill|Mise Maruyama Kofun|ja|見瀬丸山古墳}}, located approximately 800 meters northwest and the largest tumulus in Nara Prefecture, might be a more likely candidate. Other theories propose that {{ill|Soga no Iname|ja|蘇我稲目}} may be the interred individual. == Saruishi Statues == Adjacent to the {{ill|Tomb of Princess Kibi|ja|吉備姫王墓}}, stone statues known as {{ill|Saruishi|ja|猿石}} ("monkey stones") are placed nearby. These statues were excavated during the Edo period from a field just south of Umeyama Kofun and relocated to their current location in the early {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治時代}}. == References == Kofun]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]]{{citation needed|Umeyama Kofun}}[[ja:梅山古墳]] {{Infobox ancient site | name = Umeyama Kofun | image = [[File:Umeyama Kofun, zenkei-2.jpg|280px]]
Tumulus and moat
(Rear circular mound on the right, front rectangular mound in the left background) | location = {{ill|Asuka, Nara|ja|明日香村}}, Nara Prefecture | coordinates = {{Coord|34|28|7.38|N|135|48|2.80|E|region:JP-29_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳}} | Dimensions = Total length: 140m | Burial facility = Unknown | Construction era = Late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} | Interred individual = ({{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designation) {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇}} | Excavated items = Unknown | Mausoleum = Designated by the Imperial Household Agency as "Hinokuma Sakaai no misasagi" (檜隈坂合陵) | Map = {{location map|Japan Nara|lat_deg=34|lat_min=28|lat_sec=7.38|lon_deg=135|lon_min=48|lon_sec=2.8|label=Umeyama Kofun|mark=前方後円墳-西.svg|marksize=23|caption=location in Nara Prefecture|position=bottom|width=200|float=center|border=none}} }} [[File:Umeyama kohun aerial.jpg|thumb|220px|Bottom left: Kibi-hime Royal Tomb and Saruishi statues ({{National Land Aerial Photography}})]] [[File:Umeyama Kofun, haisho.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Mausoleum of {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇}}]] '''Umeyama Kofun''' (梅山古墳) is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳}} located in {{ill|Asuka, Nara|ja|明日香村}}, Nara Prefecture. The actual interred individual is unknown, but it has been designated by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} as the "Hinokuma Sakaai no misasagi" (檜隈坂合陵), attributed to {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇}}. == Overview == The tumulus features a keyhole shape, with the front mound oriented to the west along an east-west axis. Measuring approximately 140 meters in total length and 72 meters in diameter for the rear mound, it is the largest tumulus in Asuka Village. The mound is notable for its extensive use of {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}} (covering stones). Surrounding the tumulus is a water-filled moat, which was extensively remodeled during the {{ill|Bunkyū era|ja|文久}} restoration. Originally, the surrounding area was agricultural land. Some theories suggest that the restoration converted the tumulus from a double circular mound to its current keyhole shape.Theory proposed by {{ill|Kōichi Mori|ja|森浩一}} The tumulus is part of a group of burial mounds, including {{ill|Hirata Iwaya Kofun|ja|平田岩屋古墳}}, {{ill|Oni no Manaita and Oni no Senkan|ja|鬼の俎・鬼の雪隠}}, and {{ill|Noguchi Royal Tomb|ja|野口王墓}} (the joint tomb of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|天武天皇}} and {{ill|Empress Jitō|ja|持統天皇}}), all situated along the southern slope of the same hill. == Construction Period == While believed to have been built during the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, the exact construction date is uncertain due to the lack of archaeological excavations. == Interred Individual == Although designated by the Imperial Household Agency as the tomb of {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇}}, many scholars suggest that the nearby {{ill|Mise Maruyama Kofun|ja|見瀬丸山古墳}}, located approximately 800 meters northwest and the largest tumulus in Nara Prefecture, might be a more likely candidate. Other theories propose that {{ill|Soga no Iname|ja|蘇我稲目}} may be the interred individual. == Saruishi Statues == Adjacent to the {{ill|Tomb of Princess Kibi|ja|吉備姫王墓}}, stone statues known as {{ill|Saruishi|ja|猿石}} ("monkey stones") are placed nearby. These statues were excavated during the Edo period from a field just south of Umeyama Kofun and relocated to their current location in the early {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治時代}}. == References == Kofun | image = [[File:Umeyama Kofun, zenkei-2.jpg|280px]]
Tumulus and moat
(Rear circular mound on the right, front rectangular mound in the left background) | location = {{ill|Asuka, Nara|ja|明日香村}}, Nara Prefecture | coordinates = {{Coord|34|28|7.38|N|135|48|2.80|E|region:JP-29_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳}} | Dimensions = Total length: 140m | Burial facility = Unknown | Construction era = Late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} | Interred individual = ({{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designation) {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇}} | Excavated items = Unknown | Mausoleum = Designated by the Imperial Household Agency as "Hinokuma Sakaai no misasagi" (檜隈坂合陵) | Map = {{location map|Japan Nara|lat_deg=34|lat_min=28|lat_sec=7.38|lon_deg=135|lon_min=48|lon_sec=2.8|label=Umeyama Kofun|mark=前方後円墳-西.svg|marksize=23|caption=location in Nara Prefecture|position=bottom|width=200|float=center|border=none}} }} [[File:Umeyama kohun aerial.jpg|thumb|220px|Bottom left: Kibi-hime Royal Tomb and Saruishi statues ({{National Land Aerial Photography}})]] [[File:Umeyama Kofun, haisho.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Mausoleum of {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇}}]] '''Umeyama Kofun''' (梅山古墳) is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳}} located in {{ill|Asuka, Nara|ja|明日香村}}, Nara Prefecture. The actual interred individual is unknown, but it has been designated by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} as the "Hinokuma Sakaai no misasagi" (檜隈坂合陵), attributed to {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇}}. == Overview == The tumulus features a keyhole shape, with the front mound oriented to the west along an east-west axis. Measuring approximately 140 meters in total length and 72 meters in diameter for the rear mound, it is the largest tumulus in Asuka Village. The mound is notable for its extensive use of {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}} (covering stones). Surrounding the tumulus is a water-filled moat, which was extensively remodeled during the {{ill|Bunkyū era|ja|文久}} restoration. Originally, the surrounding area was agricultural land. Some theories suggest that the restoration converted the tumulus from a double circular mound to its current keyhole shape.Theory proposed by {{ill|Kōichi Mori|ja|森浩一}} The tumulus is part of a group of burial mounds, including {{ill|Hirata Iwaya Kofun|ja|平田岩屋古墳}}, {{ill|Oni no Manaita and Oni no Senkan|ja|鬼の俎・鬼の雪隠}}, and {{ill|Noguchi Royal Tomb|ja|野口王墓}} (the joint tomb of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|天武天皇}} and {{ill|Empress Jitō|ja|持統天皇}}), all situated along the southern slope of the same hill. == Construction Period == While believed to have been built during the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, the exact construction date is uncertain due to the lack of archaeological excavations. == Interred Individual == Although designated by the Imperial Household Agency as the tomb of {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇}}, many scholars suggest that the nearby {{ill|Mise Maruyama Kofun|ja|見瀬丸山古墳}}, located approximately 800 meters northwest and the largest tumulus in Nara Prefecture, might be a more likely candidate. Other theories propose that {{ill|Soga no Iname|ja|蘇我稲目}} may be the interred individual. == Saruishi Statues == Adjacent to the {{ill|Tomb of Princess Kibi|ja|吉備姫王墓}}, stone statues known as {{ill|Saruishi|ja|猿石}} ("monkey stones") are placed nearby. These statues were excavated during the Edo period from a field just south of Umeyama Kofun and relocated to their current location in the early {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治時代}}. == References == Kofun]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]]{{citation needed|Umeyama Kofun}}[[ja:梅山古墳]] {{Infobox ancient site | name = Umeyama Kofun | image = [[File:Umeyama Kofun, zenkei-2.jpg|280px]]
Tumulus and moat
(Rear circular mound on the right, front rectangular mound in the left background) | location = {{ill|Asuka, Nara|ja|明日香村}}, Nara Prefecture | coordinates = {{Coord|34|28|7.38|N|135|48|2.80|E|region:JP-29_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳}} | Dimensions = Total length: 140m | Burial facility = Unknown | Construction era = Late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} | Interred individual = ({{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designation) {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇}} | Excavated items = Unknown | Mausoleum = Designated by the Imperial Household Agency as "Hinokuma Sakaai no misasagi" (檜隈坂合陵) | Map = {{location map|Japan Nara|lat_deg=34|lat_min=28|lat_sec=7.38|lon_deg=135|lon_min=48|lon_sec=2.8|label=Umeyama Kofun|mark=前方後円墳-西.svg|marksize=23|caption=location in Nara Prefecture|position=bottom|width=200|float=center|border=none}} }} [[File:Umeyama kohun aerial.jpg|thumb|220px|Bottom left: Kibi-hime Royal Tomb and Saruishi statues ({{National Land Aerial Photography}})]] [[File:Umeyama Kofun, haisho.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Mausoleum of {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇}]]] '''Umeyama Kofun''' (梅山古墳) is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳}} located in {{ill|Asuka, Nara|ja|明日香村}}, Nara Prefecture. The actual interred individual is unknown, but it has been designated by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} as the "Hinokuma Sakaai no misasagi" (檜隈坂合陵), attributed to {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇}}. == Overview == The tumulus features a keyhole shape, with the front mound oriented to the west along an east-west axis. Measuring approximately 140 meters in total length and 72 meters in diameter for the rear mound, it is the largest tumulus in Asuka Village. The mound is notable for its extensive use of {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}} (covering stones). Surrounding the tumulus is a water-filled moat, which was extensively remodeled during the {{ill|Bunkyū era|ja|文久}} restoration. Originally, the surrounding area was agricultural land. Some theories suggest that the restoration converted the tumulus from a double circular mound to its current keyhole shape.Theory proposed by {{ill|Kōichi Mori|ja|森浩一}} The tumulus is part of a group of burial mounds, including {{ill|Hirata Iwaya Kofun|ja|平田岩屋古墳}}, {{ill|Oni no Manaita and Oni no Senkan|ja|鬼の俎・鬼の雪隠}}, and {{ill|Noguchi Royal Tomb|ja|野口王墓}} (the joint tomb of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|天武天皇}} and {{ill|Empress Jitō|ja|持統天皇}}), all situated along the southern slope of the same hill. == Construction Period == While believed to have been built during the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, the exact construction date is uncertain due to the lack of archaeological excavations. == Interred Individual == Although designated by the Imperial Household Agency as the tomb of {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇}}, many scholars suggest that the nearby {{ill|Mise Maruyama Kofun|ja|見瀬丸山古墳}}, located approximately 800 meters northwest and the largest tumulus in Nara Prefecture, might be a more likely candidate. Other theories propose that {{ill|Soga no Iname|ja|蘇我稲目}} may be the interred individual. == Saruishi Statues == Adjacent to the {{ill|Tomb of Princess Kibi|ja|吉備姫王墓}}, stone statues known as {{ill|Saruishi|ja|猿石}} ("monkey stones") are placed nearby. These statues were excavated during the Edo period from a field just south of Umeyama Kofun and relocated to their current location in the early {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治時代}}. == References == Surrounding the tumulus is a water-filled moat, which was extensively remodeled during the {{ill|Bunkyū era|ja|文久}} restoration. Originally, the surrounding area was agricultural land. Other theories propose that {{ill|Soga no Iname|ja|蘇我稲目}} may be the interred individual. == Saruishi Statues == Adjacent to the {{ill|Tomb of Princess Kibi|ja|吉備姫王墓}}, stone statues known as {{ill|Saruishi|ja|猿石}} ("monkey stones") are placed nearby. These statues were excavated during the Edo period from a field just south of Umeyama Kofun and relocated to their current location in the early {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治時代}}. == References == Tumulus and moat
(Rear circular mound on the right, front rectangular mound in the left background) | location = {{ill|Asuka, Nara|ja|明日香村}}, Nara Prefecture | coordinates = {{Coord|34|28|7.38|N|135|48|2.80|E|region:JP-29_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳}} | Dimensions = Total length: 140m | Burial facility = Unknown | Construction era = Late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} | Interred individual = ({{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designation) {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇}} | Excavated items = Unknown | Mausoleum = Designated by the Imperial Household Agency as "Hinokuma Sakaai no misasagi" (檜隈坂合陵) | Map = {{location map|Japan Nara|lat_deg=34|lat_min=28|lat_sec=7.38|lon_deg=135|lon_min=48|lon_sec=2.8|label=Umeyama Kofun|mark=前方後円墳-西.svg|marksize=23|caption=location in Nara Prefecture|position=bottom|width=200|float=center|border=none}} }} [[File:Umeyama kohun aerial.jpg|thumb|220px|Bottom left: Kibi-hime Royal Tomb and Saruishi statues ({{National Land Aerial Photography}})]] [[File:Umeyama Kofun, haisho.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Mausoleum of {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇}]]] '''Umeyama Kofun''' (梅山古墳) is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳}} located in {{ill|Asuka, Nara|ja|明日香村}}, Nara Prefecture. The actual interred individual is unknown, but it has been designated by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} as the "Hinokuma Sakaai no misasagi" (檜隈坂合陵), attributed to {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇}}. == Overview == The tumulus features a keyhole shape, with the front mound oriented to the west along an east-west axis. Measuring approximately 140 meters in total length and 72 meters in diameter for the rear mound, it is the largest tumulus in Asuka Village. The mound is notable for its extensive use of {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}} (covering stones). Surrounding the tumulus is a water-filled moat, which was extensively remodeled during the {{ill|Bunkyū era|ja|文久}} restoration. Originally, the surrounding area was agricultural land. Some theories suggest that the restoration converted the tumulus from a double circular mound to its current keyhole shape.Theory proposed by {{ill|Kōichi Mori|ja|森浩一}} The tumulus is part of a group of burial mounds, including {{ill|Hirata Iwaya Kofun|ja|平田岩屋古墳}}, {{ill|Oni no Manaita and Oni no Senkan|ja|鬼の俎・鬼の雪隠}}, and {{ill|Noguchi Royal Tomb|ja|野口王墓}} (the joint tomb of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|天武天皇}} and {{ill|Empress Jitō|ja|持統天皇}}), all situated along the southern slope of the same hill. == Construction Period == While believed to have been built during the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, the exact construction date is uncertain due to the lack of archaeological excavations. == Interred Individual == Although designated by the Imperial Household Agency as the tomb of {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇}}, many scholars suggest that the nearby {{ill|Mise Maruyama Kofun|ja|見瀬丸山古墳}}, located approximately 800 meters northwest and the largest tumulus in Nara Prefecture, might be a more likely candidate. Other theories propose that {{ill|Soga no Iname|ja|蘇我稲目}} may be the interred individual. == Saruishi Statues == Adjacent to the {{ill|Tomb of Princess Kibi|ja|吉備姫王墓}}, stone statues known as {{ill|Saruishi|ja|猿石}} ("monkey stones") are placed nearby. These statues were excavated during the Edo period from a field just south of Umeyama Kofun and relocated to their current location in the early {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治時代}}. == References == (Rear circular mound on the right, front rectangular mound in the left background) | location = {{ill|Asuka, Nara|ja|明日香村}}, Nara Prefecture | coordinates = {{Coord|34|28|7.38|N|135|48|2.80|E|region:JP-29_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳}} | Dimensions = Total length: 140m | Burial facility = Unknown | Construction era = Late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} | Interred individual = ({{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designation) {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇}} | Excavated items = Unknown | Mausoleum = Designated by the Imperial Household Agency as "Hinokuma Sakaai no misasagi" (檜隈坂合陵) | Map = {{location map|Japan Nara|lat_deg=34|lat_min=28|lat_sec=7.38|lon_deg=135|lon_min=48|lon_sec=2.8|label=Umeyama Kofun|mark=前方後円墳-西.svg|marksize=23|caption=location in Nara Prefecture|position=bottom|width=200|float=center|border=none}} }} [[File:Umeyama kohun aerial.jpg|thumb|220px|Bottom left: Kibi-hime Royal Tomb and Saruishi statues ({{National Land Aerial Photography}})]] [[File:Umeyama Kofun, haisho.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Mausoleum of {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇}]]] '''Umeyama Kofun''' (梅山古墳) is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳}} located in {{ill|Asuka, Nara|ja|明日香村}}, Nara Prefecture. The actual interred individual is unknown, but it has been designated by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} as the "Hinokuma Sakaai no misasagi" (檜隈坂合陵), attributed to {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇}}. == Overview == The tumulus features a keyhole shape, with the front mound oriented to the west along an east-west axis. Measuring approximately 140 meters in total length and 72 meters in diameter for the rear mound, it is the largest tumulus in Asuka Village. The mound is notable for its extensive use of {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}} (covering stones). Surrounding the tumulus is a water-filled moat, which was extensively remodeled during the {{ill|Bunkyū era|ja|文久}} restoration. Originally, the surrounding area was agricultural land. Some theories suggest that the restoration converted the tumulus from a double circular mound to its current keyhole shape.Theory proposed by {{ill|Kōichi Mori|ja|森浩一}} The tumulus is part of a group of burial mounds, including {{ill|Hirata Iwaya Kofun|ja|平田岩屋古墳}}, {{ill|Oni no Manaita and Oni no Senkan|ja|鬼の俎・鬼の雪隠}}, and {{ill|Noguchi Royal Tomb|ja|野口王墓}} (the joint tomb of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|天武天皇}} and {{ill|Empress Jitō|ja|持統天皇}}), all situated along the southern slope of the same hill. == Construction Period == While believed to have been built during the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, the exact construction date is uncertain due to the lack of archaeological excavations. == Interred Individual == Although designated by the Imperial Household Agency as the tomb of {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇}}, many scholars suggest that the nearby {{ill|Mise Maruyama Kofun|ja|見瀬丸山古墳}}, located approximately 800 meters northwest and the largest tumulus in Nara Prefecture, might be a more likely candidate. Other theories propose that {{ill|Soga no Iname|ja|蘇我稲目}} may be the interred individual. == Saruishi Statues == Adjacent to the {{ill|Tomb of Princess Kibi|ja|吉備姫王墓}}, stone statues known as {{ill|Saruishi|ja|猿石}} ("monkey stones") are placed nearby. These statues were excavated during the Edo period from a field just south of Umeyama Kofun and relocated to their current location in the early {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治時代}}. == References == Tumulus and moat
(Rear circular mound on the right, front rectangular mound in the left background) | location = {{ill|Asuka, Nara|ja|明日香村}}, Nara Prefecture | coordinates = {{Coord|34|28|7.38|N|135|48|2.80|E|region:JP-29_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳}} | Dimensions = Total length: 140m | Burial facility = Unknown | Construction era = Late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} | Interred individual = ({{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designation) {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇}} | Excavated items = Unknown | Mausoleum = Designated by the Imperial Household Agency as "Hinokuma Sakaai no misasagi" (檜隈坂合陵) | Map = {{location map|Japan Nara|lat_deg=34|lat_min=28|lat_sec=7.38|lon_deg=135|lon_min=48|lon_sec=2.8|label=Umeyama Kofun|mark=前方後円墳-西.svg|marksize=23|caption=location in Nara Prefecture|position=bottom|width=200|float=center|border=none}} }} [[File:Umeyama kohun aerial.jpg|thumb|220px|Bottom left: Kibi-hime Royal Tomb and Saruishi statues ({{National Land Aerial Photography}})]] [[File:Umeyama Kofun, haisho.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Mausoleum of {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇}]]] '''Umeyama Kofun''' (梅山古墳) is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳}} located in {{ill|Asuka, Nara|ja|明日香村}}, Nara Prefecture. The actual interred individual is unknown, but it has been designated by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} as the "Hinokuma Sakaai no misasagi" (檜隈坂合陵), attributed to {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇}}. == Overview == The tumulus features a keyhole shape, with the front mound oriented to the west along an east-west axis. Measuring approximately 140 meters in total length and 72 meters in diameter for the rear mound, it is the largest tumulus in Asuka Village. The mound is notable for its extensive use of {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}} (covering stones). Surrounding the tumulus is a water-filled moat, which was extensively remodeled during the {{ill|Bunkyū era|ja|文久}} restoration. Originally, the surrounding area was agricultural land. Some theories suggest that the restoration converted the tumulus from a double circular mound to its current keyhole shape.Theory proposed by {{ill|Kōichi Mori|ja|森浩一}} The tumulus is part of a group of burial mounds, including {{ill|Hirata Iwaya Kofun|ja|平田岩屋古墳}}, {{ill|Oni no Manaita and Oni no Senkan|ja|鬼の俎・鬼の雪隠}}, and {{ill|Noguchi Royal Tomb|ja|野口王墓}} (the joint tomb of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|天武天皇}} and {{ill|Empress Jitō|ja|持統天皇}}), all situated along the southern slope of the same hill. == Construction Period == While believed to have been built during the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, the exact construction date is uncertain due to the lack of archaeological excavations. == Interred Individual == Although designated by the Imperial Household Agency as the tomb of {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇}}, many scholars suggest that the nearby {{ill|Mise Maruyama Kofun|ja|見瀬丸山古墳}}, located approximately 800 meters northwest and the largest tumulus in Nara Prefecture, might be a more likely candidate. Other theories propose that {{ill|Soga no Iname|ja|蘇我稲目}} may be the interred individual. == Saruishi Statues == Adjacent to the {{ill|Tomb of Princess Kibi|ja|吉備姫王墓}}, stone statues known as {{ill|Saruishi|ja|猿石}} ("monkey stones") are placed nearby. These statues were excavated during the Edo period from a field just south of Umeyama Kofun and relocated to their current location in the early {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治時代}}. == References == [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Umeyama Kofun | image = [[File:Umeyama Kofun, zenkei-2.jpg|280px]]
Tumulus and moat
(Rear circular mound on the right, front rectangular mound in the left background) | location = {{ill|Asuka, Nara|ja|明日香村}}, Nara Prefecture | coordinates = {{Coord|34|28|7.38|N|135|48|2.80|E|region:JP-29_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳}} | Dimensions = Total length: 140m | Burial facility = Unknown | Construction era = Late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} | Interred individual = ({{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designation) {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇}} | Excavated items = Unknown | Mausoleum = Designated by the Imperial Household Agency as "Hinokuma Sakaai no misasagi" (檜隈坂合陵) | Map = {{location map|Japan Nara|lat_deg=34|lat_min=28|lat_sec=7.38|lon_deg=135|lon_min=48|lon_sec=2.8|label=Umeyama Kofun|mark=前方後円墳-西.svg|marksize=23|caption=location in Nara Prefecture|position=bottom|width=200|float=center|border=none}} }} [[File:Umeyama kohun aerial.jpg|thumb|220px|Bottom left: Kibi-hime Royal Tomb and Saruishi statues ({{National Land Aerial Photography}})]] [[File:Umeyama Kofun, haisho.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Mausoleum of {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇}]]] '''Umeyama Kofun''' (梅山古墳) is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳}} located in {{ill|Asuka, Nara|ja|明日香村}}, Nara Prefecture. The actual interred individual is unknown, but it has been designated by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} as the "Hinokuma Sakaai no misasagi" (檜隈坂合陵), attributed to {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇}}. == Overview == The tumulus features a keyhole shape, with the front mound oriented to the west along an east-west axis. Measuring approximately 140 meters in total length and 72 meters in diameter for the rear mound, it is the largest tumulus in Asuka Village. The mound is notable for its extensive use of {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}} (covering stones). Surrounding the tumulus is a water-filled moat, which was extensively remodeled during the {{ill|Bunkyū era|ja|文久}} restoration. Originally, the surrounding area was agricultural land. Some theories suggest that the restoration converted the tumulus from a double circular mound to its current keyhole shape.Theory proposed by {{ill|Kōichi Mori|ja|森浩一}} The tumulus is part of a group of burial mounds, including {{ill|Hirata Iwaya Kofun|ja|平田岩屋古墳}}, {{ill|Oni no Manaita and Oni no Senkan|ja|鬼の俎・鬼の雪隠}}, and {{ill|Noguchi Royal Tomb|ja|野口王墓}} (the joint tomb of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|天武天皇}} and {{ill|Empress Jitō|ja|持統天皇}}), all situated along the southern slope of the same hill. == Construction Period == While believed to have been built during the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, the exact construction date is uncertain due to the lack of archaeological excavations. == Interred Individual == Although designated by the Imperial Household Agency as the tomb of {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇}}, many scholars suggest that the nearby {{ill|Mise Maruyama Kofun|ja|見瀬丸山古墳}}, located approximately 800 meters northwest and the largest tumulus in Nara Prefecture, might be a more likely candidate. Other theories propose that {{ill|Soga no Iname|ja|蘇我稲目}} may be the interred individual. == Saruishi Statues == Adjacent to the {{ill|Tomb of Princess Kibi|ja|吉備姫王墓}}, stone statues known as {{ill|Saruishi|ja|猿石}} ("monkey stones") are placed nearby. These statues were excavated during the Edo period from a field just south of Umeyama Kofun and relocated to their current location in the early {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治時代}}. == References == The actual interred individual is unknown, but it has been designated by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} as the "Hinokuma Sakaai no misasagi" (檜隈坂合陵), attributed to {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇}}. == Overview == The tumulus features a keyhole shape, with the front mound oriented to the west along an east-west axis. Measuring approximately 140 meters in total length and 72 meters in diameter for the rear mound, it is the largest tumulus in Asuka Village. The mound is notable for its extensive use of {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}} (covering stones). Surrounding the tumulus is a water-filled moat, which was extensively remodeled during the {{ill|Bunkyū era|ja|文久}} restoration. Originally, the surrounding area was agricultural land. Some theories suggest that the restoration converted the tumulus from a double circular mound to its current keyhole shape.Theory proposed by {{ill|Kōichi Mori|ja|森浩一}} The tumulus is part of a group of burial mounds, including {{ill|Hirata Iwaya Kofun|ja|平田岩屋古墳}}, {{ill|Oni no Manaita and Oni no Senkan|ja|鬼の俎・鬼の雪隠}}, and {{ill|Noguchi Royal Tomb|ja|野口王墓}} (the joint tomb of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|天武天皇}} and {{ill|Empress Jitō|ja|持統天皇}}), all situated along the southern slope of the same hill. == Construction Period == While believed to have been built during the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, the exact construction date is uncertain due to the lack of archaeological excavations. == Interred Individual == Although designated by the Imperial Household Agency as the tomb of {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇}}, many scholars suggest that the nearby {{ill|Mise Maruyama Kofun|ja|見瀬丸山古墳}}, located approximately 800 meters northwest and the largest tumulus in Nara Prefecture, might be a more likely candidate. Other theories propose that {{ill|Soga no Iname|ja|蘇我稲目}} may be the interred individual. == Saruishi Statues == Adjacent to the {{ill|Tomb of Princess Kibi|ja|吉備姫王墓}}, stone statues known as {{ill|Saruishi|ja|猿石}} ("monkey stones") are placed nearby. These statues were excavated during the Edo period from a field just south of Umeyama Kofun and relocated to their current location in the early {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治時代}}. == References == Kofun | image = [[File:Umeyama Kofun, zenkei-2.jpg|280px]]
Tumulus and moat
(Rear circular mound on the right, front rectangular mound in the left background) | location = {{ill|Asuka, Nara|ja|明日香村}}, Nara Prefecture | coordinates = {{Coord|34|28|7.38|N|135|48|2.80|E|region:JP-29_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳}} | Dimensions = Total length: 140m | Burial facility = Unknown | Construction era = Late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} | Interred individual = ({{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designation) {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇}} | Excavated items = Unknown | Mausoleum = Designated by the Imperial Household Agency as "Hinokuma Sakaai no misasagi" (檜隈坂合陵) | Map = {{location map|Japan Nara|lat_deg=34|lat_min=28|lat_sec=7.38|lon_deg=135|lon_min=48|lon_sec=2.8|label=Umeyama Kofun|mark=前方後円墳-西.svg|marksize=23|caption=location in Nara Prefecture|position=bottom|width=200|float=center|border=none}} }} [[File:Umeyama kohun aerial.jpg|thumb|220px|Bottom left: Kibi-hime Royal Tomb and Saruishi statues ({{National Land Aerial Photography}})]] [[File:Umeyama Kofun, haisho.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Mausoleum of {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇}]]] '''Umeyama Kofun''' (梅山古墳) is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳}} located in {{ill|Asuka, Nara|ja|明日香村}}, Nara Prefecture. The actual interred individual is unknown, but it has been designated by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} as the "Hinokuma Sakaai no misasagi" (檜隈坂合陵), attributed to {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇}}. == Overview == The tumulus features a keyhole shape, with the front mound oriented to the west along an east-west axis. Measuring approximately 140 meters in total length and 72 meters in diameter for the rear mound, it is the largest tumulus in Asuka Village. The mound is notable for its extensive use of {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}} (covering stones). Surrounding the tumulus is a water-filled moat, which was extensively remodeled during the {{ill|Bunkyū era|ja|文久}} restoration. Originally, the surrounding area was agricultural land. Some theories suggest that the restoration converted the tumulus from a double circular mound to its current keyhole shape.Theory proposed by {{ill|Kōichi Mori|ja|森浩一}} The tumulus is part of a group of burial mounds, including {{ill|Hirata Iwaya Kofun|ja|平田岩屋古墳}}, {{ill|Oni no Manaita and Oni no Senkan|ja|鬼の俎・鬼の雪隠}}, and {{ill|Noguchi Royal Tomb|ja|野口王墓}} (the joint tomb of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|天武天皇}} and {{ill|Empress Jitō|ja|持統天皇}}), all situated along the southern slope of the same hill. == Construction Period == While believed to have been built during the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, the exact construction date is uncertain due to the lack of archaeological excavations. == Interred Individual == Although designated by the Imperial Household Agency as the tomb of {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇}}, many scholars suggest that the nearby {{ill|Mise Maruyama Kofun|ja|見瀬丸山古墳}}, located approximately 800 meters northwest and the largest tumulus in Nara Prefecture, might be a more likely candidate. Other theories propose that {{ill|Soga no Iname|ja|蘇我稲目}} may be the interred individual. == Saruishi Statues == Adjacent to the {{ill|Tomb of Princess Kibi|ja|吉備姫王墓}}, stone statues known as {{ill|Saruishi|ja|猿石}} ("monkey stones") are placed nearby. These statues were excavated during the Edo period from a field just south of Umeyama Kofun and relocated to their current location in the early {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治時代}}. == References == The actual interred individual is unknown, but it has been designated by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} as the "Hinokuma Sakaai no misasagi" (檜隈坂合陵), attributed to {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇}}. == Overview == The tumulus features a keyhole shape, with the front mound oriented to the west along an east-west axis. Surrounding the tumulus is a water-filled moat, which was extensively remodeled during the {{ill|Bunkyū era|ja|文久}} restoration. Originally, the surrounding area was agricultural land. Some theories suggest that the restoration converted the tumulus from a double circular mound to its current keyhole shape.Theory proposed by {{ill|Kōichi Mori|ja|森浩一}} The tumulus is part of a group of burial mounds, including {{ill|Hirata Iwaya Kofun|ja|平田岩屋古墳}}, {{ill|Oni no Manaita and Oni no Senkan|ja|鬼の俎・鬼の雪隠}}, and {{ill|Noguchi Royal Tomb|ja|野口王墓}} (the joint tomb of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|天武天皇}} and {{ill|Empress Jitō|ja|持統天皇}}), all situated along the southern slope of the same hill. == Construction Period == While believed to have been built during the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, the exact construction date is uncertain due to the lack of archaeological excavations. == Interred Individual == Although designated by the Imperial Household Agency as the tomb of {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇}}, many scholars suggest that the nearby {{ill|Mise Maruyama Kofun|ja|見瀬丸山古墳}}, located approximately 800 meters northwest and the largest tumulus in Nara Prefecture, might be a more likely candidate. Other theories propose that {{ill|Soga no Iname|ja|蘇我稲目}} may be the interred individual. == Saruishi Statues == Adjacent to the {{ill|Tomb of Princess Kibi|ja|吉備姫王墓}}, stone statues known as {{ill|Saruishi|ja|猿石}} ("monkey stones") are placed nearby. These statues were excavated during the Edo period from a field just south of Umeyama Kofun and relocated to their current location in the early {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治時代}}. == References == Kofun]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]]{{citation needed|Umeyama Kofun}}[[ja:梅山古墳]] {{Infobox ancient site | name = Umeyama Kofun | image = [[File:Umeyama Kofun, zenkei-2.jpg|280px]]
Tumulus and moat
(Rear circular mound on the right, front rectangular mound in the left background) | location = {{ill|Asuka, Nara|ja|明日香村}}, Nara Prefecture | coordinates = {{Coord|34|28|7.38|N|135|48|2.80|E|region:JP-29_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳}} | Dimensions = Total length: 140m | Burial facility = Unknown | Construction era = Late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} | Interred individual = ({{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designation) {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇}} | Excavated items = Unknown | Mausoleum = Designated by the Imperial Household Agency as "Hinokuma Sakaai no misasagi" (檜隈坂合陵) | Map = {{location map|Japan Nara|lat_deg=34|lat_min=28|lat_sec=7.38|lon_deg=135|lon_min=48|lon_sec=2.8|label=Umeyama Kofun|mark=前方後円墳-西.svg|marksize=23|caption=location in Nara Prefecture|position=bottom|width=200|float=center|border=none}} }} [[File:Umeyama kohun aerial.jpg|thumb|220px|Bottom left: Kibi-hime Royal Tomb and Saruishi statues ({{National Land Aerial Photography}})]] [[File:Umeyama Kofun, haisho.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Mausoleum of {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇}]]] '''Umeyama Kofun''' (梅山古墳) is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳}} located in {{ill|Asuka, Nara|ja|明日香村}}, Nara Prefecture. The actual interred individual is unknown, but it has been designated by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} as the "Hinokuma Sakaai no misasagi" (檜隈坂合陵), attributed to {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇}}. == Overview == The tumulus features a keyhole shape, with the front mound oriented to the west along an east-west axis. Measuring approximately 140 meters in total length and 72 meters in diameter for the rear mound, it is the largest tumulus in Asuka Village. The mound is notable for its extensive use of {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}} (covering stones). Surrounding the tumulus is a water-filled moat, which was extensively remodeled during the {{ill|Bunkyū era|ja|文久}} restoration. Originally, the surrounding area was agricultural land. Some theories suggest that the restoration converted the tumulus from a double circular mound to its current keyhole shape.Theory proposed by {{ill|Kōichi Mori|ja|森浩一}} The tumulus is part of a group of burial mounds, including {{ill|Hirata Iwaya Kofun|ja|平田岩屋古墳}}, {{ill|Oni no Manaita and Oni no Senkan|ja|鬼の俎・鬼の雪隠}}, and {{ill|Noguchi Royal Tomb|ja|野口王墓}} (the joint tomb of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|天武天皇}} and {{ill|Empress Jitō|ja|持統天皇}}), all situated along the southern slope of the same hill. == Construction Period == While believed to have been built during the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, the exact construction date is uncertain due to the lack of archaeological excavations. == Interred Individual == Although designated by the Imperial Household Agency as the tomb of {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇}}, many scholars suggest that the nearby {{ill|Mise Maruyama Kofun|ja|見瀬丸山古墳}}, located approximately 800 meters northwest and the largest tumulus in Nara Prefecture, might be a more likely candidate. Other theories propose that {{ill|Soga no Iname|ja|蘇我稲目}} may be the interred individual. == Saruishi Statues == Adjacent to the {{ill|Tomb of Princess Kibi|ja|吉備姫王墓}}, stone statues known as {{ill|Saruishi|ja|猿石}} ("monkey stones") are placed nearby. These statues were excavated during the Edo period from a field just south of Umeyama Kofun and relocated to their current location in the early {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治時代}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Kanazuka Kofun|ja|カナヅカ古墳}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Umeyama Kofun}} * [https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/guide/029/index.html Hinokuma Sakaai no misasagi] - Imperial Household Agency [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Ancient tumuli in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Umeyama Kofun]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]]{{citation needed|Umeyama Kofun}}[[ja:梅山古墳]] {{Infobox ancient site | name = Umeyama Kofun | image = [[File:Umeyama Kofun, zenkei-2.jpg|280px]]
Tumulus and moat
(Rear circular mound on the right, front rectangular mound in the left background) | location = {{ill|Asuka, Nara|ja|明日香村}}, Nara Prefecture | coordinates = {{Coord|34|28|7.38|N|135|48|2.80|E|region:JP-29_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳}} | Dimensions = Total length: 140m | Burial facility = Unknown | Construction era = Late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} | Interred individual = ({{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designation) {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇}} | Excavated items = Unknown | Mausoleum = Designated by the Imperial Household Agency as "Hinokuma Sakaai no misasagi" (檜隈坂合陵) | Map = {{location map|Japan Nara|lat_deg=34|lat_min=28|lat_sec=7.38|lon_deg=135|lon_min=48|lon_sec=2.8|label=Umeyama Kofun|mark=前方後円墳-西.svg|marksize=23|caption=location in Nara Prefecture|position=bottom|width=200|float=center|border=none}} }} [[File:Umeyama kohun aerial.jpg|thumb|220px|Bottom left: Kibi-hime Royal Tomb and Saruishi statues ({{National Land Aerial Photography}})]] [[File:Umeyama Kofun, haisho.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Mausoleum of {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇}]]] '''Umeyama Kofun''' (梅山古墳) is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳}} located in {{ill|Asuka, Nara|ja|明日香村}}, Nara Prefecture. The actual interred individual is unknown, but it has been designated by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} as the "Hinokuma Sakaai no misasagi" (檜隈坂合陵), attributed to {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇}}. == Overview == The tumulus features a keyhole shape, with the front mound oriented to the west along an east-west axis. Measuring approximately 140 meters in total length and 72 meters in diameter for the rear mound, it is the largest tumulus in Asuka Village. The mound is notable for its extensive use of {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}} (covering stones). Surrounding the tumulus is a water-filled moat, which was extensively remodeled during the {{ill|Bunkyū era|ja|文久}} restoration. Originally, the surrounding area was agricultural land. Some theories suggest that the restoration converted the tumulus from a double circular mound to its current keyhole shape.Theory proposed by {{ill|Kōichi Mori|ja|森浩一}} The tumulus is part of a group of burial mounds, including {{ill|Hirata Iwaya Kofun|ja|平田岩屋古墳}}, {{ill|Oni no Manaita and Oni no Senkan|ja|鬼の俎・鬼の雪隠}}, and {{ill|Noguchi Royal Tomb|ja|野口王墓}} (the joint tomb of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|天武天皇}} and {{ill|Empress Jitō|ja|持統天皇}}), all situated along the southern slope of the same hill. == Construction Period == While believed to have been built during the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, the exact construction date is uncertain due to the lack of archaeological excavations. == Interred Individual == Although designated by the Imperial Household Agency as the tomb of {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇}}, many scholars suggest that the nearby {{ill|Mise Maruyama Kofun|ja|見瀬丸山古墳}}, located approximately 800 meters northwest and the largest tumulus in Nara Prefecture, might be a more likely candidate. Other theories propose that {{ill|Soga no Iname|ja|蘇我稲目}} may be the interred individual. == Saruishi Statues == Adjacent to the {{ill|Tomb of Princess Kibi|ja|吉備姫王墓}}, stone statues known as {{ill|Saruishi|ja|猿石}} ("monkey stones") are placed nearby. These statues were excavated during the Edo period from a field just south of Umeyama Kofun and relocated to their current location in the early {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治時代}}. == References == misasagi] - Imperial Household Agency [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Ancient tumuli in Nara Prefecture]] (Rear circular mound on the right, front rectangular mound in the left background) | location = {{ill|Asuka, Nara|ja|明日香村}}, Nara Prefecture | coordinates = {{Coord|34|28|7.38|N|135|48|2.80|E|region:JP-29_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳}} | Dimensions = Total length: 140m | Burial facility = Unknown | Construction era = Late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} | Interred individual = ({{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designation) {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇}} | Excavated items = Unknown | Mausoleum = Designated by the Imperial Household Agency as "Hinokuma Sakaai no misasagi" (檜隈坂合陵) | Map = {{location map|Japan Nara|lat_deg=34|lat_min=28|lat_sec=7.38|lon_deg=135|lon_min=48|lon_sec=2.8|label=Umeyama Kofun|mark=前方後円墳-西.svg|marksize=23|caption=location in Nara Prefecture|position=bottom|width=200|float=center|border=none}} }} [[File:Umeyama kohun aerial.jpg|thumb|220px|Bottom left: Kibi-hime Royal Tomb and Saruishi statues ({{National Land Aerial Photography}})]] [[File:Umeyama Kofun, haisho.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Mausoleum of {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇}]]] '''Umeyama Kofun''' (梅山古墳) is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳}} located in {{ill|Asuka, Nara|ja|明日香村}}, Nara Prefecture. The actual interred individual is unknown, but it has been designated by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} as the "Hinokuma Sakaai no misasagi" (檜隈坂合陵), attributed to {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇}}. == Overview == The tumulus features a keyhole shape, with the front mound oriented to the west along an east-west axis. Measuring approximately 140 meters in total length and 72 meters in diameter for the rear mound, it is the largest tumulus in Asuka Village. The mound is notable for its extensive use of {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}} (covering stones). Surrounding the tumulus is a water-filled moat, which was extensively remodeled during the {{ill|Bunkyū era|ja|文久}} restoration. Originally, the surrounding area was agricultural land. Some theories suggest that the restoration converted the tumulus from a double circular mound to its current keyhole shape.Theory proposed by {{ill|Kōichi Mori|ja|森浩一}} The tumulus is part of a group of burial mounds, including {{ill|Hirata Iwaya Kofun|ja|平田岩屋古墳}}, {{ill|Oni no Manaita and Oni no Senkan|ja|鬼の俎・鬼の雪隠}}, and {{ill|Noguchi Royal Tomb|ja|野口王墓}} (the joint tomb of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|天武天皇}} and {{ill|Empress Jitō|ja|持統天皇}}), all situated along the southern slope of the same hill. == Construction Period == While believed to have been built during the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, the exact construction date is uncertain due to the lack of archaeological excavations. == Interred Individual == Although designated by the Imperial Household Agency as the tomb of {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇}}, many scholars suggest that the nearby {{ill|Mise Maruyama Kofun|ja|見瀬丸山古墳}}, located approximately 800 meters northwest and the largest tumulus in Nara Prefecture, might be a more likely candidate. Other theories propose that {{ill|Soga no Iname|ja|蘇我稲目}} may be the interred individual. == Saruishi Statues == Adjacent to the {{ill|Tomb of Princess Kibi|ja|吉備姫王墓}}, stone statues known as {{ill|Saruishi|ja|猿石}} ("monkey stones") are placed nearby. These statues were excavated during the Edo period from a field just south of Umeyama Kofun and relocated to their current location in the early {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治時代}}. == References == [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Umeyama Kofun]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]]{{citation needed|Umeyama Kofun}}[[ja:梅山古墳]] {{Infobox ancient site | name = Umeyama Kofun | image = [[File:Umeyama Kofun, zenkei-2.jpg|280px]]
Tumulus and moat
(Rear circular mound on the right, front rectangular mound in the left background) | location = {{ill|Asuka, Nara|ja|明日香村}}, Nara Prefecture | coordinates = {{Coord|34|28|7.38|N|135|48|2.80|E|region:JP-29_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳}} | Dimensions = Total length: 140m | Burial facility = Unknown | Construction era = Late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} | Interred individual = ({{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designation) {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇}} | Excavated items = Unknown | Mausoleum = Designated by the Imperial Household Agency as "Hinokuma Sakaai no misasagi" (檜隈坂合陵) | Map = {{location map|Japan Nara|lat_deg=34|lat_min=28|lat_sec=7.38|lon_deg=135|lon_min=48|lon_sec=2.8|label=Umeyama Kofun|mark=前方後円墳-西.svg|marksize=23|caption=location in Nara Prefecture|position=bottom|width=200|float=center|border=none}} }} [[File:Umeyama kohun aerial.jpg|thumb|220px|Bottom left: Kibi-hime Royal Tomb and Saruishi statues ({{National Land Aerial Photography}})]] [[File:Umeyama Kofun, haisho.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Mausoleum of {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇}]]] '''Umeyama Kofun''' (梅山古墳) is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳}} located in {{ill|Asuka, Nara|ja|明日香村}}, Nara Prefecture. The actual interred individual is unknown, but it has been designated by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} as the "Hinokuma Sakaai no misasagi" (檜隈坂合陵), attributed to {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇}}. == Overview == The tumulus features a keyhole shape, with the front mound oriented to the west along an east-west axis. Measuring approximately 140 meters in total length and 72 meters in diameter for the rear mound, it is the largest tumulus in Asuka Village. The mound is notable for its extensive use of {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}} (covering stones). Surrounding the tumulus is a water-filled moat, which was extensively remodeled during the {{ill|Bunkyū era|ja|文久}} restoration. Originally, the surrounding area was agricultural land. Some theories suggest that the restoration converted the tumulus from a double circular mound to its current keyhole shape.Theory proposed by {{ill|Kōichi Mori|ja|森浩一}} The tumulus is part of a group of burial mounds, including {{ill|Hirata Iwaya Kofun|ja|平田岩屋古墳}}, {{ill|Oni no Manaita and Oni no Senkan|ja|鬼の俎・鬼の雪隠}}, and {{ill|Noguchi Royal Tomb|ja|野口王墓}} (the joint tomb of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|天武天皇}} and {{ill|Empress Jitō|ja|持統天皇}}), all situated along the southern slope of the same hill. == Construction Period == While believed to have been built during the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, the exact construction date is uncertain due to the lack of archaeological excavations. == Interred Individual == Although designated by the Imperial Household Agency as the tomb of {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇}}, many scholars suggest that the nearby {{ill|Mise Maruyama Kofun|ja|見瀬丸山古墳}}, located approximately 800 meters northwest and the largest tumulus in Nara Prefecture, might be a more likely candidate. Other theories propose that {{ill|Soga no Iname|ja|蘇我稲目}} may be the interred individual. == Saruishi Statues == Adjacent to the {{ill|Tomb of Princess Kibi|ja|吉備姫王墓}}, stone statues known as {{ill|Saruishi|ja|猿石}} ("monkey stones") are placed nearby. These statues were excavated during the Edo period from a field just south of Umeyama Kofun and relocated to their current location in the early {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治時代}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Kanazuka Kofun|ja|カナヅカ古墳}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Umeyama Kofun}} * [https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/guide/029/index.html Hinokuma Sakaai no misasagi] - Imperial Household Agency [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Ancient tumuli in Nara Prefecture]] (Rear circular mound on the right, front rectangular mound in the left background) | location = {{ill|Asuka, Nara|ja|明日香村}}, Nara Prefecture | coordinates = {{Coord|34|28|7.38|N|135|48|2.80|E|region:JP-29_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳}} | Dimensions = Total length: 140m | Burial facility = Unknown | Construction era = Late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} | Interred individual = ({{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designation) {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇}} | Excavated items = Unknown | Mausoleum = Designated by the Imperial Household Agency as "Hinokuma Sakaai no misasagi" (檜隈坂合陵) | Map = {{location map|Japan Nara|lat_deg=34|lat_min=28|lat_sec=7.38|lon_deg=135|lon_min=48|lon_sec=2.8|label=Umeyama Kofun|mark=前方後円墳-西.svg|marksize=23|caption=location in Nara Prefecture|position=bottom|width=200|float=center|border=none}} }} [[File:Umeyama kohun aerial.jpg|thumb|220px|Bottom left: Kibi-hime Royal Tomb and Saruishi statues ({{National Land Aerial Photography}})]] [[File:Umeyama Kofun, haisho.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Mausoleum of {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇}]]] '''Umeyama Kofun''' (梅山古墳) is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳}} located in {{ill|Asuka, Nara|ja|明日香村}}, Nara Prefecture. The actual interred individual is unknown, but it has been designated by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} as the "Hinokuma Sakaai no misasagi" (檜隈坂合陵), attributed to {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇}}. == Overview == The tumulus features a keyhole shape, with the front mound oriented to the west along an east-west axis. Measuring approximately 140 meters in total length and 72 meters in diameter for the rear mound, it is the largest tumulus in Asuka Village. The mound is notable for its extensive use of {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}} (covering stones). Surrounding the tumulus is a water-filled moat, which was extensively remodeled during the {{ill|Bunkyū era|ja|文久}} restoration. Originally, the surrounding area was agricultural land. Some theories suggest that the restoration converted the tumulus from a double circular mound to its current keyhole shape.Theory proposed by {{ill|Kōichi Mori|ja|森浩一}} The tumulus is part of a group of burial mounds, including {{ill|Hirata Iwaya Kofun|ja|平田岩屋古墳}}, {{ill|Oni no Manaita and Oni no Senkan|ja|鬼の俎・鬼の雪隠}}, and {{ill|Noguchi Royal Tomb|ja|野口王墓}} (the joint tomb of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|天武天皇}} and {{ill|Empress Jitō|ja|持統天皇}}), all situated along the southern slope of the same hill. == Construction Period == While believed to have been built during the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, the exact construction date is uncertain due to the lack of archaeological excavations. == Interred Individual == Although designated by the Imperial Household Agency as the tomb of {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇}}, many scholars suggest that the nearby {{ill|Mise Maruyama Kofun|ja|見瀬丸山古墳}}, located approximately 800 meters northwest and the largest tumulus in Nara Prefecture, might be a more likely candidate. These statues were excavated during the Edo period from a field just south of Umeyama Kofun and relocated to their current location in the early {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治時代}}. == References == [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Umeyama Kofun]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]]{{citation needed|Umeyama Kofun}}[[ja:梅山古墳]] {{Infobox ancient site | name = Umeyama Kofun | image = [[File:Umeyama Kofun, zenkei-2.jpg|280px]]
Tumulus and moat
(Rear circular mound on the right, front rectangular mound in the left background) | location = {{ill|Asuka, Nara|ja|明日香村}}, Nara Prefecture | coordinates = {{Coord|34|28|7.38|N|135|48|2.80|E|region:JP-29_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} | Dimensions = Total length: 140m | Burial facility = Unknown | Construction era = Late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} | Interred individual = ({{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designation) {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇}} | Excavated items = Unknown | Mausoleum = Designated by the Imperial Household Agency as "Hinokuma Sakaai no misasagi" (檜隈坂合陵) | Map = {{location map|Japan Nara|lat_deg=34|lat_min=28|lat_sec=7.38|lon_deg=135|lon_min=48|lon_sec=2.8|label=Umeyama Kofun|mark=前方後円墳-西.svg|marksize=23|caption=location in Nara Prefecture|position=bottom|width=200|float=center|border=none}} }} [[File:Umeyama kohun aerial.jpg|thumb|220px|Bottom left: Kibi-hime Royal Tomb and Saruishi statues ({{National Land Aerial Photography}})]] [[File:Umeyama Kofun, haisho.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Mausoleum of {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇}]]] '''Umeyama Kofun''' (梅山古墳) is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} located in {{ill|Asuka, Nara|ja|明日香村}}, Nara Prefecture. The actual interred individual is unknown, but it has been designated by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} as the "Hinokuma Sakaai no misasagi" (檜隈坂合陵), attributed to {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇}}. == Overview == The tumulus features a keyhole shape, with the front mound oriented to the west along an east-west axis. Measuring approximately 140 meters in total length and 72 meters in diameter for the rear mound, it is the largest tumulus in Asuka Village. The mound is notable for its extensive use of {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}} (covering stones). Surrounding the tumulus is a water-filled moat, which was extensively remodeled during the {{ill|Bunkyū era|ja|文久}} restoration. Originally, the surrounding area was agricultural land. Some theories suggest that the restoration converted the tumulus from a double circular mound to its current keyhole shape.Theory proposed by {{ill|Kōichi Mori|ja|森浩一}} The tumulus is part of a group of burial mounds, including {{ill|Hirata Iwaya Kofun|ja|平田岩屋古墳}}, {{ill|Oni no Manaita and Oni no Senkan|ja|鬼の俎・鬼の雪隠}}, and {{ill|Noguchi Royal Tomb|ja|野口王墓}} (the joint tomb of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|天武天皇}} and {{ill|Empress Jitō|ja|持統天皇}}), all situated along the southern slope of the same hill. == Construction Period == While believed to have been built during the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, the exact construction date is uncertain due to the lack of archaeological excavations. == Interred Individual == Although designated by the Imperial Household Agency as the tomb of {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇}}, many scholars suggest that the nearby {{ill|Mise Maruyama Kofun|ja|見瀬丸山古墳}}, located approximately 800 meters northwest and the largest tumulus in Nara Prefecture, might be a more likely candidate. Other theories propose that {{ill|Soga no Iname|ja|蘇我稲目}} may be the interred individual. == Saruishi Statues == Adjacent to the {{ill|Tomb of Princess Kibi|ja|吉備姫王墓}}, stone statues known as {{ill|Saruishi|ja|猿石}} ("monkey stones") are placed nearby. These statues were excavated during the Edo period from a field just south of Umeyama Kofun and relocated to their current location in the early {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治時代}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Kanazuka Kofun|ja|カナヅカ古墳}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Umeyama Kofun}} * [https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/guide/029/index.html Hinokuma Sakaai no misasagi] - Imperial Household Agency [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Ancient tumuli in Nara Prefecture]] (Rear circular mound on the right, front rectangular mound in the left background) | location = {{ill|Asuka, Nara|ja|明日香村}}, Nara Prefecture | coordinates = {{Coord|34|28|7.38|N|135|48|2.80|E|region:JP-29_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} | Dimensions = Total length: 140m | Burial facility = Unknown | Construction era = Late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} | Interred individual = ({{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designation) {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇}} | Excavated items = Unknown | Mausoleum = Designated by the Imperial Household Agency as "Hinokuma Sakaai no misasagi" (檜隈坂合陵) | Map = {{location map|Japan Nara|lat_deg=34|lat_min=28|lat_sec=7.38|lon_deg=135|lon_min=48|lon_sec=2.8|label=Umeyama Kofun|mark=前方後円墳-西.svg|marksize=23|caption=location in Nara Prefecture|position=bottom|width=200|float=center|border=none}} }} [[File:Umeyama kohun aerial.jpg|thumb|220px|Bottom left: Kibi-hime Royal Tomb and Saruishi statues ({{National Land Aerial Photography}})]] [[File:Umeyama Kofun, haisho.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Mausoleum of {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇}]]] '''Umeyama Kofun''' (梅山古墳) is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} located in {{ill|Asuka, Nara|ja|明日香村}}, Nara Prefecture. The actual interred individual is unknown, but it has been designated by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} as the "Hinokuma Sakaai no misasagi" (檜隈坂合陵), attributed to {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇}}. == Overview == The tumulus features a keyhole shape, with the front mound oriented to the west along an east-west axis. Measuring approximately 140 meters in total length and 72 meters in diameter for the rear mound, it is the largest tumulus in Asuka Village. The mound is notable for its extensive use of {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}} (covering stones). Surrounding the tumulus is a water-filled moat, which was extensively remodeled during the {{ill|Bunkyū era|ja|文久}} restoration. Originally, the surrounding area was agricultural land. Some theories suggest that the restoration converted the tumulus from a double circular mound to its current keyhole shape.Theory proposed by {{ill|Kōichi Mori|ja|森浩一}} The tumulus is part of a group of burial mounds, including {{ill|Hirata Iwaya Kofun|ja|平田岩屋古墳}}, {{ill|Oni no Manaita and Oni no Senkan|ja|鬼の俎・鬼の雪隠}}, and {{ill|Noguchi Royal Tomb|ja|野口王墓}} (the joint tomb of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|天武天皇}} and {{ill|Empress Jitō|ja|持統天皇}}), all situated along the southern slope of the same hill. == Construction Period == While believed to have been built during the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, the exact construction date is uncertain due to the lack of archaeological excavations. == Interred Individual == Although designated by the Imperial Household Agency as the tomb of {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇}}, many scholars suggest that the nearby {{ill|Mise Maruyama Kofun|ja|見瀬丸山古墳|1=Maruyama Kofun (Kashihara)}}, located approximately 800 meters northwest and the largest tumulus in Nara Prefecture, might be a more likely candidate. Other theories propose that {{ill|Soga no Iname|ja|蘇我稲目}} may be the interred individual. == Saruishi Statues == Adjacent to the {{ill|Tomb of Princess Kibi|ja|吉備姫王墓}}, stone statues known as {{ill|Saruishi|ja|猿石}} ("monkey stones") are placed nearby. These statues were excavated during the Edo period from a field just south of Umeyama Kofun and relocated to their current location in the early {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治時代}}. == References ==
