[[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Umeyama Kofun]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]]{{citation needed|Umeyama Kofun}}[[ja:梅山古墳]] {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Umeyama Kofun | image = [[File:Umeyama Kofun, zenkei-2.jpg|280px]]
Kofun and moat
(Rear circular mound on the right, front rectangular mound in the left background) | location = {{ill|Asuka, Nara|ja|明日香村}}, Nara Prefecture | coordinates = {{Coord|34|28|7.38|N|135|48|2.80|E|region:JP-29_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} | Dimensions = Total length: 140m | Burial facility = Unknown | Construction era = Late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} | Interred individual = ({{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designation) {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇}} | Excavated items = Unknown | Mausoleum = Designated by the Imperial Household Agency as "Hinokuma Sakaai no misasagi" (檜隈坂合陵) | Map = {{location map|Japan Nara|lat_deg=34|lat_min=28|lat_sec=7.38|lon_deg=135|lon_min=48|lon_sec=2.8|label=Umeyama Kofun|mark=前方後円墳-西.svg|marksize=23|caption=location in Nara Prefecture|position=bottom|width=200|float=center|border=none}} }} [[File:Umeyama kohun aerial.jpg|thumb|220px|Bottom left: Kibi-hime Royal Tomb and Saruishi statues ({{National Land Aerial Photography}})]] [[File:Umeyama Kofun, haisho.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Mausoleum of {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇}]]] '''Umeyama Kofun''' (梅山古墳) is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} located in {{ill|Asuka, Nara|ja|明日香村}}, Nara Prefecture. The actual interred individual is unknown, but it has been designated by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} as the "Hinokuma Sakaai no misasagi" (檜隈坂合陵), attributed to {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇}}. == Overview == The tumulus features a keyhole shape, with the front mound oriented to the west along an east-west axis. Measuring approximately 140 meters in total length and 72 meters in diameter for the rear mound, it is the largest tumulus in Asuka Village. The mound is notable for its extensive use of {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}} (covering stones). Surrounding the tumulus is a water-filled moat, which was extensively remodeled during the {{ill|Bunkyū era|ja|文久}} restoration. Originally, the surrounding area was agricultural land. Some theories suggest that the restoration converted the tumulus from a double circular mound to its current keyhole shape.Theory proposed by {{ill|Kōichi Mori|ja|森浩一}} The tumulus is part of a group of Kofuns, including {{ill|Hirata Iwaya Kofun|ja|平田岩屋古墳}}, {{ill|Oni no Manaita and Oni no Senkan|ja|鬼の俎・鬼の雪隠}}, and {{ill|Noguchi Royal Tomb|ja|野口王墓}} (the joint tomb of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu}} and {{ill|Empress Jitō|ja|持統天皇}}), all situated along the southern slope of the same hill. == Construction Period == While believed to have been built during the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, the exact construction date is uncertain due to the lack of archaeological excavations. == Interred Individual == Although designated by the Imperial Household Agency as the tomb of {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇}}, many scholars suggest that the nearby {{ill|Mise Maruyama Kofun|ja|見瀬丸山古墳|1=Maruyama Kofun (Kashihara)}}, located approximately 800 meters northwest and the largest tumulus in Nara Prefecture, might be a more likely candidate. Other theories propose that {{ill|Soga no Iname|ja|蘇我稲目}} may be the interred individual. == Saruishi Statues == Adjacent to the {{ill|Tomb of Princess Kibi|ja|吉備姫王墓}}, stone statues known as {{ill|Saruishi|ja|猿石}} ("monkey stones") are placed nearby. These statues were excavated during the Edo period from a field just south of Umeyama Kofun and relocated to their current location in the early {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治時代}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Kanazuka Kofun|ja|カナヅカ古墳}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Umeyama Kofun}} * [https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/guide/029/index.html Hinokuma Sakaai no misasagi] - Imperial Household Agency [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Ancient tumuli in Nara Prefecture]] Umeyama Kofun | image = [[File:Umeyama Kofun, zenkei-2.jpg|280px]]
Kofun and moat
(Rear circular mound on the right, front rectangular mound in the left background) | location = {{ill|Asuka, Nara|ja|明日香村}}, Nara Prefecture | coordinates = {{Coord|34|28|7.38|N|135|48|2.80|E|region:JP-29_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} | Dimensions = Total length: 140m | Burial facility = Unknown | Construction era = Late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} | Interred individual = ({{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designation) {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇}} | Excavated items = Unknown | Mausoleum = Designated by the Imperial Household Agency as "Hinokuma Sakaai no misasagi" (檜隈坂合陵) | Map = {{location map|Japan Nara|lat_deg=34|lat_min=28|lat_sec=7.38|lon_deg=135|lon_min=48|lon_sec=2.8|label=Umeyama Kofun|mark=前方後円墳-西.svg|marksize=23|caption=location in Nara Prefecture|position=bottom|width=200|float=center|border=none}} }} [[File:Umeyama kohun aerial.jpg|thumb|220px|Bottom left: Kibi-hime Royal Tomb and Saruishi statues ({{National Land Aerial Photography}})]] [[File:Umeyama Kofun, haisho.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Mausoleum of {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇}]]] '''Umeyama Kofun''' (梅山古墳) is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} located in {{ill|Asuka, Nara|ja|明日香村}}, Nara Prefecture. The actual interred individual is unknown, but it has been designated by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} as the "Hinokuma Sakaai no misasagi" (檜隈坂合陵), attributed to {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇}}. == Overview == The tumulus features a keyhole shape, with the front mound oriented to the west along an east-west axis. Measuring approximately 140 meters in total length and 72 meters in diameter for the rear mound, it is the largest tumulus in Asuka Village. The mound is notable for its extensive use of {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}} (covering stones). Surrounding the tumulus is a water-filled moat, which was extensively remodeled during the {{ill|Bunkyū era|ja|文久}} restoration. Originally, the surrounding area was agricultural land. Some theories suggest that the restoration converted the tumulus from a double circular mound to its current keyhole shape.Theory proposed by {{ill|Kōichi Mori|ja|森浩一}} The tumulus is part of a group of Kofuns, including {{ill|Hirata Iwaya Kofun|ja|平田岩屋古墳}}, {{ill|Oni no Manaita and Oni no Senkan|ja|鬼の俎・鬼の雪隠}}, and {{ill|Noguchi Royal Tomb|ja|野口王墓}} (the joint tomb of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu}} and {{ill|Empress Jitō|ja|持統天皇}}), all situated along the southern slope of the same hill. == Construction Period == While believed to have been built during the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, the exact construction date is uncertain due to the lack of archaeological excavations. == Interred Individual == Although designated by the Imperial Household Agency as the tomb of {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇}}, many scholars suggest that the nearby {{ill|Mise Maruyama Kofun|ja|見瀬丸山古墳|1=Maruyama Kofun (Kashihara)}}, located approximately 800 meters northwest and the largest tumulus in Nara Prefecture, might be a more likely candidate. Other theories propose that {{ill|Soga no Iname|ja|蘇我稲目}} may be the interred individual. == Saruishi Statues == Adjacent to the {{ill|Tomb of Princess Kibi|ja|吉備姫王墓}}, stone statues known as {{ill|Saruishi|ja|猿石}} ("monkey stones") are placed nearby. These statues were excavated during the Edo period from a field just south of Umeyama Kofun and relocated to their current location in the early {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治時代}}. == References == Kofun]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]]{{citation needed|Umeyama Kofun}}[[ja:梅山古墳]] {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Umeyama Kofun | image = [[File:Umeyama Kofun, zenkei-2.jpg|280px]]
Kofun and moat
(Rear circular mound on the right, front rectangular mound in the left background) | location = {{ill|Asuka, Nara|ja|明日香村}}, Nara Prefecture | coordinates = {{Coord|34|28|7.38|N|135|48|2.80|E|region:JP-29_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} | Dimensions = Total length: 140m | Burial facility = Unknown | Construction era = Late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} | Interred individual = ({{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designation) {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇}} | Excavated items = Unknown | Mausoleum = Designated by the Imperial Household Agency as "Hinokuma Sakaai no misasagi" (檜隈坂合陵) | map = {{location map|Japan Nara|lat_deg=34|lat_min=28|lat_sec=7.38|lon_deg=135|lon_min=48|lon_sec=2.8|label=Umeyama Kofun|mark=前方後円墳-西.svg|marksize=23|caption=location in Nara Prefecture|position=bottom|width=200|float=center|border=none}} }} [[File:Umeyama kohun aerial.jpg|thumb|220px|Bottom left: Kibi-hime Royal Tomb and Saruishi statues ({{National Land Aerial Photography}})]] [[File:Umeyama Kofun, haisho.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Mausoleum of {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇}]]] '''Umeyama Kofun''' (梅山古墳) is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} located in {{ill|Asuka, Nara|ja|明日香村}}, Nara Prefecture. The actual interred individual is unknown, but it has been designated by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} as the "Hinokuma Sakaai no misasagi" (檜隈坂合陵), attributed to {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇}}. == Overview == The tumulus features a keyhole shape, with the front mound oriented to the west along an east-west axis. Measuring approximately 140 meters in total length and 72 meters in diameter for the rear mound, it is the largest tumulus in Asuka Village. The mound is notable for its extensive use of {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}} (covering stones). Surrounding the tumulus is a water-filled moat, which was extensively remodeled during the {{ill|Bunkyū era|ja|文久}} restoration. Originally, the surrounding area was agricultural land. Some theories suggest that the restoration converted the tumulus from a double circular mound to its current keyhole shape.Theory proposed by {{ill|Kōichi Mori|ja|森浩一}} The tumulus is part of a group of Kofuns, including {{ill|Hirata Iwaya Kofun|ja|平田岩屋古墳}}, {{ill|Oni no Manaita and Oni no Senkan|ja|鬼の俎・鬼の雪隠}}, and {{ill|Noguchi Royal Tomb|ja|野口王墓}} (the joint tomb of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu}} and {{ill|Empress Jitō|ja|持統天皇}}), all situated along the southern slope of the same hill. == built == While believed to have been built during the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, the exact construction date is uncertain due to the lack of archaeological excavations. == Interred Individual == Although designated by the Imperial Household Agency as the tomb of {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇}}, many scholars suggest that the nearby {{ill|Mise Maruyama Kofun|ja|見瀬丸山古墳|1=Maruyama Kofun (Kashihara)}}, located approximately 800 meters northwest and the largest tumulus in Nara Prefecture, might be a more likely candidate. Other theories propose that {{ill|Soga no Iname|ja|蘇我稲目}} may be the interred individual. == Saruishi Statues == Adjacent to the {{ill|Tomb of Princess Kibi|ja|吉備姫王墓}}, stone statues known as {{ill|Saruishi|ja|猿石}} ("monkey stones") are placed nearby. These statues were excavated during the Edo period from a field just south of Umeyama Kofun and relocated to their current location in the early {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治時代}}. == References == misasagi] - Imperial Household Agency [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Ancient tumuli in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Umeyama Kofun]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]]{{citation needed|Umeyama Kofun}}[[ja:梅山古墳]] {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Umeyama Kofun | image = [[File:Umeyama Kofun, zenkei-2.jpg|280px]]
Kofun and moat
(Rear circular mound on the right, front rectangular mound in the left background) | location = {{ill|Asuka, Nara|ja|明日香村}}, Nara Prefecture | coordinates = {{Coord|34|28|7.38|N|135|48|2.80|E|region:JP-29_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} | Dimensions = Total length: 140m | Burial facility = Unknown | Construction era = Late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} | Interred_individual = ({{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designation) {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇}} | Excavated items = Unknown | Mausoleum = Designated by the Imperial Household Agency as "Hinokuma Sakaai no misasagi" (檜隈坂合陵) | map = {{location map|Japan Nara|lat_deg=34|lat_min=28|lat_sec=7.38|lon_deg=135|lon_min=48|lon_sec=2.8|label=Umeyama Kofun|mark=前方後円墳-西.svg|marksize=23|caption=location in Nara Prefecture|position=bottom|width=200|float=center|border=none}} }} [[File:Umeyama kohun aerial.jpg|thumb|220px|Bottom left: Kibi-hime Royal Tomb and Saruishi statues ({{National Land Aerial Photography}})]] [[File:Umeyama Kofun, haisho.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Mausoleum of {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇}]]] '''Umeyama Kofun''' (梅山古墳) is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} located in {{ill|Asuka, Nara|ja|明日香村}}, Nara Prefecture. The actual interred individual is unknown, but it has been designated by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} as the "Hinokuma Sakaai no misasagi" (檜隈坂合陵), attributed to {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇}}. == Overview == The tumulus features a keyhole shape, with the front mound oriented to the west along an east-west axis. Measuring approximately 140 meters in total length and 72 meters in diameter for the rear mound, it is the largest tumulus in Asuka Village. The mound is notable for its extensive use of {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}} (covering stones). Surrounding the tumulus is a water-filled moat, which was extensively remodeled during the {{ill|Bunkyū era|ja|文久}} restoration. Originally, the surrounding area was agricultural land. Some theories suggest that the restoration converted the tumulus from a double circular mound to its current keyhole shape.Theory proposed by {{ill|Kōichi Mori|ja|森浩一}} The tumulus is part of a group of Kofuns, including {{ill|Hirata Iwaya Kofun|ja|平田岩屋古墳}}, {{ill|Oni no Manaita and Oni no Senkan|ja|鬼の俎・鬼の雪隠}}, and {{ill|Noguchi Royal Tomb|ja|野口王墓}} (the joint tomb of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu}} and {{ill|Empress Jitō|ja|持統天皇}}), all situated along the southern slope of the same hill. == built == While believed to have been built during the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, the exact construction date is uncertain due to the lack of archaeological excavations. == Interred Individual == Although designated by the Imperial Household Agency as the tomb of {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇}}, many scholars suggest that the nearby {{ill|Mise Maruyama Kofun|ja|見瀬丸山古墳|1=Maruyama Kofun (Kashihara)}}, located approximately 800 meters northwest and the largest tumulus in Nara Prefecture, might be a more likely candidate. Other theories propose that {{ill|Soga no Iname|ja|蘇我稲目}} may be the interred individual. == Saruishi Statues == Adjacent to the {{ill|Tomb of Princess Kibi|ja|吉備姫王墓}}, stone statues known as {{ill|Saruishi|ja|猿石}} ("monkey stones") are placed nearby. These statues were excavated during the Edo period from a field just south of Umeyama Kofun and relocated to their current location in the early {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治時代}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Kanazuka Kofun|ja|カナヅカ古墳}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Umeyama Kofun}} * [https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/guide/029/index.html Hinokuma Sakaai no misasagi] - Imperial Household Agency [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Ancient tumuli in Nara Prefecture]] But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Kofun and moat
(Rear circular mound on the right, front rectangular mound in the left background) | location = {{ill|Asuka, Nara|ja|明日香村}}, Nara Prefecture | coordinates = {{Coord|34|28|7.38|N|135|48|2.80|E|region:JP-29_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} | area = Total length: 140m | Burial Facility = Unknown | built = Late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} | Interred_individual = ({{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designation) {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇}} | excavations = Unknown | Mausoleum = Designated by the Imperial Household Agency as "Hinokuma Sakaai no misasagi" (檜隈坂合陵) | map = {{location map|Japan Nara|lat_deg=34|lat_min=28|lat_sec=7.38|lon_deg=135|lon_min=48|lon_sec=2.8|label=Umeyama Kofun|mark=前方後円墳-西.svg|marksize=23|caption=location in Nara Prefecture|position=bottom|width=200|float=center|border=none}} }} [[File:Umeyama kohun aerial.jpg|thumb|220px|Bottom left: Kibi-hime Royal Tomb and Saruishi statues ({{National Land Aerial Photography}})]] [[File:Umeyama Kofun, haisho.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Mausoleum of {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇}]]] '''Umeyama Kofun''' (梅山古墳) is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} located in {{ill|Asuka, Nara|ja|明日香村}}, Nara Prefecture. The actual interred individual is unknown, but it has been designated by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} as the "Hinokuma Sakaai no misasagi" (檜隈坂合陵), attributed to {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇}}. == Overview == The tumulus features a keyhole shape, with the front mound oriented to the west along an east-west axis. Measuring approximately 140 meters in total length and 72 meters in diameter for the rear mound, it is the largest tumulus in Asuka Village. The mound is notable for its extensive use of {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}} (covering stones). Surrounding the tumulus is a water-filled moat, which was extensively remodeled during the {{ill|Bunkyū era|ja|文久}} restoration. Originally, the surrounding area was agricultural land. Some theories suggest that the restoration converted the tumulus from a double circular mound to its current keyhole shape.Theory proposed by {{ill|Kōichi Mori|ja|森浩一}} The tumulus is part of a group of Kofuns, including {{ill|Hirata Iwaya Kofun|ja|平田岩屋古墳}}, {{ill|Oni no Manaita and Oni no Senkan|ja|鬼の俎・鬼の雪隠}}, and {{ill|Noguchi Royal Tomb|ja|野口王墓}} (the joint tomb of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu}} and {{ill|Empress Jitō|ja|持統天皇}}), all situated along the southern slope of the same hill. == built == While believed to have been built during the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, the exact construction date is uncertain due to the lack of archaeological excavations. == Interred Individual == Although designated by the Imperial Household Agency as the tomb of {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇}}, many scholars suggest that the nearby {{ill|Mise Maruyama Kofun|ja|見瀬丸山古墳|1=Maruyama Kofun (Kashihara)}}, located approximately 800 meters northwest and the largest tumulus in Nara Prefecture, might be a more likely candidate. Other theories propose that {{ill|Soga no Iname|ja|蘇我稲目}} may be the interred individual. == Saruishi Statues == Adjacent to the {{ill|Tomb of Princess Kibi|ja|吉備姫王墓}}, stone statues known as {{ill|Saruishi|ja|猿石}} ("monkey stones") are placed nearby. These statues were excavated during the Edo period from a field just south of Umeyama Kofun and relocated to their current location in the early {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治時代}}. == References == [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Umeyama Kofun]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]]{{citation needed|Umeyama Kofun}}[[ja:梅山古墳]] {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Umeyama Kofun | image = [[File:Umeyama Kofun, zenkei-2.jpg|280px]]
Kofun and moat
(Rear circular mound on the right, front rectangular mound in the left background) | location = {{ill|Asuka, Nara|ja|明日香村}}, Nara Prefecture | coordinates = {{Coord|34|28|7.38|N|135|48|2.80|E|region:JP-29_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} | area = Total length: 140m | Burial Facility = Unknown | built = Late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} | Interred_individual = ({{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designation) {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇}} | excavations = Unknown | Mausoleum = Designated by the Imperial Household Agency as "Hinokuma Sakaai no misasagi" (檜隈坂合陵) | map = {{location map|Japan Nara|lat_deg=34|lat_min=28|lat_sec=7.38|lon_deg=135|lon_min=48|lon_sec=2.8|label=Umeyama Kofun|mark=前方後円墳-西.svg|marksize=23|caption=location in Nara Prefecture|position=bottom|width=200|float=center|border=none}} }} [[File:Umeyama kohun aerial.jpg|thumb|220px|Bottom left: Kibi-hime Royal Tomb and Saruishi statues ({{National Land Aerial Photography}})]] [[File:Umeyama Kofun, haisho.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Mausoleum of {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇}]]] '''Umeyama Kofun''' (梅山古墳) is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} located in {{ill|Asuka, Nara|ja|明日香村}}, Nara Prefecture. The actual interred individual is unknown, but it has been designated by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} as the "Hinokuma Sakaai no misasagi" (檜隈坂合陵), attributed to {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇}}. == Overview == The tumulus features a keyhole shape, with the front mound oriented to the west along an east-west axis. Measuring approximately 140 meters in total length and 72 meters in diameter for the rear mound, it is the largest tumulus in Asuka Village. The mound is notable for its extensive use of {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}} (covering stones). Surrounding the tumulus is a water-filled moat, which was extensively remodeled during the {{ill|Bunkyū era|ja|文久}} restoration. Originally, the surrounding area was agricultural land. Some theories suggest that the restoration converted the tumulus from a double circular mound to its current keyhole shape.Theory proposed by {{ill|Kōichi Mori|ja|森浩一}} The tumulus is part of a group of Kofuns, including {{ill|Hirata Iwaya Kofun|ja|平田岩屋古墳}}, {{ill|Oni no Manaita and Oni no Senkan|ja|鬼の俎・鬼の雪隠}}, and {{ill|Noguchi Royal Tomb|ja|野口王墓}} (the joint tomb of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu}} and {{ill|Empress Jitō|ja|持統天皇}}), all situated along the southern slope of the same hill. == built == While believed to have been built during the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, the exact construction date is uncertain due to the lack of archaeological excavations. == Interred Individual == Although designated by the Imperial Household Agency as the tomb of {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇}}, many scholars suggest that the nearby {{ill|Mise Maruyama Kofun|ja|見瀬丸山古墳|1=Maruyama Kofun (Kashihara)}}, located approximately 800 meters northwest and the largest tumulus in Nara Prefecture, might be a more likely candidate. Other theories propose that {{ill|Soga no Iname|ja|蘇我稲目}} may be the interred individual. == Saruishi Statues == Adjacent to the {{ill|Tomb of Princess Kibi|ja|吉備姫王墓}}, stone statues known as {{ill|Saruishi|ja|猿石}} ("monkey stones") are placed nearby. These statues were excavated during the Edo period from a field just south of Umeyama Kofun and relocated to their current location in the early {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治時代}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Kanazuka Kofun|ja|カナヅカ古墳}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Umeyama Kofun}} * [https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/guide/029/index.html Hinokuma Sakaai no misasagi] - Imperial Household Agency [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Ancient tumuli in Nara Prefecture]] (Rear circular mound on the right, front rectangular mound in the left background) | location = {{ill|Asuka, Nara|ja|明日香村}}, Nara Prefecture | coordinates = {{Coord|34|28|7.38|N|135|48|2.80|E|region:JP-29_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} | area = Total length: 140m | Burial Facility = Unknown | built = Late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} | Interred_individual = ({{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designation) {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇}} | excavations = Unknown | Mausoleum = Designated by the Imperial Household Agency as "Hinokuma Sakaai no misasagi" (檜隈坂合陵) | map = {{location map|Japan Nara|lat_deg=34|lat_min=28|lat_sec=7.38|lon_deg=135|lon_min=48|lon_sec=2.8|label=Umeyama Kofun|mark=前方後円墳-西.svg|marksize=23|caption=location in Nara Prefecture|position=bottom|width=200|float=center|border=none}} }} [[File:Umeyama kohun aerial.jpg|thumb|220px|Bottom left: Kibi-hime Royal Tomb and Saruishi statues ({{National Land Aerial Photography}})]] [[File:Umeyama Kofun, haisho.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Mausoleum of {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇}]]] '''Umeyama Kofun''' (梅山古墳) is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} located in {{ill|Asuka, Nara|ja|明日香村}}, Nara Prefecture. The actual interred individual is unknown, but it has been designated by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} as the "Hinokuma Sakaai no misasagi" (檜隈坂合陵), attributed to {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇}}. == Overview == The tumulus features a keyhole shape, with the front mound oriented to the west along an east-west axis. Measuring approximately 140 meters in total length and 72 meters in diameter for the rear mound, it is the largest tumulus in Asuka Village. The mound is notable for its extensive use of {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}} (covering stones). Surrounding the tumulus is a water-filled moat, which was extensively remodeled during the {{ill|Bunkyū era|ja|文久}} restoration. Originally, the surrounding area was agricultural land. Some theories suggest that the restoration converted the tumulus from a double circular mound to its current keyhole shape.Theory proposed by {{ill|Kōichi Mori|ja|森浩一}} The tumulus is part of a group of Kofuns, including {{ill|Hirata Iwaya Kofun|ja|平田岩屋古墳}}, {{ill|Oni no Manaita and Oni no Senkan|ja|鬼の俎・鬼の雪隠}}, and {{ill|Noguchi Royal Tomb|ja|野口王墓}} (the joint tomb of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu}} and {{ill|Empress Jitō|ja|持統天皇}}), all situated along the southern slope of the same hill. == built == While believed to have been built during the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, the exact construction date is uncertain due to the lack of archaeological excavations. == Interred Individual == Although designated by the Imperial Household Agency as the tomb of {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇}}, many scholars suggest that the nearby {{ill|Mise Maruyama Kofun|ja|見瀬丸山古墳|1=Maruyama Kofun (Kashihara)}}, located approximately 800 meters northwest and the largest tumulus in Nara Prefecture, might be a more likely candidate. Other theories propose that {{ill|Soga no Iname|ja|蘇我稲目}} may be the interred individual. == Saruishi Statues == Adjacent to the {{ill|Tomb of Princess Kibi|ja|吉備姫王墓}}, stone statues known as {{ill|Saruishi|ja|猿石}} ("monkey stones") are placed nearby. These statues were excavated during the Edo period from a field just south of Umeyama Kofun and relocated to their current location in the early {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治時代}}. == References == Kofun and moat
(Rear circular mound on the right, front rectangular mound in the left background) | location = {{ill|Asuka, Nara|ja|明日香村}}, Nara Prefecture | coordinates = {{Coord|34|28|7.38|N|135|48|2.80|E|region:JP-29_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} | area = Total length: 140m | Burial Facility = Unknown | built = Late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} | Interred_individual = ({{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designation) {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇}} | excavations = Unknown | Mausoleum = Designated by the Imperial Household Agency as "Hinokuma Sakaai no misasagi" (檜隈坂合陵) | map = {{location map|Japan Nara|lat_deg=34|lat_min=28|lat_sec=7.38|lon_deg=135|lon_min=48|lon_sec=2.8|label=Umeyama Kofun|mark=前方後円墳-西.svg|marksize=23|caption=location in Nara Prefecture|position=bottom|width=200|float=center|border=none}} }} [[File:Umeyama kohun aerial.jpg|thumb|220px|Bottom left: Kibi-hime Royal Tomb and Saruishi statues ({{National Land Aerial Photography}})]] [[File:Umeyama Kofun, haisho.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Mausoleum of {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇}]]] '''Umeyama Kofun''' (梅山古墳) is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} located in {{ill|Asuka, Nara|ja|明日香村}}, Nara Prefecture. The actual interred individual is unknown, but it has been designated by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} as the "Hinokuma Sakaai no misasagi" (檜隈坂合陵), attributed to {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇}}. == Overview == The tumulus features a keyhole shape, with the front mound oriented to the west along an east-west axis. Measuring approximately 140 meters in total length and 72 meters in diameter for the rear mound, it is the largest tumulus in Asuka Village. The mound is notable for its extensive use of {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}} (covering stones). Surrounding the tumulus is a water-filled moat, which was extensively remodeled during the {{ill|Bunkyū era|ja|文久}} restoration. Originally, the surrounding area was agricultural land. Some theories suggest that the restoration converted the tumulus from a double circular mound to its current keyhole shape.Theory proposed by {{ill|Kōichi Mori|ja|森浩一}} The tumulus is part of a group of Kofuns, including {{ill|Hirata Iwaya Kofun|ja|平田岩屋古墳}}, {{ill|Oni no Manaita and Oni no Senkan|ja|鬼の俎・鬼の雪隠}}, and {{ill|Noguchi Royal Tomb|ja|野口王墓}} (the joint tomb of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu}} and {{ill|Empress Jitō|ja|持統天皇}}), all situated along the southern slope of the same hill. == built == While believed to have been built during the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, the exact construction date is uncertain due to the lack of archaeological excavations. == Interred Individual == Although designated by the Imperial Household Agency as the tomb of {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇}}, many scholars suggest that the nearby {{ill|Mise Maruyama Kofun|ja|見瀬丸山古墳|1=Maruyama Kofun (Kashihara)}}, located approximately 800 meters northwest and the largest tumulus in Nara Prefecture, might be a more likely candidate. Other theories propose that {{ill|Soga no Iname|ja|蘇我稲目}} may be the interred individual. == Saruishi Statues == Adjacent to the {{ill|Tomb of Princess Kibi|ja|吉備姫王墓}}, stone statues known as {{ill|Saruishi|ja|猿石}} ("monkey stones") are placed nearby. These statues were excavated during the Edo period from a field just south of Umeyama Kofun and relocated to their current location in the early {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治時代}}. == References == [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Umeyama Kofun]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]]{{citation needed|Umeyama Kofun}}[[ja:梅山古墳]] {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Umeyama Kofun | image = [[File:Umeyama Kofun, zenkei-2.jpg|280px]]
Kofun and moat
(Rear circular mound on the right, front rectangular mound in the left background) | location = {{ill|Asuka, Nara|ja|明日香村}}, Nara Prefecture | coordinates = {{Coord|34|28|7.38|N|135|48|2.80|E|region:JP-29_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} | area = Total length: 140m | Burial Facility = Unknown | built = Late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} | Interred_individual = ({{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designation) {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇}} | excavations = Unknown | Mausoleum = Designated by the Imperial Household Agency as "Hinokuma Sakaai no misasagi" (檜隈坂合陵) | map = {{location map|Japan Nara|lat_deg=34|lat_min=28|lat_sec=7.38|lon_deg=135|lon_min=48|lon_sec=2.8|label=Umeyama Kofun|mark=前方後円墳-西.svg|marksize=23|caption=location in Nara Prefecture|position=bottom|width=200|float=center|border=none}} }} [[File:Umeyama kohun aerial.jpg|thumb|220px|Bottom left: Kibi-hime Royal Tomb and Saruishi statues ({{National Land Aerial Photography}})]] [[File:Umeyama Kofun, haisho.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Mausoleum of {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇}]]] '''Umeyama Kofun''' (梅山古墳) is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} located in {{ill|Asuka, Nara|ja|明日香村}}, Nara Prefecture. The actual interred individual is unknown, but it has been designated by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} as the "Hinokuma Sakaai no misasagi" (檜隈坂合陵), attributed to {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇}}. == Overview == The tumulus features a keyhole shape, with the front mound oriented to the west along an east-west axis. Measuring approximately 140 meters in total length and 72 meters in diameter for the rear mound, it is the largest tumulus in Asuka Village. The mound is notable for its extensive use of {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}} (covering stones). Surrounding the tumulus is a water-filled moat, which was extensively remodeled during the {{ill|Bunkyū era|ja|文久}} restoration. Originally, the surrounding area was agricultural land. Some theories suggest that the restoration converted the tumulus from a double circular mound to its current keyhole shape.Theory proposed by {{ill|Kōichi Mori|ja|森浩一}} The tumulus is part of a group of Kofuns, including {{ill|Hirata Iwaya Kofun|ja|平田岩屋古墳}}, {{ill|Oni no Manaita and Oni no Senkan|ja|鬼の俎・鬼の雪隠}}, and {{ill|Noguchi Royal Tomb|ja|野口王墓}} (the joint tomb of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu}} and {{ill|Empress Jitō|ja|持統天皇}}), all situated along the southern slope of the same hill. == built == While believed to have been built during the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, the exact construction date is uncertain due to the lack of archaeological excavations. == Interred Individual == Although designated by the Imperial Household Agency as the tomb of {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇}}, many scholars suggest that the nearby {{ill|Mise Maruyama Kofun|ja|見瀬丸山古墳|1=Maruyama Kofun (Kashihara)}}, located approximately 800 meters northwest and the largest tumulus in Nara Prefecture, might be a more likely candidate. Other theories propose that {{ill|Soga no Iname|ja|蘇我稲目}} may be the interred individual. == Saruishi Statues == Adjacent to the {{ill|Tomb of Princess Kibi|ja|吉備姫王墓}}, stone statues known as {{ill|Saruishi|ja|猿石}} ("monkey stones") are placed nearby. These statues were excavated during the Edo period from a field just south of Umeyama Kofun and relocated to their current location in the early {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治時代}}. == References == (Rear circular mound on the right, front rectangular mound in the left background) | location = {{ill|Asuka, Nara|ja|明日香村}}, Nara Prefecture | coordinates = {{Coord|34|28|7.38|N|135|48|2.80|E|region:JP-29_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} | area = Total length: 140m | Burial Facility = Unknown | built = Late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} | Interred_individual = ({{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designation) {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇}} | excavations = Unknown | Mausoleum = Designated by the Imperial Household Agency as "Hinokuma Sakaai no misasagi" (檜隈坂合陵) | map = {{location map|Japan Nara|lat_deg=34|lat_min=28|lat_sec=7.38|lon_deg=135|lon_min=48|lon_sec=2.8|label=Umeyama Kofun|mark=前方後円墳-西.svg|marksize=23|caption=location in Nara Prefecture|position=bottom|width=200|float=center|border=none}} }} [[File:Umeyama kohun aerial.jpg|thumb|220px|Bottom left: Kibi-hime Royal Tomb and Saruishi statues ({{National Land Aerial Photography}})]] [[File:Umeyama Kofun, haisho.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Mausoleum of {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇}]]] '''Umeyama Kofun''' (梅山古墳) is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} located in {{ill|Asuka, Nara|ja|明日香村}}, Nara Prefecture. The actual interred individual is unknown, but it has been designated by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} as the "Hinokuma Sakaai no misasagi" (檜隈坂合陵), attributed to {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇}}. == Overview == The tumulus features a keyhole shape, with the front mound oriented to the west along an east-west axis. Measuring approximately 140 meters in total length and 72 meters in diameter for the rear mound, it is the largest tumulus in Asuka Village. The mound is notable for its extensive use of {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}} (covering stones). Surrounding the tumulus is a water-filled moat, which was extensively remodeled during the {{ill|Bunkyū era|ja|文久}} restoration. Originally, the surrounding area was agricultural land. Some theories suggest that the restoration converted the tumulus from a double circular mound to its current keyhole shape.Theory proposed by {{ill|Kōichi Mori|ja|森浩一}} The tumulus is part of a group of Kofuns, including {{ill|Hirata Iwaya Kofun|ja|平田岩屋古墳}}, {{ill|Oni no Manaita and Oni no Senkan|ja|鬼の俎・鬼の雪隠}}, and {{ill|Noguchi Royal Tomb|ja|野口王墓}} (the joint tomb of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu}} and {{ill|Empress Jitō|ja|持統天皇}}), all situated along the southern slope of the same hill. == built == While believed to have been built during the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, the exact construction date is uncertain due to the lack of archaeological excavations. == Interred Individual == Although designated by the Imperial Household Agency as the tomb of {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇}}, many scholars suggest that the nearby {{ill|Mise Maruyama Kofun|ja|見瀬丸山古墳|1=Maruyama Kofun (Kashihara)}}, located approximately 800 meters northwest and the largest tumulus in Nara Prefecture, might be a more likely candidate. Other theories propose that {{ill|Soga no Iname|ja|蘇我稲目}} may be the interred individual. == Saruishi Statues == Adjacent to the {{ill|Tomb of Princess Kibi|ja|吉備姫王墓}}, stone statues known as {{ill|Saruishi|ja|猿石}} ("monkey stones") are placed nearby. These statues were excavated during the Edo period from a field just south of Umeyama Kofun and relocated to their current location in the early {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治時代}}. == References == revision.text:
{{日本の古墳 |名称=梅山古墳 |画像=[[File:Umeyama Kofun, zenkei-2.jpg|280px]]
墳丘と周濠
（右に後円部、左奥に前方部） |所在地=[[奈良県]][[高市郡]][[明日香村]]平田 |位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|28|7.38|N|135|48|2.80|E|region:JP-29_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=梅山古墳（伝欽明天皇陵）}} |形状=[[前方後円墳]] |規模=墳丘長140[[メートル|m]] |埋葬施設= |築造年代=[[古墳時代後期]] |被葬者=（[[宮内庁]]治定）第29代[[欽明天皇]] |出土品= |陵墓=[[宮内庁]]治定「檜隈坂合陵」 |特記事項= |地図2= {{Location map|Japan Nara|lat_deg=34|lat_min=28|lat_sec=7.38|lon_deg=135|lon_min=48|lon_sec=2.8|label=梅山古墳|mark=前方後円墳-西.svg|marksize=23|caption=奈良県内の位置|position=bottom|width=200|float=center|border=none}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|28|7.38|N|135|48|2.8|E}}}} }} [[ファイル:Umeyama kohun aerial.jpg|thumb|220px|左下に吉備姫皇女王墓・猿石（{{国土航空写真}}）]] [[File:Umeyama Kofun, haisho.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|[[欽明天皇]]檜隈坂合陵 拝所}}]] '''梅山古墳'''（うめやまこふん）は、[[奈良県]][[高市郡]][[明日香村]]にある[[古墳]]。形状は[[前方後円墳]]。 実際の被葬者は明らかでないが、[[宮内庁]]により「'''檜隈坂合陵'''（ひのくまのさかあいのみささぎ）」として第29代[[欽明天皇]]の[[天皇陵|陵]]に治定されている。檜隈坂合陵には[[推古天皇]]20年（612年）2月に[[蘇我堅塩媛|堅塩媛]]も[[改葬]]されている。『日本書記』、巻第二十二 == 概要 == 墳丘は前方部を西に向け東西に正確に主軸をとった[[前方後円墳]]で、全長約140[[メートル|m]] 後円部径72mで、明日香村内では最大の古墳である。非常に多くの[[葺石]]があることでも知られている。現在は回りに水を湛えた周濠を持っているが、これは[[文久]]の修復で大幅に改築されたものであり元は[[田]]であった。なお、この修復の際に双円墳から前方後円墳に改造されたとする考えもある[[森浩一]]による説。他にも[[外池昇]]『検証 天皇陵』p.97でも、『文久山陵図』の荒無図の檜隈坂合陵は双円墳のようと評している。。 同古墳と[[平田岩屋古墳]]、[[鬼の爼・鬼の雪隠|鬼の俎・雪隠]]古墳、[[野口王墓]]（天武・持統合葬陵）は同じ丘陵の南側の地形を利用して造られており、4つの古墳が東西に並んでいる。 == 築造時期 == [[古墳時代後期]]の築造とみられているが、[[発掘調査]]が行われていないため正確な築造時期を推定することは難しい。 == 埋葬者 == 宮内庁によって欽明天皇陵とされているが、梅山古墳から北西に約800m行った所にある奈良県下最大の古墳の[[見瀬丸山古墳]]を欽明天皇陵とする学説も多い。梅山古墳の埋葬者については[[蘇我稲目]]とする考えもある。 == 猿石 == 隣接する[[吉備姫王]]墓に[[猿石]]と呼ばれる石像物が置かれているが、これは江戸時代に梅山古墳のすぐ南の田（小字池田）で掘り出されて古墳のかたわらに置かれていたもので、[[明治]]初期に現在の場所に移された。 == 脚注 == {{Reflist}} == 関連項目 == * [[カナヅカ古墳]] == 外部リンク == {{Commonscat|Umeyama Kofun}} *[https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/guide/029/index.html 檜隈坂合陵] - 宮内庁 {{デフォルトソート:うめやまこふん}} [[Category:奈良県の古墳]] [[Category:明日香村の歴史]] [[Category:前方後円墳]] [[Category:日本の陵墓]] [[Category:欽明天皇]]

{{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'Burial Facility' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Umeyama Kofun | image = [[File:Umeyama Kofun, zenkei-2.jpg|280px]]
Kofun and moat
(Rear circular mound on the right, front rectangular mound in the left background) | location = {{ill|Asuka, Nara|ja|明日香村|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Nara Prefecture | coordinates = {{Coord|34|28|7.38|N|135|48|2.80|E|region:JP-29_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | area = Total length: 140m | Burial Facility = Unknown | built = Late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | Interred_individual = ({{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁|qq=draft|qq=draft}} designation) {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | excavations = Unknown | Mausoleum = Designated by the Imperial Household Agency as "Hinokuma Sakaai no misasagi" (檜隈坂合陵) | map = {{location map|Japan Nara|lat_deg=34|lat_min=28|lat_sec=7.38|lon_deg=135|lon_min=48|lon_sec=2.8|label=Umeyama Kofun|mark=前方後円墳-西.svg|marksize=23|caption=location in Nara Prefecture|position=bottom|width=200|float=center|border=none}} }} [[File:Umeyama kohun aerial.jpg|thumb|220px|Bottom left: Kibi-hime Royal Tomb and Saruishi statues ({{National Land Aerial Photography}})]] [[File:Umeyama Kofun, haisho.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Mausoleum of {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇}]]] '''Umeyama Kofun''' (梅山古墳) is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|qq=draft|qq=draft}} located in {{ill|Asuka, Nara|ja|明日香村|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Nara Prefecture. The actual interred individual is unknown, but it has been designated by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁|qq=draft|qq=draft}} as the "Hinokuma Sakaai no misasagi" (檜隈坂合陵), attributed to {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. == Overview == The tumulus features a keyhole shape, with the front mound oriented to the west along an east-west axis. Measuring approximately 140 meters in total length and 72 meters in diameter for the rear mound, it is the largest tumulus in Asuka Village. The mound is notable for its extensive use of {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (covering stones). Surrounding the tumulus is a water-filled moat, which was extensively remodeled during the {{ill|Bunkyū era|ja|文久|qq=draft|qq=draft}} restoration. Originally, the surrounding area was agricultural land. Some theories suggest that the restoration converted the tumulus from a double circular mound to its current keyhole shape.Theory proposed by {{ill|Kōichi Mori|ja|森浩一|qq=draft|qq=draft}} The tumulus is part of a group of Kofuns, including {{ill|Hirata Iwaya Kofun|ja|平田岩屋古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Oni no Manaita and Oni no Senkan|ja|鬼の俎・鬼の雪隠|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and {{ill|Noguchi Royal Tomb|ja|野口王墓|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (the joint tomb of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Empress Jitō|ja|持統天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}}), all situated along the southern slope of the same hill. == built == While believed to have been built during the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, the exact construction date is uncertain due to the lack of archaeological excavations. == Interred Individual == Although designated by the Imperial Household Agency as the tomb of {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, many scholars suggest that the nearby {{ill|Mise Maruyama Kofun|ja|見瀬丸山古墳|1=Maruyama Kofun (Kashihara)|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, located approximately 800 meters northwest and the largest tumulus in Nara Prefecture, might be a more likely candidate. Other theories propose that {{ill|Soga no Iname|ja|蘇我稲目|qq=draft|qq=draft}} may be the interred individual. == Saruishi Statues == Adjacent to the {{ill|Tomb of Princess Kibi|ja|吉備姫王墓|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, stone statues known as {{ill|Saruishi|ja|猿石|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ("monkey stones") are placed nearby. These statues were excavated during the Edo period from a field just south of Umeyama Kofun and relocated to their current location in the early {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治時代|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Kanazuka Kofun|ja|カナヅカ古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Umeyama Kofun}} * [https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/guide/029/index.html Hinokuma Sakaai no misasagi] - Imperial Household Agency {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|梅山古墳|version=103253627|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Umeyama Kofun]] [[Category:Ancient tumuli in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Asuka, Nara]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Imperial mausolea in Japan]] [[Category:Emperor Kinmei]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Umeyama Kofun]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Umeyama Kofun]] [[ja:梅山古墳]] [[en:Umeyama Kofun]] [[sn:meyama Kofun]]

Kofun and moat
(Rear circular mound on the right, front rectangular mound in the left background) | location = {{ill|Asuka, Nara|ja|明日香村|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Nara Prefecture | coordinates = {{Coord|34|28|7.38|N|135|48|2.80|E|region:JP-29_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | area = Total length: 140m | Burial Facility = Unknown | built = Late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | Interred_individual = ({{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁|qq=draft|qq=draft}} designation) {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | excavations = Unknown | Mausoleum = Designated by the Imperial Household Agency as "Hinokuma Sakaai no misasagi" (檜隈坂合陵) | map = {{location map|Japan Nara|lat_deg=34|lat_min=28|lat_sec=7.38|lon_deg=135|lon_min=48|lon_sec=2.8|label=Umeyama Kofun|mark=前方後円墳-西.svg|marksize=23|caption=location in Nara Prefecture|position=bottom|width=200|float=center|border=none}} }} [[File:Umeyama kohun aerial.jpg|thumb|220px|Bottom left: Kibi-hime Royal Tomb and Saruishi statues ({{National Land Aerial Photography}})]] [[File:Umeyama Kofun, haisho.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Mausoleum of {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇}]]] '''Umeyama Kofun''' (梅山古墳) is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|qq=draft|qq=draft}} located in {{ill|Asuka, Nara|ja|明日香村|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Nara Prefecture. The actual interred individual is unknown, but it has been designated by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁|qq=draft|qq=draft}} as the "Hinokuma Sakaai no misasagi" (檜隈坂合陵), attributed to {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. == Overview == The tumulus features a keyhole shape, with the front mound oriented to the west along an east-west axis. Measuring approximately 140 meters in total length and 72 meters in diameter for the rear mound, it is the largest tumulus in Asuka Village. The mound is notable for its extensive use of {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (covering stones). Surrounding the tumulus is a water-filled moat, which was extensively remodeled during the {{ill|Bunkyū era|ja|文久|qq=draft|qq=draft}} restoration. Originally, the surrounding area was agricultural land. Some theories suggest that the restoration converted the tumulus from a double circular mound to its current keyhole shape.Theory proposed by {{ill|Kōichi Mori|ja|森浩一|qq=draft|qq=draft}} The tumulus is part of a group of Kofuns, including {{ill|Hirata Iwaya Kofun|ja|平田岩屋古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Oni no Manaita and Oni no Senkan|ja|鬼の俎・鬼の雪隠|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and {{ill|Noguchi Royal Tomb|ja|野口王墓|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (the joint tomb of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Empress Jitō|ja|持統天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}}), all situated along the southern slope of the same hill. == built == While believed to have been built during the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, the exact construction date is uncertain due to the lack of archaeological excavations. == Interred Individual == Although designated by the Imperial Household Agency as the tomb of {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, many scholars suggest that the nearby {{ill|Mise Maruyama Kofun|ja|見瀬丸山古墳|1=Maruyama Kofun (Kashihara)|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, located approximately 800 meters northwest and the largest tumulus in Nara Prefecture, might be a more likely candidate. Other theories propose that {{ill|Soga no Iname|ja|蘇我稲目|qq=draft|qq=draft}} may be the interred individual. == Saruishi Statues == Adjacent to the {{ill|Tomb of Princess Kibi|ja|吉備姫王墓|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, stone statues known as {{ill|Saruishi|ja|猿石|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ("monkey stones") are placed nearby. These statues were excavated during the Edo period from a field just south of Umeyama Kofun and relocated to their current location in the early {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治時代|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Kanazuka Kofun|ja|カナヅカ古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Umeyama Kofun}} * [https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/guide/029/index.html Hinokuma Sakaai no misasagi] - Imperial Household Agency {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|梅山古墳|version=103253627|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Umeyama Kofun]] [[Category:Ancient tumuli in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Asuka, Nara]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Imperial mausolea in Japan]] [[Category:Emperor Kinmei]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[sn:Umeyama Kofun]]}} [[sn:Umeyama Kofun]] [[ja:梅山古墳]] [[en:Umeyama Kofun]] [[sn:meyama Kofun]]

Kofun and moat
(Rear circular mound on the right, front rectangular mound in the left background) | location = {{ill|Asuka, Nara|ja|明日香村|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asuka, Nara|qq=}}, Nara Prefecture | coordinates = {{Coord|34|28|7.38|N|135|48|2.80|E|region:JP-29_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Keyhole-shaped tumulus|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpokoenfun|qq=}} | area = Total length: 140m | Burial Facility = Unknown | built = Late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofun period|qq=}} | Interred_individual = ({{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Household Agency|qq=}} designation) {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Kinmei|qq=}} | excavations = Unknown | Mausoleum = Designated by the Imperial Household Agency as "Hinokuma Sakaai no misasagi" (檜隈坂合陵) | map = {{location map|Japan Nara|lat_deg=34|lat_min=28|lat_sec=7.38|lon_deg=135|lon_min=48|lon_sec=2.8|label=Umeyama Kofun|mark=前方後円墳-西.svg|marksize=23|caption=location in Nara Prefecture|position=bottom|width=200|float=center|border=none}} }} [[File:Umeyama kohun aerial.jpg|thumb|220px|Bottom left: Kibi-hime Royal Tomb and Saruishi statues ({{National Land Aerial Photography}})]] [[File:Umeyama Kofun, haisho.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Mausoleum of {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇}]]] '''Umeyama Kofun''' (梅山古墳) is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/keyhole-shaped tumulus|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpokoenfun|qq=}} located in {{ill|Asuka, Nara|ja|明日香村|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asuka, Nara|qq=}}, Nara Prefecture. The actual interred individual is unknown, but it has been designated by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Household Agency|qq=}} as the "Hinokuma Sakaai no misasagi" (檜隈坂合陵), attributed to {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Kinmei|qq=}}. == Overview == The tumulus features a keyhole shape, with the front mound oriented to the west along an east-west axis. Measuring approximately 140 meters in total length and 72 meters in diameter for the rear mound, it is the largest tumulus in Asuka Village. The mound is notable for its extensive use of {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/fukiishi|qq=}} (covering stones). Surrounding the tumulus is a water-filled moat, which was extensively remodeled during the {{ill|Bunkyū era|ja|文久|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bunkyū era|qq=}} restoration. Originally, the surrounding area was agricultural land. Some theories suggest that the restoration converted the tumulus from a double circular mound to its current keyhole shape.Theory proposed by {{ill|Kōichi Mori|ja|森浩一|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōichi Mori|qq=}} The tumulus is part of a group of Kofuns, including {{ill|Hirata Iwaya Kofun|ja|平田岩屋古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirata Iwaya Kofun|qq=}}, {{ill|Oni no Manaita and Oni no Senkan|ja|鬼の俎・鬼の雪隠|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oni no Manaita and Oni no Senkan|qq=}}, and {{ill|Noguchi Royal Tomb|ja|野口王墓|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Noguchi Royal Tomb|qq=}} (the joint tomb of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|qq=}} and {{ill|Empress Jitō|ja|持統天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Empress Jitō|qq=}}), all situated along the southern slope of the same hill. == built == While believed to have been built during the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofun period|qq=}}, the exact construction date is uncertain due to the lack of archaeological excavations. == Interred Individual == Although designated by the Imperial Household Agency as the tomb of {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Kinmei|qq=}}, many scholars suggest that the nearby {{ill|Mise Maruyama Kofun|ja|見瀬丸山古墳|1=Maruyama Kofun (Kashihara)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mise Maruyama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Maruyama Kofun (Kashihara)|qq=}}, located approximately 800 meters northwest and the largest tumulus in Nara Prefecture, might be a more likely candidate. Other theories propose that {{ill|Soga no Iname|ja|蘇我稲目|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Soga no Iname|qq=}} may be the interred individual. == Saruishi Statues == Adjacent to the {{ill|Tomb of Princess Kibi|ja|吉備姫王墓|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomb of Princess Kibi|qq=}}, stone statues known as {{ill|Saruishi|ja|猿石|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saruishi|qq=}} ("monkey stones") are placed nearby. These statues were excavated during the Edo period from a field just south of Umeyama Kofun and relocated to their current location in the early {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治時代|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji period|qq=}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Kanazuka Kofun|ja|カナヅカ古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanazuka Kofun|qq=}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Umeyama Kofun}} * [https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/guide/029/index.html Hinokuma Sakaai no misasagi] - Imperial Household Agency {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|梅山古墳|version=103253627|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Umeyama Kofun]] [[Category:Ancient tumuli in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Asuka, Nara]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Imperial mausolea in Japan]] [[Category:Emperor Kinmei]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[sn:Umeyama Kofun]]}} [[sn:Umeyama Kofun]] [[ja:梅山古墳]] [[en:Umeyama Kofun]] [[sn:meyama Kofun]]

Some theories suggest that the restoration converted the tumulus from a double circular mound to its current keyhole shape.Theory proposed by {{ill|Kōichi Mori|ja|森浩一|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōichi Mori|qq=}} The tumulus is part of a group of Kofuns, including {{ill|Hirata Iwaya Kofun|ja|平田岩屋古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirata Iwaya Kofun|qq=}}, {{ill|Oni no Manaita and Oni no Senkan|ja|鬼の俎・鬼の雪隠|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oni no Manaita and Oni no Senkan|qq=}}, and {{ill|Noguchi Royal Tomb|ja|野口王墓|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Noguchi Royal Tomb|qq=}} (the joint tomb of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|qq=}} and {{ill|Empress Jitō|ja|持統天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Empress Jitō|qq=}}), all situated along the southern slope of the same hill. == built == While believed to have been built during the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofun period|qq=}}, the exact construction date is uncertain due to the lack of archaeological excavations. == Interred Individual == Although designated by the Imperial Household Agency as the tomb of {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Kinmei|qq=}}, many scholars suggest that the nearby {{ill|Mise Maruyama Kofun|ja|見瀬丸山古墳|1=Maruyama Kofun (Kashihara)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mise Maruyama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Maruyama Kofun (Kashihara)|qq=}}, located approximately 800 meters northwest and the largest tumulus in Nara Prefecture, might be a more likely candidate. Other theories propose that {{ill|Soga no Iname|ja|蘇我稲目|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Soga no Iname|qq=}} may be the interred individual. == Saruishi Statues == Adjacent to the {{ill|Tomb of Princess Kibi|ja|吉備姫王墓|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomb of Princess Kibi|qq=}}, stone statues known as {{ill|Saruishi|ja|猿石|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saruishi|qq=}} ("monkey stones") are placed nearby. These statues were excavated during the Edo period from a field just south of Umeyama Kofun and relocated to their current location in the early {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治|lt=Meiji period|1=Meiji era|2=ja|3=明治|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji period|qq=}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Kanazuka Kofun|ja|カナヅカ古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanazuka Kofun|qq=}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Umeyama Kofun}} * [https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/guide/029/index.html Hinokuma Sakaai no misasagi] - Imperial Household Agency {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|梅山古墳|version=103253627|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Umeyama Kofun]] [[Category:Ancient tumuli in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Asuka, Nara]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Imperial mausolea in Japan]] [[Category:Emperor Kinmei]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[sn:Umeyama Kofun]]}} [[sn:Umeyama Kofun]] [[ja:梅山古墳]] [[en:Umeyama Kofun]] [[sn:meyama Kofun]]

revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'Burial Facility' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Umeyama Kofun | image = [[File:Umeyama Kofun, zenkei-2.jpg|280px]]
Kofun and moat
(Rear circular mound on the right, front rectangular mound in the left background) | location = {{ill|Asuka, Nara|ja|明日香村|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asuka, Nara|qq=}}, Nara Prefecture | coordinates = {{Coord|34|28|7.38|N|135|48|2.80|E|region:JP-29_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Keyhole-shaped tumulus|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpokoenfun|qq=}} | area = Total length: 140m | Burial Facility = Unknown | built = Late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofun period|qq=}} | Interred_individual = ({{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Household Agency|qq=}} designation) {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Kinmei|qq=}} | excavations = Unknown | Mausoleum = Designated by the Imperial Household Agency as "Hinokuma Sakaai no misasagi" (檜隈坂合陵) | map = {{location map|Japan Nara|lat_deg=34|lat_min=28|lat_sec=7.38|lon_deg=135|lon_min=48|lon_sec=2.8|label=Umeyama Kofun|mark=前方後円墳-西.svg|marksize=23|caption=location in Nara Prefecture|position=bottom|width=200|float=center|border=none}} }} [[File:Umeyama kohun aerial.jpg|thumb|220px|Bottom left: Kibi-hime Royal Tomb and Saruishi statues ({{National Land Aerial Photography}})]] [[File:Umeyama Kofun, haisho.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Mausoleum of {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇}]]] '''Umeyama Kofun''' (梅山古墳) is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/keyhole-shaped tumulus|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpokoenfun|qq=}} located in {{ill|Asuka, Nara|ja|明日香村|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asuka, Nara|qq=}}, Nara Prefecture. The actual interred individual is unknown, but it has been designated by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Household Agency|qq=}} as the "Hinokuma Sakaai no misasagi" (檜隈坂合陵), attributed to {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Kinmei|qq=}}. == Overview == The tumulus features a keyhole shape, with the front mound oriented to the west along an east-west axis. Measuring approximately 140 meters in total length and 72 meters in diameter for the rear mound, it is the largest tumulus in Asuka Village. The mound is notable for its extensive use of {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/fukiishi|qq=}} (covering stones). Surrounding the tumulus is a water-filled moat, which was extensively remodeled during the {{ill|Bunkyū era|ja|文久|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bunkyū era|qq=}} restoration. Originally, the surrounding area was agricultural land. Some theories suggest that the restoration converted the tumulus from a double circular mound to its current keyhole shape.Theory proposed by {{ill|Kōichi Mori|ja|森浩一|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōichi Mori|qq=}} The tumulus is part of a group of Kofuns, including {{ill|Kanazuka Kofun|lt=Kanazuka Kofun|lt=Hirata Iwaya Kofun|ja|平田岩屋古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirata Iwaya Kofun|qq=}}, {{ill|Oni no Manaita and Oni no Senkan|ja|鬼の俎・鬼の雪隠|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oni no Manaita and Oni no Senkan|qq=}}, and {{ill|Noguchi Royal Tomb|ja|野口王墓|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Noguchi Royal Tomb|qq=}} (the joint tomb of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|qq=}} and {{ill|Empress Jitō|ja|持統天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Empress Jitō|qq=}}), all situated along the southern slope of the same hill. == built == While believed to have been built during the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofun period|qq=}}, the exact construction date is uncertain due to the lack of archaeological excavations. == Interred Individual == Although designated by the Imperial Household Agency as the tomb of {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Kinmei|qq=}}, many scholars suggest that the nearby {{ill|Mise Maruyama Kofun|ja|見瀬丸山古墳|1=Maruyama Kofun (Kashihara)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mise Maruyama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Maruyama Kofun (Kashihara)|qq=}}, located approximately 800 meters northwest and the largest tumulus in Nara Prefecture, might be a more likely candidate. Other theories propose that {{ill|Soga no Iname|ja|蘇我稲目|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Soga no Iname|qq=}} may be the interred individual. == Saruishi Statues == Adjacent to the {{ill|Tomb of Princess Kibi|ja|吉備姫王墓|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomb of Princess Kibi|qq=}}, stone statues known as {{ill|Saruishi|ja|猿石|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saruishi|qq=}} ("monkey stones") are placed nearby. These statues were excavated during the Edo period from a field just south of Umeyama Kofun and relocated to their current location in the early {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治|lt=Meiji period|1=Meiji era|2=ja|3=明治|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji period|qq=}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Kanazuka Kofun|ja|カナヅカ古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanazuka Kofun|qq=}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Umeyama Kofun}} * [https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/guide/029/index.html Hinokuma Sakaai no misasagi] - Imperial Household Agency {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|梅山古墳|version=103253627|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Umeyama Kofun]] [[Category:Ancient tumuli in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Asuka, Nara]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Imperial mausolea in Japan]] [[Category:Emperor Kinmei]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[sn:Umeyama Kofun]]}} [[sn:Umeyama Kofun]] [[ja:梅山古墳]] [[en:Umeyama Kofun]] [[sn:meyama Kofun]]

Nara|lat_deg=34|lat_min=28|lat_sec=7.38|lon_deg=135|lon_min=48|lon_sec=2.8|label=Umeyama Kofun|mark=前方後円墳-西.svg|marksize=23|caption=location in Nara Prefecture|position=bottom|width=200|float=center|border=none}} }} [[File:Umeyama kohun aerial.jpg|thumb|220px|Bottom left: Kibi-hime Royal Tomb and Saruishi statues ({{National Land Aerial Photography}})]] [[File:Umeyama Kofun, haisho.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Mausoleum of {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇}]]] '''Umeyama Kofun''' (梅山古墳) is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/keyhole-shaped tumulus|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpokoenfun|qq=}} located in {{ill|Asuka, Nara|ja|明日香村|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asuka, Nara|qq=}}, Nara Prefecture. The actual interred individual is unknown, but it has been designated by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Household Agency|qq=}} as the "Hinokuma Sakaai no misasagi" (檜隈坂合陵), attributed to {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Kinmei|qq=}}. == Overview == The tumulus features a keyhole shape, with the front mound oriented to the west along an east-west axis. Measuring approximately 140 meters in total length and 72 meters in diameter for the rear mound, it is the largest tumulus in Asuka Village. The mound is notable for its extensive use of {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/fukiishi|qq=}} (covering stones). Surrounding the tumulus is a water-filled moat, which was extensively remodeled during the {{ill|Bunkyū era|ja|文久|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bunkyū era|qq=}} restoration. Originally, the surrounding area was agricultural land. Some theories suggest that the restoration converted the tumulus from a double circular mound to its current keyhole shape.Theory proposed by {{ill|Kōichi Mori|ja|森浩一|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōichi Mori|qq=}} The tumulus is part of a group of Kofuns, including {{ill|Kanazuka Kofun|lt=Kanazuka Kofun|lt=Hirata Iwaya Kofun|ja|平田岩屋古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirata Iwaya Kofun|qq=}}, {{ill|Oni no Manaita and Oni no Senkan|ja|鬼の俎・鬼の雪隠|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oni no Manaita and Oni no Senkan|qq=}}, and {{ill|Noguchi-ōsaka Kofun|lt=Noguchi-ōsaka Kofun|lt=Noguchi Royal Tomb|ja|野口王墓|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Noguchi Royal Tomb|qq=}} (the joint tomb of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|qq=}} and {{ill|Empress Jitō|ja|持統天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Empress Jitō|qq=}}), all situated along the southern slope of the same hill. == built == While believed to have been built during the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofun period|qq=}}, the exact construction date is uncertain due to the lack of archaeological excavations. == Interred Individual == Although designated by the Imperial Household Agency as the tomb of {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Kinmei|qq=}}, many scholars suggest that the nearby {{ill|Mise Maruyama Kofun|ja|見瀬丸山古墳|1=Maruyama Kofun (Kashihara)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mise Maruyama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Maruyama Kofun (Kashihara)|qq=}}, located approximately 800 meters northwest and the largest tumulus in Nara Prefecture, might be a more likely candidate. Other theories propose that {{ill|Soga no Iname|ja|蘇我稲目|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Soga no Iname|qq=}} may be the interred individual. == Saruishi Statues == Adjacent to the {{ill|Tomb of Princess Kibi|ja|吉備姫王墓|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomb of Princess Kibi|qq=}}, stone statues known as {{ill|Saruishi|ja|猿石|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saruishi|qq=}} ("monkey stones") are placed nearby. These statues were excavated during the Edo period from a field just south of Umeyama Kofun and relocated to their current location in the early {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治|lt=Meiji period|1=Meiji era|2=ja|3=明治|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji period|qq=}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Kanazuka Kofun|ja|カナヅカ古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanazuka Kofun|qq=}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Umeyama Kofun}} * [https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/guide/029/index.html Hinokuma Sakaai no misasagi] - Imperial Household Agency {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|梅山古墳|version=103253627|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Ancient tumuli in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Asuka, Nara]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Imperial mausolea in Japan]] [[Category:Emperor Kinmei]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[sn:Umeyama Kofun]]}} [[sn:Umeyama Kofun]] [[ja:梅山古墳]] [[en:Umeyama Kofun]] [[sn:meyama Kofun]]

The actual interred individual is unknown, but it has been designated by the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] as the "Hinokuma Sakaai no misasagi" (檜隈坂合陵), attributed to [[:en:Emperor Kinmei|Emperor Kinmei]]. == Overview == The tumulus features a keyhole shape, with the front mound oriented to the west along an east-west axis. Measuring approximately 140 meters in total length and 72 meters in diameter for the rear mound, it is the largest tumulus in Asuka Village. The mound is notable for its extensive use of [[:en:Fukiishi|fukiishi]] (covering stones). These statues were excavated during the Edo period from a field just south of Umeyama Kofun and relocated to their current location in the early [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji period]]. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Kanazuka Kofun|ja|カナヅカ古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanazuka Kofun|qq=}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Umeyama Kofun}} * [https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/guide/029/index.html Hinokuma Sakaai no misasagi] - Imperial Household Agency {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|梅山古墳|version=103253627|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Ancient tumuli in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Asuka, Nara]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Imperial mausolea in Japan]] [[Category:Emperor Kinmei]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[sn:Umeyama Kofun]]}} [[sn:Umeyama Kofun]] [[ja:梅山古墳]] [[en:Umeyama Kofun]] [[sn:meyama Kofun]]
