Kofun and moat
(Rear circular mound on the right, front rectangular mound in the left background) | location = [[:en:Asuka, Nara|Asuka, Nara]], Nara Prefecture | coordinates = {{Coord|34|28|7.38|N|135|48|2.80|E|region:JP-29_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | type = [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] | area = Total length: 140m | Burial Facility = Unknown | built = Late [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]] | Interred_individual = ([[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] designation) [[:en:Emperor Kinmei|Emperor Kinmei]] | excavations = Unknown | Mausoleum = Designated by the Imperial Household Agency as "Hinokuma Sakaai no misasagi" (檜隈坂合陵) | map = {{location map|Japan Nara|lat_deg=34|lat_min=28|lat_sec=7.38|lon_deg=135|lon_min=48|lon_sec=2.8|label=Umeyama Kofun|mark=前方後円墳-西.svg|marksize=23|caption=location in Nara Prefecture|position=bottom|width=200|float=center|border=none}} }} [[File:Umeyama kohun aerial.jpg|thumb|220px|Bottom left: Kibi-hime Royal Tomb and Saruishi statues ({{National Land Aerial Photography}})]] [[File:Umeyama Kofun, haisho.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Mausoleum of [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] located in [[:en:Asuka, Nara|Asuka, Nara]], Nara Prefecture. The actual interred individual is unknown, but it has been designated by the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] as the "Hinokuma Sakaai no misasagi" (檜隈坂合陵), attributed to [[:en:Emperor Kinmei|Emperor Kinmei]]. == Overview == The tumulus features a keyhole shape, with the front mound oriented to the west along an east-west axis. Measuring approximately 140 meters in total length and 72 meters in diameter for the rear mound, it is the largest tumulus in Asuka Village. The mound is notable for its extensive use of [[:en:Fukiishi|fukiishi]] (covering stones). Surrounding the tumulus is a water-filled moat, which was extensively remodeled during the [[:en:Bunkyū|Bunkyū era]] restoration. Originally, the surrounding area was agricultural land. Some theories suggest that the restoration converted the tumulus from a double circular mound to its current keyhole shape.Theory proposed by {{ill|Kōichi Mori|ja|森浩一|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōichi Mori|qq=}} The tumulus is part of a group of Kofuns, including {{ill|Kanazuka Kofun|lt=Kanazuka Kofun|lt=Hirata Iwaya Kofun|ja|平田岩屋古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirata Iwaya Kofun|qq=}}, {{ill|Oni-no-Manaita and Oni-no-Secchin|lt=Oni-no-Manaita and Oni-no-Secchin|lt=Oni no Manaita and Oni no Senkan|ja|鬼の俎・鬼の雪隠|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oni no Manaita and Oni no Senkan|qq=}}, and {{ill|Noguchi-ōsaka Kofun|lt=Noguchi-ōsaka Kofun|lt=Noguchi Royal Tomb|ja|野口王墓|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Noguchi Royal Tomb|qq=}} (the joint tomb of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|qq=}} and [[:en:Empress Jitō|Empress Jitō]]), all situated along the southern slope of the same hill. == built == While believed to have been built during the late [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]], the exact construction date is uncertain due to the lack of archaeological excavations. == Interred Individual == Although designated by the Imperial Household Agency as the tomb of [[:en:Emperor Kinmei|Emperor Kinmei]], many scholars suggest that the nearby {{ill|Mise Maruyama Kofun|ja|見瀬丸山古墳|1=Maruyama Kofun (Kashihara)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mise Maruyama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Maruyama Kofun (Kashihara)|qq=}}, located approximately 800 meters northwest and the largest tumulus in Nara Prefecture, might be a more likely candidate. Other theories propose that [[:en:Soga no Iname|Soga no Iname]] may be the interred individual. == Saruishi Statues == Adjacent to the {{ill|Tomb of Princess Kibi|ja|吉備姫王墓|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomb of Princess Kibi|qq=}}, stone statues known as {{ill|Saruishi|ja|猿石|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saruishi|qq=}} ("monkey stones") are placed nearby. These statues were excavated during the Edo period from a field just south of Umeyama Kofun and relocated to their current location in the early [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji period]]. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Kanazuka Kofun|ja|カナヅカ古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanazuka Kofun|qq=}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Umeyama Kofun}} * [https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/guide/029/index.html Hinokuma Sakaai no misasagi] - Imperial Household Agency {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|梅山古墳|version=103253627|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Ancient tumuli in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Asuka, Nara]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Imperial mausolea in Japan]] [[Category:Emperor Kinmei]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[sn:Umeyama Kofun]]}} [[sn:Umeyama Kofun]] [[ja:梅山古墳]] [[en:Umeyama Kofun]] [[sn:meyama Kofun]] [[Category:奈良県の古墳]]

Some theories suggest that the restoration converted the tumulus from a double circular mound to its current keyhole shape.Theory proposed by {{ill|Kōichi Mori|ja|森浩一|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōichi Mori|qq=}} The tumulus is part of a group of Kofuns, including {{ill|Kanazuka Kofun|lt=Kanazuka Kofun|lt=Hirata Iwaya Kofun|ja|平田岩屋古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirata Iwaya Kofun|qq=}}, {{ill|Oni-no-Manaita and Oni-no-Secchin|lt=Oni-no-Manaita and Oni-no-Secchin|lt=Oni no Manaita and Oni no Senkan|ja|鬼の俎・鬼の雪隠|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oni no Manaita and Oni no Senkan|qq=}}, and {{ill|Noguchi-ōsaka Kofun|lt=Noguchi-ōsaka Kofun|lt=Noguchi Royal Tomb|ja|野口王墓|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Noguchi Royal Tomb|qq=}} (the joint tomb of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|qq=}} and [[:en:Empress Jitō|Empress Jitō]]), all situated along the southern slope of the same hill. == built == While believed to have been built during the late [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]], the exact construction date is uncertain due to the lack of archaeological excavations. == Interred Individual == Although designated by the Imperial Household Agency as the tomb of [[:en:Emperor Kinmei|Emperor Kinmei]], many scholars suggest that the nearby {{ill|Mise Maruyama Kofun|ja|見瀬丸山古墳|1=Maruyama Kofun (Kashihara)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mise Maruyama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Maruyama Kofun (Kashihara)|qq=}}, located approximately 800 meters northwest and the largest tumulus in Nara Prefecture, might be a more likely candidate. Other theories propose that [[:en:Soga no Iname|Soga no Iname]] may be the interred individual. == Saruishi Statues == Adjacent to the {{ill|Tomb of Princess Kibi|ja|吉備姫王墓|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomb of Princess Kibi|qq=}}, stone statues known as {{ill|Saruishi|ja|猿石|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saruishi|qq=}} ("monkey stones") are placed nearby. These statues were excavated during the Edo period from a field just south of Umeyama Kofun and relocated to their current location in the early [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji period]]. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Kanazuka Kofun|ja|カナヅカ古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanazuka Kofun|qq=}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Umeyama Kofun}} * [https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/guide/029/index.html Hinokuma Sakaai no misasagi] - Imperial Household Agency {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|梅山古墳|version=103253627|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Ancient tumuli in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Asuka, Nara]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Imperial mausolea in Japan]] [[Category:Emperor Kinmei]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[sn:Umeyama Kofun]]}} [[sn:Umeyama Kofun]] [[ja:梅山古墳]] [[en:Umeyama Kofun]] [[sn:meyama Kofun]] [[Category:奈良県の古墳]] [[Category:Japanese imperial tombs]] [[Category:明日香村の歴史]] [[Category:欽明天皇]]

These statues were excavated during the Edo period from a field just south of Umeyama Kofun and relocated to their current location in the early [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji period]]. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Kanazuka Kofun|ja|カナヅカ古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanazuka Kofun|qq=}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Umeyama Kofun}} * [https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/guide/029/index.html Hinokuma Sakaai no misasagi] - Imperial Household Agency {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|梅山古墳|version=103253627|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Ancient tumuli in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Asuka, Nara]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Imperial mausolea in Japan]] [[Category:Emperor Kinmei]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[sn:Umeyama Kofun]]}} [[sn:Umeyama Kofun]] [[ja:梅山古墳]] [[sn:meyama Kofun]] [[Category:Kofun of Nara prefecture]] [[Category:Japanese imperial tombs]] [[Category:明日香村の歴史]] [[Category:欽明天皇]] {{Commons category|Umeyama Kofun}}

{{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'Burial Facility' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Umeyama Kofun | image = [[File:Umeyama Kofun, zenkei-2.jpg|280px]]
Kofun and moat
(Rear circular mound on the right, front rectangular mound in the left background) | location = [[:en:Asuka, Nara|Asuka, Nara]], Nara Prefecture | coordinates = {{Coord|34|28|7.38|N|135|48|2.80|E|region:JP-29_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | type = [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] | area = Total length: 140m | Burial Facility = Unknown | built = Late [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]] | Interred_individual = ([[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] designation) [[:en:Emperor Kinmei|Emperor Kinmei]] | excavations = Unknown | Mausoleum = Designated by the Imperial Household Agency as "Hinokuma Sakaai no misasagi" (檜隈坂合陵) | map = {{location map|Japan Nara|lat_deg=34|lat_min=28|lat_sec=7.38|lon_deg=135|lon_min=48|lon_sec=2.8|label=Umeyama Kofun|mark=前方後円墳-西.svg|marksize=23|caption=location in Nara Prefecture|position=bottom|width=200|float=center|border=none}} }} [[File:Umeyama kohun aerial.jpg|thumb|220px|Bottom left: Kibi-hime Royal Tomb and Saruishi statues ({{National Land Aerial Photography}})]] [[File:Umeyama Kofun, haisho.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Mausoleum of [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] located in [[:en:Asuka, Nara|Asuka, Nara]], Nara Prefecture. The actual interred individual is unknown, but it has been designated by the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] as the "Hinokuma Sakaai no misasagi" (檜隈坂合陵), attributed to [[:en:Emperor Kinmei|Emperor Kinmei]]. == Overview == The tumulus features a keyhole shape, with the front mound oriented to the west along an east-west axis. Measuring approximately 140 meters in total length and 72 meters in diameter for the rear mound, it is the largest tumulus in Asuka Village. The mound is notable for its extensive use of [[:en:Fukiishi|fukiishi]] (covering stones). Surrounding the tumulus is a water-filled moat, which was extensively remodeled during the [[:en:Bunkyū|Bunkyū era]] restoration. Originally, the surrounding area was agricultural land. Some theories suggest that the restoration converted the tumulus from a double circular mound to its current keyhole shape.Theory proposed by {{ill|Kōichi Mori|ja|森浩一|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōichi Mori|qq=}} The tumulus is part of a group of Kofuns, including {{ill|Kanazuka Kofun|lt=Kanazuka Kofun|lt=Hirata Iwaya Kofun|ja|平田岩屋古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirata Iwaya Kofun|qq=}}, {{ill|Oni-no-Manaita and Oni-no-Secchin|lt=Oni-no-Manaita and Oni-no-Secchin|lt=Oni no Manaita and Oni no Senkan|ja|鬼の俎・鬼の雪隠|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oni no Manaita and Oni no Senkan|qq=}}, and {{ill|Noguchi-ōsaka Kofun|lt=Noguchi-ōsaka Kofun|lt=Noguchi Royal Tomb|ja|野口王墓|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Noguchi Royal Tomb|qq=}} (the joint tomb of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|qq=}} and [[:en:Empress Jitō|Empress Jitō]]), all situated along the southern slope of the same hill. == built == While believed to have been built during the late [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]], the exact construction date is uncertain due to the lack of archaeological excavations. == Interred Individual == Although designated by the Imperial Household Agency as the tomb of [[:en:Emperor Kinmei|Emperor Kinmei]], many scholars suggest that the nearby {{ill|Mise Maruyama Kofun|ja|見瀬丸山古墳|1=Maruyama Kofun (Kashihara)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mise Maruyama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Maruyama Kofun (Kashihara)|qq=}}, located approximately 800 meters northwest and the largest tumulus in Nara Prefecture, might be a more likely candidate. Other theories propose that [[:en:Soga no Iname|Soga no Iname]] may be the interred individual. == Saruishi Statues == Adjacent to the {{ill|Tomb of Princess Kibi|ja|吉備姫王墓|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomb of Princess Kibi|qq=}}, stone statues known as {{ill|Saruishi|ja|猿石|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saruishi|qq=}} ("monkey stones") are placed nearby. These statues were excavated during the Edo period from a field just south of Umeyama Kofun and relocated to their current location in the early [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji period]]. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Kanazuka Kofun|ja|カナヅカ古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanazuka Kofun|qq=}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Umeyama Kofun}} * [https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/guide/029/index.html Hinokuma Sakaai no misasagi] - Imperial Household Agency {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|梅山古墳|version=103253627|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Ancient tumuli in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Asuka, Nara]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Imperial mausolea in Japan]] [[Category:Emperor Kinmei]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[sn:Umeyama Kofun]]}} [[sn:Umeyama Kofun]] [[ja:梅山古墳]] [[sn:meyama Kofun]] [[Category:Kofun of Nara prefecture]] [[Category:Japanese imperial tombs]] [[Category:History of Asuka, Nara�]] [[Category:欽明天皇]] {{Commons category|Umeyama Kofun}}

revision.comment: Bot: remove deleted category Ancient tumuli in Nara Prefecture
revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'Burial Facility' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Umeyama Kofun | image = [[File:Umeyama Kofun, zenkei-2.jpg|280px]]
Kofun and moat
(Rear circular mound on the right, front rectangular mound in the left background) | location = [[:en:Asuka, Nara|Asuka, Nara]], Nara Prefecture | coordinates = {{Coord|34|28|7.38|N|135|48|2.80|E|region:JP-29_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | type = [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] | area = Total length: 140m | Burial Facility = Unknown | built = Late [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]] | Interred_individual = ([[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] designation) [[:en:Emperor Kinmei|Emperor Kinmei]] | excavations = Unknown | Mausoleum = Designated by the Imperial Household Agency as "Hinokuma Sakaai no misasagi" (檜隈坂合陵) | map = {{location map|Japan Nara|lat_deg=34|lat_min=28|lat_sec=7.38|lon_deg=135|lon_min=48|lon_sec=2.8|label=Umeyama Kofun|mark=前方後円墳-西.svg|marksize=23|caption=location in Nara Prefecture|position=bottom|width=200|float=center|border=none}} }} [[File:Umeyama kohun aerial.jpg|thumb|220px|Bottom left: Kibi-hime Royal Tomb and Saruishi statues ({{National Land Aerial Photography}})]] [[File:Umeyama Kofun, haisho.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Mausoleum of [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] located in [[:en:Asuka, Nara|Asuka, Nara]], Nara Prefecture. The actual interred individual is unknown, but it has been designated by the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] as the "Hinokuma Sakaai no misasagi" (檜隈坂合陵), attributed to [[:en:Emperor Kinmei|Emperor Kinmei]]. == Overview == The tumulus features a keyhole shape, with the front mound oriented to the west along an east-west axis. Measuring approximately 140 meters in total length and 72 meters in diameter for the rear mound, it is the largest tumulus in Asuka Village. The mound is notable for its extensive use of [[:en:Fukiishi|fukiishi]] (covering stones). Surrounding the tumulus is a water-filled moat, which was extensively remodeled during the [[:en:Bunkyū|Bunkyū era]] restoration. Originally, the surrounding area was agricultural land. Some theories suggest that the restoration converted the tumulus from a double circular mound to its current keyhole shape.Theory proposed by {{ill|Kōichi Mori|ja|森浩一|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōichi Mori|qq=}} The tumulus is part of a group of Kofuns, including {{ill|Kanazuka Kofun|lt=Kanazuka Kofun|lt=Hirata Iwaya Kofun|ja|平田岩屋古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirata Iwaya Kofun|qq=}}, {{ill|Oni-no-Manaita and Oni-no-Secchin|lt=Oni-no-Manaita and Oni-no-Secchin|lt=Oni no Manaita and Oni no Senkan|ja|鬼の俎・鬼の雪隠|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oni no Manaita and Oni no Senkan|qq=}}, and {{ill|Noguchi-ōsaka Kofun|lt=Noguchi-ōsaka Kofun|lt=Noguchi Royal Tomb|ja|野口王墓|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Noguchi Royal Tomb|qq=}} (the joint tomb of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|qq=}} and [[:en:Empress Jitō|Empress Jitō]]), all situated along the southern slope of the same hill. == built == While believed to have been built during the late [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]], the exact construction date is uncertain due to the lack of archaeological excavations. == Interred Individual == Although designated by the Imperial Household Agency as the tomb of [[:en:Emperor Kinmei|Emperor Kinmei]], many scholars suggest that the nearby {{ill|Mise Maruyama Kofun|ja|見瀬丸山古墳|1=Maruyama Kofun (Kashihara)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mise Maruyama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Maruyama Kofun (Kashihara)|qq=}}, located approximately 800 meters northwest and the largest tumulus in Nara Prefecture, might be a more likely candidate. Other theories propose that [[:en:Soga no Iname|Soga no Iname]] may be the interred individual. == Saruishi Statues == Adjacent to the {{ill|Tomb of Princess Kibi|ja|吉備姫王墓|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomb of Princess Kibi|qq=}}, stone statues known as {{ill|Saruishi|ja|猿石|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saruishi|qq=}} ("monkey stones") are placed nearby. These statues were excavated during the Edo period from a field just south of Umeyama Kofun and relocated to their current location in the early [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji period]]. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Kanazuka Kofun|ja|カナヅカ古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanazuka Kofun|qq=}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Umeyama Kofun}} * [https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/guide/029/index.html Hinokuma Sakaai no misasagi] - Imperial Household Agency {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|梅山古墳|version=103253627|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:History of Asuka, Nara]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Imperial mausolea in Japan]] [[Category:Emperor Kinmei]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[sn:Umeyama Kofun]]}} [[sn:Umeyama Kofun]] [[ja:梅山古墳]] [[sn:meyama Kofun]] [[Category:Kofun of Nara prefecture]] [[Category:Japanese imperial tombs]] [[Category:History of Asuka, Nara]] [[Category:Emperor Kinmei]] {{Commons category|Umeyama Kofun}}

{{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'Burial Facility' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Umeyama Kofun | image = [[File:Umeyama Kofun, zenkei-2.jpg|280px]]
Kofun and moat
(Rear circular mound on the right, front rectangular mound in the left background) | location = [[:en:Asuka, Nara|Asuka, Nara]], Nara Prefecture | coordinates = {{Coord|34|28|7.38|N|135|48|2.80|E|region:JP-29_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | type = [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] | area = Total length: 140m | Burial Facility = Unknown | built = Late [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]] | Interred_individual = ([[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] designation) [[:en:Emperor Kinmei|Emperor Kinmei]] | excavations = Unknown | Mausoleum = Designated by the Imperial Household Agency as "Hinokuma Sakaai no misasagi" (檜隈坂合陵) | map = {{location map|Japan Nara|lat_deg=34|lat_min=28|lat_sec=7.38|lon_deg=135|lon_min=48|lon_sec=2.8|label=Umeyama Kofun|mark=前方後円墳-西.svg|marksize=23|caption=location in Nara Prefecture|position=bottom|width=200|float=center|border=none}} }} [[File:Umeyama kohun aerial.jpg|thumb|220px|Bottom left: Kibi-hime Royal Tomb and Saruishi statues ({{National Land Aerial Photography}})]] [[File:Umeyama Kofun, haisho.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Mausoleum of [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] located in [[:en:Asuka, Nara|Asuka, Nara]], Nara Prefecture. The actual interred individual is unknown, but it has been designated by the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] as the "Hinokuma Sakaai no misasagi" (檜隈坂合陵), attributed to [[:en:Emperor Kinmei|Emperor Kinmei]]. == Overview == The tumulus features a keyhole shape, with the front mound oriented to the west along an east-west axis. Measuring approximately 140 meters in total length and 72 meters in diameter for the rear mound, it is the largest tumulus in Asuka Village. The mound is notable for its extensive use of [[:en:Fukiishi|fukiishi]] (covering stones). Surrounding the tumulus is a water-filled moat, which was extensively remodeled during the [[:en:Bunkyū|Bunkyū era]] restoration. Originally, the surrounding area was agricultural land. Some theories suggest that the restoration converted the tumulus from a double circular mound to its current keyhole shape.Theory proposed by {{ill|Kōichi Mori|ja|森浩一|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōichi Mori|qq=}} The tumulus is part of a group of Kofuns, including {{ill|Kanazuka Kofun|lt=Kanazuka Kofun|lt=Hirata Iwaya Kofun|ja|平田岩屋古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirata Iwaya Kofun|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to カナヅカ古墳}}, {{ill|Oni-no-Manaita and Oni-no-Secchin|lt=Oni-no-Manaita and Oni-no-Secchin|lt=Oni no Manaita and Oni no Senkan|ja|鬼の俎・鬼の雪隠|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oni no Manaita and Oni no Senkan|qq=}}, and {{ill|Noguchi-ōsaka Kofun|lt=Noguchi-ōsaka Kofun|lt=Noguchi Royal Tomb|ja|野口王墓|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Noguchi Royal Tomb|qq=}} (the joint tomb of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and [[:en:Empress Jitō|Empress Jitō]]), all situated along the southern slope of the same hill. == built == While believed to have been built during the late [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]], the exact construction date is uncertain due to the lack of archaeological excavations. == Interred Individual == Although designated by the Imperial Household Agency as the tomb of [[:en:Emperor Kinmei|Emperor Kinmei]], many scholars suggest that the nearby {{ill|Mise Maruyama Kofun|ja|見瀬丸山古墳|1=Maruyama Kofun (Kashihara)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mise Maruyama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Maruyama Kofun (Kashihara)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 丸山古墳 (橿原市)}}, located approximately 800 meters northwest and the largest tumulus in Nara Prefecture, might be a more likely candidate. Other theories propose that [[:en:Soga no Iname|Soga no Iname]] may be the interred individual. == Saruishi Statues == Adjacent to the {{ill|Tomb of Princess Kibi|ja|吉備姫王墓|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomb of Princess Kibi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, stone statues known as {{ill|Saruishi|ja|猿石|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saruishi|qq=}} ("monkey stones") are placed nearby. The mound is notable for its extensive use of [[:en:Fukiishi|fukiishi]] (covering stones). Surrounding the tumulus is a water-filled moat, which was extensively remodeled during the [[:en:Bunkyū|Bunkyū era]] restoration. Originally, the surrounding area was agricultural land. Some theories suggest that the restoration converted the tumulus from a double circular mound to its current keyhole shape.Theory proposed by {{ill|Kōichi Mori|ja|森浩一|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōichi Mori|qq=}} The tumulus is part of a group of Kofuns, including {{ill|Kanazuka Kofun|lt=Kanazuka Kofun|lt=Hirata Iwaya Kofun|ja|平田岩屋古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirata Iwaya Kofun|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to カナヅカ古墳}}, {{ill|Oni-no-Manaita and Oni-no-Secchin|lt=Oni-no-Manaita and Oni-no-Secchin|lt=Oni no Manaita and Oni no Senkan|ja|鬼の俎・鬼の雪隠|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oni no Manaita and Oni no Senkan|qq=}}, and {{ill|Noguchi-ōsaka Kofun|lt=Noguchi-ōsaka Kofun|lt=Noguchi Royal Tomb|ja|野口王墓|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Noguchi Royal Tomb|qq=}} (the joint tomb of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and [[:en:Empress Jitō|Empress Jitō]]), all situated along the southern slope of the same hill. == built == While believed to have been built during the late [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]], the exact construction date is uncertain due to the lack of archaeological excavations. == Interred Individual == Although designated by the Imperial Household Agency as the tomb of [[:en:Emperor Kinmei|Emperor Kinmei]], many scholars suggest that the nearby {{ill|Mise Maruyama Kofun|ja|見瀬丸山古墳|1=Maruyama Kofun (Kashihara)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mise Maruyama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Maruyama Kofun (Kashihara)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 丸山古墳 (橿原市)}}, located approximately 800 meters northwest and the largest tumulus in Nara Prefecture, might be a more likely candidate. Other theories propose that [[:en:Soga no Iname|Soga no Iname]] may be the interred individual. == Saruishi Statues == Adjacent to the {{ill|Tomb of Princess Kibi|ja|吉備姫王墓|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomb of Princess Kibi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, stone statues known as {{ill|Saruishi|ja|猿石|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saruishi|qq=}} ("monkey stones") are placed nearby. These statues were excavated during the Edo period from a field just south of Umeyama Kofun and relocated to their current location in the early [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji period]]. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Kanazuka Kofun|ja|カナヅカ古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanazuka Kofun|qq=}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Umeyama Kofun}} * [https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/guide/029/index.html Hinokuma Sakaai no misasagi] - Imperial Household Agency {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|梅山古墳|version=103253627|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:History of Asuka, Nara]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Emperor Kinmei]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[sn:Umeyama Kofun]]}} [[sn:Umeyama Kofun]] [[ja:梅山古墳]] [[sn:meyama Kofun]] [[Category:Kofun of Nara prefecture]] [[Category:Japanese imperial tombs]] [[Category:History of Asuka, Nara]] [[Category:Emperor Kinmei]] {{Commons category|Umeyama Kofun}}

(Rear circular mound on the right, front rectangular mound in the left background) | location = [[:en:Asuka, Nara|Asuka, Nara]], Nara Prefecture | coordinates = {{Coord|34|28|7.38|N|135|48|2.80|E|region:JP-29_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | type = [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] | area = Total length: 140m | Burial Facility = Unknown | built = Late [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]] | Interred_individual = ([[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] designation) [[:en:Emperor Kinmei|Emperor Kinmei]] | excavations = Unknown | Mausoleum = Designated by the Imperial Household Agency as "Hinokuma Sakaai no misasagi" (檜隈坂合陵) | map = {{location map|Japan Nara|lat_deg=34|lat_min=28|lat_sec=7.38|lon_deg=135|lon_min=48|lon_sec=2.8|label=Umeyama Kofun|mark=前方後円墳-西.svg|marksize=23|caption=location in Nara Prefecture|position=bottom|width=200|float=center|border=none}} }} [[File:Umeyama kohun aerial.jpg|thumb|220px|Bottom left: Kibi-hime Royal Tomb and Saruishi statues ({{National Land Aerial Photography}})]] [[File:Umeyama Kofun, haisho.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Mausoleum of [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] located in [[:en:Asuka, Nara|Asuka, Nara]], Nara Prefecture. The actual interred individual is unknown, but it has been designated by the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] as the "Hinokuma Sakaai no misasagi" (檜隈坂合陵), attributed to [[:en:Emperor Kinmei|Emperor Kinmei]]. == Overview == The tumulus features a keyhole shape, with the front mound oriented to the west along an east-west axis. Measuring approximately 140 meters in total length and 72 meters in diameter for the rear mound, it is the largest tumulus in Asuka Village. The mound is notable for its extensive use of [[:en:Fukiishi|fukiishi]] (covering stones). Surrounding the tumulus is a water-filled moat, which was extensively remodeled during the [[:en:Bunkyū|Bunkyū era]] restoration. Originally, the surrounding area was agricultural land. Some theories suggest that the restoration converted the tumulus from a double circular mound to its current keyhole shape.Theory proposed by {{ill|Kōichi Mori|ja|森浩一|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōichi Mori|qq=}} The tumulus is part of a group of Kofuns, including {{ill|Kanazuka Kofun|lt=Kanazuka Kofun|lt=Hirata Iwaya Kofun|ja|平田岩屋古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirata Iwaya Kofun|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to カナヅカ古墳}}, {{ill|Oni-no-Manaita and Oni-no-Secchin|lt=Oni-no-Manaita and Oni-no-Secchin|lt=Oni no Manaita and Oni no Senkan|ja|鬼の俎・鬼の雪隠|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oni no Manaita and Oni no Senkan|qq=}}, and {{ill|Noguchi-ōsaka Kofun|lt=Noguchi-ōsaka Kofun|lt=Noguchi Royal Tomb|ja|野口王墓|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Noguchi Royal Tomb|qq=}} (the joint tomb of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and [[:en:Empress Jitō|Empress Jitō]]), all situated along the southern slope of the same hill. == built == While believed to have been built during the late [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]], the exact construction date is uncertain due to the lack of archaeological excavations. == Interred Individual == Although designated by the Imperial Household Agency as the tomb of [[:en:Emperor Kinmei|Emperor Kinmei]], many scholars suggest that the nearby {{ill|Mise Maruyama Kofun|ja|見瀬丸山古墳|1=Maruyama Kofun (Kashihara)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mise Maruyama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Maruyama Kofun (Kashihara)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 丸山古墳 (橿原市)}}, located approximately 800 meters northwest and the largest tumulus in Nara Prefecture, might be a more likely candidate. Other theories propose that [[:en:Soga no Iname|Soga no Iname]] may be the interred individual. == Saruishi Statues == Adjacent to the {{ill|Tomb of Princess Kibi|ja|吉備姫王墓|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomb of Princess Kibi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, stone statues known as {{ill|Saruishi|ja|猿石|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saruishi|qq=}} ("monkey stones") are placed nearby. These statues were excavated during the Edo period from a field just south of Umeyama Kofun and relocated to their current location in the early [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji period]]. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Kanazuka Kofun|ja|カナヅカ古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanazuka Kofun|qq=}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Umeyama Kofun}} * [https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/guide/029/index.html Hinokuma Sakaai no misasagi] - Imperial Household Agency {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|梅山古墳|version=103253627|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:History of Asuka, Nara]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Emperor Kinmei]] [[sn:Umeyama Kofun]]}} [[sn:Umeyama Kofun]] [[ja:梅山古墳]] [[sn:meyama Kofun]] [[Category:Kofun of Nara prefecture]] [[Category:Japanese imperial tombs]] [[Category:History of Asuka, Nara]] [[Category:Emperor Kinmei]] {{Commons category|Umeyama Kofun}}

But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}}
{{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under  and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through  to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}}

{{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]]
| name = Umeyama Kofun
| image = File:Umeyama Kofun, zenkei-2.jpg
| location = {{ill|Asuka|qid=Q752397}}
| coordinates = {{Coord|34.468717|N|135.800778|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}
| type = [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]]
| area = Total length: 140m
| Burial Facility = Unknown
| built = Late [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]]
| Interred_individual = ([[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] designation) [[:en:Emperor Kinmei|Emperor Kinmei]]
| excavations = Unknown
| Mausoleum = Designated by the Imperial Household Agency as "Hinokuma Sakaai no misasagi" (檜隈坂合陵)
| map = {{location map|Japan Nara|lat_deg=34|lat_min=28|lat_sec=7.38|lon_deg=135|lon_min=48|lon_sec=2.8|label=Umeyama Kofun|mark=前方後円墳-西.svg|marksize=23|caption=location in Nara Prefecture|position=bottom|width=200|float=center|border=none}}
| architectural_styles = {{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|qid=Q11268718|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}}
| commemorates = {{ill|Kinmei|qid=Q329792}}
}}

[[File:Umeyama kohun aerial.jpg|thumb|220px|Bottom left: Kibi-hime Royal Tomb and Saruishi statues ({{National Land Aerial Photography}})]]
[[File:Umeyama Kofun, haisho.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Mausoleum of [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] located in [[:en:Asuka, Nara|Asuka, Nara]], Nara Prefecture.

The actual interred individual is unknown, but it has been designated by the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] as the "Hinokuma Sakaai no misasagi" (檜隈坂合陵), attributed to [[:en:Emperor Kinmei|Emperor Kinmei]].

== Overview ==

The tumulus features a keyhole shape, with the front mound oriented to the west along an east-west axis. Measuring approximately 140 meters in total length and 72 meters in diameter for the rear mound, it is the largest tumulus in Asuka Village. The mound is notable for its extensive use of [[:en:Fukiishi|fukiishi]] (covering stones). Surrounding the tumulus is a water-filled moat, which was extensively remodeled during the [[:en:Bunkyū|Bunkyū era]] restoration. Originally, the surrounding area was agricultural land. Some theories suggest that the restoration converted the tumulus from a double circular mound to its current keyhole shape.<ref>Theory proposed by {{ill|Kōichi Mori|ja|森浩一|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōichi Mori|qq=}}</ref>

The tumulus is part of a group of Kofuns, including {{ill|Kanazuka Kofun|lt=Kanazuka Kofun|lt=Hirata Iwaya Kofun|ja|平田岩屋古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirata Iwaya Kofun|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to カナヅカ古墳}}, {{ill|Oni-no-Manaita and Oni-no-Secchin|lt=Oni-no-Manaita and Oni-no-Secchin|lt=Oni no Manaita and Oni no Senkan|ja|鬼の俎・鬼の雪隠|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oni no Manaita and Oni no Senkan|qq=}}, and {{ill|Noguchi-ōsaka Kofun|lt=Noguchi-ōsaka Kofun|lt=Noguchi Royal Tomb|ja|野口王墓|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Noguchi Royal Tomb|qq=}} (the joint tomb of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|1=Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|1=Emperor Tenmu|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q335181}} and [[:en:Empress Jitō|Empress Jitō]]), all situated along the southern slope of the same hill.

== built ==

While believed to have been built during the late [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]], the exact construction date is uncertain due to the lack of archaeological excavations.

== Interred Individual ==

Although designated by the Imperial Household Agency as the tomb of [[:en:Emperor Kinmei|Emperor Kinmei]], many scholars suggest that the nearby {{ill|Mise Maruyama Kofun|ja|見瀬丸山古墳|1=Maruyama Kofun (Kashihara)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mise Maruyama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Maruyama Kofun (Kashihara)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 丸山古墳 (橿原市)}}, located approximately 800 meters northwest and the largest tumulus in Nara Prefecture, might be a more likely candidate. Other theories propose that [[:en:Soga no Iname|Soga no Iname]] may be the interred individual.

== Saruishi Statues ==

Adjacent to the {{ill|Tomb of Princess Kibi|ja|吉備姫王墓|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomb of Princess Kibi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, stone statues known as {{ill|Saruishi|ja|猿石|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saruishi|qq=}} ("monkey stones") are placed nearby. These statues were excavated during the Edo period from a field just south of Umeyama Kofun and relocated to their current location in the early [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji period]].

== References ==
{{Reflist}}

== Related Articles ==
* {{ill|Kanazuka Kofun|ja|カナヅカ古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanazuka Kofun|qq=}}

== External Links ==
{{Commonscat|Umeyama Kofun}}
* [https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/guide/029/index.html Hinokuma Sakaai no misasagi] - Imperial Household Agency

Surrounding the tumulus is a water-filled moat, which was extensively remodeled during the [[:en:Bunkyū|Bunkyū era]] restoration. Originally, the surrounding area was agricultural land. The tumulus is part of a group of Kofuns, including {{ill|Kanazuka Kofun|lt=Kanazuka Kofun|lt=Hirata Iwaya Kofun|ja|平田岩屋古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirata Iwaya Kofun|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to カナヅカ古墳}}, {{ill|Oni-no-Manaita and Oni-no-Secchin|lt=Oni-no-Manaita and Oni-no-Secchin|lt=Oni no Manaita and Oni no Senkan|ja|鬼の俎・鬼の雪隠|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oni no Manaita and Oni no Senkan|qq=}}, and {{ill|Noguchi-ōsaka Kofun|lt=Noguchi-ōsaka Kofun|lt=Noguchi Royal Tomb|ja|野口王墓|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Noguchi Royal Tomb|qq=}} (the joint tomb of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|1=Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|1=Emperor Tenmu|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q335181}} and [[:en:Empress Jitō|Empress Jitō]]), all situated along the southern slope of the same hill.

== built ==

While believed to have been built during the late [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]], the exact construction date is uncertain due to the lack of archaeological excavations.

== Interred Individual ==

Although designated by the Imperial Household Agency as the tomb of [[:en:Emperor Kinmei|Emperor Kinmei]], many scholars suggest that the nearby {{ill|Mise Maruyama Kofun|ja|見瀬丸山古墳|1=Maruyama Kofun (Kashihara)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mise Maruyama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Maruyama Kofun (Kashihara)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 丸山古墳 (橿原市)}}, located approximately 800 meters northwest and the largest tumulus in Nara Prefecture, might be a more likely candidate. Other theories propose that [[:en:Soga no Iname|Soga no Iname]] may be the interred individual.

== Saruishi Statues ==

Adjacent to the {{ill|Tomb of Princess Kibi|ja|吉備姫王墓|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomb of Princess Kibi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, stone statues known as {{ill|Saruishi|ja|猿石|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saruishi|qq=}} ("monkey stones") are placed nearby. These statues were excavated during the Edo period from a field just south of Umeyama Kofun and relocated to their current location in the early [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji period]].

The tumulus is part of a group of Kofuns, including {{ill|Kanazuka Kofun|lt=Kanazuka Kofun|lt=Hirata Iwaya Kofun|ja|平田岩屋古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirata Iwaya Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to カナヅカ古墳}}, {{ill|Oni-no-Manaita and Oni-no-Secchin|lt=Oni-no-Manaita and Oni-no-Secchin|lt=Oni no Manaita and Oni no Senkan|ja|鬼の俎・鬼の雪隠|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oni no Manaita and Oni no Senkan|qq=}}, and {{ill|Noguchi-ōsaka Kofun|lt=Noguchi-ōsaka Kofun|lt=Noguchi Royal Tomb|ja|野口王墓|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Noguchi Royal Tomb|qq=}} (the joint tomb of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|1=Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|1=Emperor Tenmu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q335181}} and [[:en:Empress Jitō|Empress Jitō]]), all situated along the southern slope of the same hill.

== built ==

While believed to have been built during the late [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]], the exact construction date is uncertain due to the lack of archaeological excavations.

== Interred Individual ==

Although designated by the Imperial Household Agency as the tomb of [[:en:Emperor Kinmei|Emperor Kinmei]], many scholars suggest that the nearby {{ill|Mise Maruyama Kofun|ja|見瀬丸山古墳|1=Maruyama Kofun (Kashihara)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mise Maruyama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Maruyama Kofun (Kashihara)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 丸山古墳 (橿原市)}}, located approximately 800 meters northwest and the largest tumulus in Nara Prefecture, might be a more likely candidate. Other theories propose that [[:en:Soga no Iname|Soga no Iname]] may be the interred individual.

== Saruishi Statues ==

Adjacent to the {{ill|Tomb of Princess Kibi|ja|吉備姫王墓|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomb of Princess Kibi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, stone statues known as {{ill|Saruishi|ja|猿石|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saruishi|qq=}} ("monkey stones") are placed nearby. These statues were excavated during the Edo period from a field just south of Umeyama Kofun and relocated to their current location in the early [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji period]].

Some theories suggest that the restoration converted the tumulus from a double circular mound to its current keyhole shape.<ref>Theory proposed by {{ill|Kōichi Mori|ja|森浩一|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōichi Mori|qq=}}</ref>

The tumulus is part of a group of Kofuns, including {{ill|Kanazuka Kofun|lt=Kanazuka Kofun|lt=Hirata Iwaya Kofun|ja|平田岩屋古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirata Iwaya Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to カナヅカ古墳}}, {{ill|Oni-no-Manaita and Oni-no-Secchin|lt=Oni-no-Manaita and Oni-no-Secchin|lt=Oni no Manaita and Oni no Senkan|ja|鬼の俎・鬼の雪隠|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oni no Manaita and Oni no Senkan|qq=}}, and {{ill|Noguchi-ōsaka Kofun|lt=Noguchi-ōsaka Kofun|lt=Noguchi Royal Tomb|ja|野口王墓|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Noguchi Royal Tomb|qq=}} (the joint tomb of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|1=Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|1=Emperor Tenmu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q335181}} and [[:en:Empress Jitō|Empress Jitō]]), all situated along the southern slope of the same hill.

== built ==

While believed to have been built during the late [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]], the exact construction date is uncertain due to the lack of archaeological excavations.

== Interred Individual ==

Although designated by the Imperial Household Agency as the tomb of [[:en:Emperor Kinmei|Emperor Kinmei]], many scholars suggest that the nearby {{ill|Mise Maruyama Kofun|ja|見瀬丸山古墳|1=Maruyama Kofun (Kashihara)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mise Maruyama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Maruyama Kofun (Kashihara)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 丸山古墳 (橿原市)}}, located approximately 800 meters northwest and the largest tumulus in Nara Prefecture, might be a more likely candidate. Other theories propose that [[:en:Soga no Iname|Soga no Iname]] may be the interred individual.

== Saruishi Statues ==

Adjacent to the {{ill|Tomb of Princess Kibi|ja|吉備姫王墓|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomb of Princess Kibi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, stone statues known as {{ill|Saruishi|ja|猿石|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saruishi|qq=}} ("monkey stones") are placed nearby. These statues were excavated during the Edo period from a field just south of Umeyama Kofun and relocated to their current location in the early [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji period]].

The tumulus is part of a group of Kofuns, including {{ill|Kanazuka Kofun|lt=Kanazuka Kofun|lt=Hirata Iwaya Kofun|ja|平田岩屋古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirata Iwaya Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to カナヅカ古墳}}, {{ill|Oni-no-Manaita and Oni-no-Secchin|lt=Oni-no-Manaita and Oni-no-Secchin|lt=Oni no Manaita and Oni no Senkan|ja|鬼の俎・鬼の雪隠|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oni no Manaita and Oni no Senkan|qq=}}, and {{ill|Noguchi-ōsaka Kofun|lt=Noguchi-ōsaka Kofun|lt=Noguchi Royal Tomb|ja|野口王墓|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Noguchi Royal Tomb|qq=}} (the joint tomb of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|1=Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|1=Emperor Tenmu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q335181}} and [[:en:Empress Jitō|Empress Jitō]]), all situated along the southern slope of the same hill.

== built ==

While believed to have been built during the late [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]], the exact construction date is uncertain due to the lack of archaeological excavations.

== Interred Individual ==

Although designated by the Imperial Household Agency as the tomb of [[:en:Emperor Kinmei|Emperor Kinmei]], many scholars suggest that the nearby {{ill|Mise Maruyama Kofun|ja|見瀬丸山古墳|1=Maruyama Kofun (Kashihara)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mise Maruyama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Maruyama Kofun (Kashihara)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 丸山古墳 (橿原市)}}, located approximately 800 meters northwest and the largest tumulus in Nara Prefecture, might be a more likely candidate. Other theories propose that [[:en:Soga no Iname|Soga no Iname]] may be the interred individual.

== Saruishi Statues ==

Adjacent to the {{ill|Tomb of Princess Kibi|ja|吉備姫王墓|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomb of Princess Kibi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, stone statues known as {{ill|Saruishi|ja|猿石|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saruishi|qq=}} ("monkey stones") are placed nearby. These statues were excavated during the Edo period from a field just south of Umeyama Kofun and relocated to their current location in the early [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji period]].

The actual interred individual is unknown, but it has been designated by the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] as the "Hinokuma Sakaai no misasagi" (檜隈坂合陵), attributed to [[:en:Emperor Kinmei|Emperor Kinmei]].

== Overview ==

The tumulus features a keyhole shape, with the front mound oriented to the west along an east-west axis. Measuring approximately 140 meters in total length and 72 meters in diameter for the rear mound, it is the largest tumulus in Asuka Village. The mound is notable for its extensive use of [[:en:Fukiishi|fukiishi]] (covering stones). Surrounding the tumulus is a water-filled moat, which was extensively remodeled during the [[:en:Bunkyū|Bunkyū era]] restoration. Originally, the surrounding area was agricultural land. Some theories suggest that the restoration converted the tumulus from a double circular mound to its current keyhole shape.<ref>Theory proposed by {{ill|Kōichi Mori|ja|森浩一|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōichi Mori|qq=}}</ref>

The tumulus is part of a group of Kofuns, including {{ill|Kanazuka Kofun|lt=Kanazuka Kofun|lt=Hirata Iwaya Kofun|ja|平田岩屋古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirata Iwaya Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to カナヅカ古墳}}, {{ill|Oni-no-Manaita and Oni-no-Secchin|lt=Oni-no-Manaita and Oni-no-Secchin|lt=Oni no Manaita and Oni no Senkan|ja|鬼の俎・鬼の雪隠|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oni no Manaita and Oni no Senkan|qq=}}, and {{ill|Noguchi-ōsaka Kofun|lt=Noguchi-ōsaka Kofun|lt=Noguchi Royal Tomb|ja|野口王墓|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Noguchi Royal Tomb|qq=}} (the joint tomb of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|1=Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|1=Emperor Tenmu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q335181}} and [[:en:Empress Jitō|Empress Jitō]]), all situated along the southern slope of the same hill.

== built ==

While believed to have been built during the late [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]], the exact construction date is uncertain due to the lack of archaeological excavations.

== Interred Individual ==

Although designated by the Imperial Household Agency as the tomb of [[:en:Emperor Kinmei|Emperor Kinmei]], many scholars suggest that the nearby {{ill|Mise Maruyama Kofun|ja|見瀬丸山古墳|1=Maruyama Kofun (Kashihara)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mise Maruyama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Maruyama Kofun (Kashihara)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 丸山古墳 (橿原市)}}, located approximately 800 meters northwest and the largest tumulus in Nara Prefecture, might be a more likely candidate. Other theories propose that [[:en:Soga no Iname|Soga no Iname]] may be the interred individual.

Adjacent to the {{ill|Tomb of Princess Kibi|ja|吉備姫王墓|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomb of Princess Kibi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, stone statues known as {{ill|Saruishi|ja|猿石|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saruishi|qq=}} ("monkey stones") are placed nearby. The tumulus is part of a group of Kofuns, including {{ill|Kanazuka Kofun|lt=Kanazuka Kofun|lt=Hirata Iwaya Kofun|ja|平田岩屋古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirata Iwaya Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to カナヅカ古墳}}, {{ill|Oni-no-Manaita and Oni-no-Secchin|lt=Oni-no-Manaita and Oni-no-Secchin|lt=Oni no Manaita and Oni no Senkan|ja|鬼の俎・鬼の雪隠|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oni no Manaita and Oni no Senkan|qq=}}, and {{ill|Noguchi-ōsaka Kofun|lt=Noguchi-ōsaka Kofun|lt=Noguchi Royal Tomb|ja|野口王墓|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Noguchi Royal Tomb|qq=}} (the joint tomb of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|1=Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|1=Emperor Tenmu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q335181}} and [[:en:Empress Jitō|Empress Jitō]]), all situated along the southern slope of the same hill.
== built ==
While believed to have been built during the late [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]], the exact construction date is uncertain due to the lack of archaeological excavations.
== Interred Individual ==
Although designated by the Imperial Household Agency as the tomb of [[:en:Emperor Kinmei|Emperor Kinmei]], many scholars suggest that the nearby {{ill|Mise Maruyama Kofun|ja|見瀬丸山古墳|1=Maruyama Kofun (Kashihara)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mise Maruyama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Maruyama Kofun (Kashihara)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 丸山古墳 (橿原市)}}, located approximately 800 meters northwest and the largest tumulus in Nara Prefecture, might be a more likely candidate. Other theories propose that [[:en:Soga no Iname|Soga no Iname]] may be the interred individual.
== Saruishi Statues ==
Adjacent to the {{ill|Tomb of Princess Kibi|ja|吉備姫王墓|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomb of Princess Kibi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, stone statues known as {{ill|Saruishi|ja|猿石|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saruishi|qq=}} ("monkey stones") are placed nearby. These statues were excavated during the Edo period from a field just south of Umeyama Kofun and relocated to their current location in the early [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji period]].

| name = Umeyama Kofun
| image = File:Umeyama Kofun, zenkei-2.jpg
| location = {{ill|Asuka|WD=Q752397}}
| coordinates = {{Coord|34.468717|N|135.800778|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}
| type = [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]]
| area = Total length: 140m
| Burial Facility = Unknown
| built = Late [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]]
| Interred_individual = ([[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] designation) [[:en:Emperor Kinmei|Emperor Kinmei]]
| excavations = Unknown
| Mausoleum = Designated by the Imperial Household Agency as "Hinokuma Sakaai no misasagi" (檜隈坂合陵)
| map = {{location map|Japan Nara|lat_deg=34|lat_min=28|lat_sec=7.38|lon_deg=135|lon_min=48|lon_sec=2.8|label=Umeyama Kofun|mark=前方後円墳-西.svg|marksize=23|caption=location in Nara Prefecture|position=bottom|width=200|float=center|border=none}}
| architectural_styles = {{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}}
| commemorates = {{ill|Kinmei|WD=Q329792}}
}}

[[File:Umeyama kohun aerial.jpg|thumb|220px|Bottom left: Kibi-hime Royal Tomb and Saruishi statues ({{National Land Aerial Photography}})]]
[[File:Umeyama Kofun, haisho.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Mausoleum of [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] located in [[:en:Asuka, Nara|Asuka, Nara]], Nara Prefecture.

The actual interred individual is unknown, but it has been designated by the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] as the "Hinokuma Sakaai no misasagi" (檜隈坂合陵), attributed to [[:en:Emperor Kinmei|Emperor Kinmei]].

== Overview ==

The tumulus features a keyhole shape, with the front mound oriented to the west along an east-west axis. Measuring approximately 140 meters in total length and 72 meters in diameter for the rear mound, it is the largest tumulus in Asuka Village. The mound is notable for its extensive use of [[:en:Fukiishi|fukiishi]] (covering stones). Surrounding the tumulus is a water-filled moat, which was extensively remodeled during the [[:en:Bunkyū|Bunkyū era]] restoration. Originally, the surrounding area was agricultural land. Some theories suggest that the restoration converted the tumulus from a double circular mound to its current keyhole shape.<ref>Theory proposed by {{ill|Kōichi Mori|ja|森浩一|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōichi Mori|qq=}}</ref>

The tumulus is part of a group of Kofuns, including {{ill|Kanazuka Kofun|lt=Kanazuka Kofun|lt=Hirata Iwaya Kofun|ja|平田岩屋古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirata Iwaya Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to カナヅカ古墳}}, {{ill|Oni-no-Manaita and Oni-no-Secchin|lt=Oni-no-Manaita and Oni-no-Secchin|lt=Oni no Manaita and Oni no Senkan|ja|鬼の俎・鬼の雪隠|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oni no Manaita and Oni no Senkan|qq=}}, and {{ill|Noguchi-ōsaka Kofun|lt=Noguchi-ōsaka Kofun|lt=Noguchi Royal Tomb|ja|野口王墓|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Noguchi Royal Tomb|qq=}} (the joint tomb of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|1=Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|1=Emperor Tenmu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q335181}} and [[:en:Empress Jitō|Empress Jitō]]), all situated along the southern slope of the same hill.

== built ==

While believed to have been built during the late [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]], the exact construction date is uncertain due to the lack of archaeological excavations.

== Interred Individual ==

Although designated by the Imperial Household Agency as the tomb of [[:en:Emperor Kinmei|Emperor Kinmei]], many scholars suggest that the nearby {{ill|Mise Maruyama Kofun|ja|見瀬丸山古墳|1=Maruyama Kofun (Kashihara)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mise Maruyama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Maruyama Kofun (Kashihara)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 丸山古墳 (橿原市)}}, located approximately 800 meters northwest and the largest tumulus in Nara Prefecture, might be a more likely candidate. Other theories propose that [[:en:Soga no Iname|Soga no Iname]] may be the interred individual.

== Saruishi Statues ==

Adjacent to the {{ill|Tomb of Princess Kibi|ja|吉備姫王墓|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomb of Princess Kibi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, stone statues known as {{ill|Saruishi|ja|猿石|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saruishi|qq=}} ("monkey stones") are placed nearby. These statues were excavated during the Edo period from a field just south of Umeyama Kofun and relocated to their current location in the early [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji period]].

The mound is notable for its extensive use of [[:en:Fukiishi|fukiishi]] (covering stones). Some theories suggest that the restoration converted the tumulus from a double circular mound to its current keyhole shape.<ref>Theory proposed by {{ill|Kōichi Mori|ja|森浩一|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōichi Mori|qq=}}</ref>

The tumulus is part of a group of Kofuns, including {{ill|Kanazuka Kofun|lt=Kanazuka Kofun|lt=Hirata Iwaya Kofun|ja|平田岩屋古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirata Iwaya Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to カナヅカ古墳}}, {{ill|Oni-no-Manaita and Oni-no-Secchin|lt=Oni-no-Manaita and Oni-no-Secchin|lt=Oni no Manaita and Oni no Senkan|ja|鬼の俎・鬼の雪隠|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oni no Manaita and Oni no Senkan|qq=}}, and {{ill|Noguchi-ōsaka Kofun|lt=Noguchi-ōsaka Kofun|lt=Noguchi Royal Tomb|ja|野口王墓|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Noguchi Royal Tomb|qq=}} (the joint tomb of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|1=Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|1=Emperor Tenmu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q335181}} and [[:en:Empress Jitō|Empress Jitō]]), all situated along the southern slope of the same hill.

== built ==

While believed to have been built during the late [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]], the exact construction date is uncertain due to the lack of archaeological excavations.

== Interred Individual ==

Although designated by the Imperial Household Agency as the tomb of [[:en:Emperor Kinmei|Emperor Kinmei]], many scholars suggest that the nearby {{ill|Mise Maruyama Kofun|ja|見瀬丸山古墳|1=Maruyama Kofun (Kashihara)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mise Maruyama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Maruyama Kofun (Kashihara)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 丸山古墳 (橿原市)}}, located approximately 800 meters northwest and the largest tumulus in Nara Prefecture, might be a more likely candidate. Other theories propose that [[:en:Soga no Iname|Soga no Iname]] may be the interred individual.

== Saruishi Statues ==

Adjacent to the {{ill|Tomb of Princess Kibi|ja|吉備姫王墓|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomb of Princess Kibi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, stone statues known as {{ill|Saruishi|ja|猿石|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saruishi|qq=}} ("monkey stones") are placed nearby. These statues were excavated during the Edo period from a field just south of Umeyama Kofun and relocated to their current location in the early [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji period]].

The tumulus is part of a group of Kofuns, including {{ill|Kanazuka Kofun|lt=Kanazuka Kofun|lt=Hirata Iwaya Kofun|ja|平田岩屋古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirata Iwaya Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to カナヅカ古墳}}, {{ill|Oni-no-Manaita and Oni-no-Secchin|lt=Oni-no-Manaita and Oni-no-Secchin|lt=Oni no Manaita and Oni no Senkan|ja|鬼の俎・鬼の雪隠|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oni no Manaita and Oni no Senkan|qq=}}, and {{ill|Noguchi-ōsaka Kofun|lt=Noguchi-ōsaka Kofun|lt=Noguchi Royal Tomb|ja|野口王墓|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Noguchi Royal Tomb|qq=}} (the joint tomb of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|1=Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|1=Emperor Tenmu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q335181}} and [[:en:Empress Jitō|Empress Jitō]]), all situated along the southern slope of the same hill.

== built ==

While believed to have been built during the late [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]], the exact construction date is uncertain due to the lack of archaeological excavations.

== Interred Individual ==

Although designated by the Imperial Household Agency as the tomb of [[:en:Emperor Kinmei|Emperor Kinmei]], many scholars suggest that the nearby {{ill|Mise Maruyama Kofun|ja|見瀬丸山古墳|1=Maruyama Kofun (Kashihara)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mise Maruyama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Maruyama Kofun (Kashihara)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 丸山古墳 (橿原市)}}, located approximately 800 meters northwest and the largest tumulus in Nara Prefecture, might be a more likely candidate. Other theories propose that [[:en:Soga no Iname|Soga no Iname]] may be the interred individual.

== Saruishi Statues ==

Adjacent to the {{ill|Tomb of Princess Kibi|ja|吉備姫王墓|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomb of Princess Kibi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, stone statues known as {{ill|Saruishi|ja|猿石|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saruishi|qq=}} ("monkey stones") are placed nearby. These statues were excavated during the Edo period from a field just south of Umeyama Kofun and relocated to their current location in the early [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji period]].

Some theories suggest that the restoration converted the tumulus from a double circular mound to its current keyhole shape.<ref>Theory proposed by {{ill|Kōichi Mori|ja|森浩一|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōichi Mori|qq=}}</ref>

While believed to have been built during the late [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]], the exact construction date is uncertain due to the lack of archaeological excavations.

Although designated by the Imperial Household Agency as the tomb of [[:en:Emperor Kinmei|Emperor Kinmei]], many scholars suggest that the nearby {{ill|Mise Maruyama Kofun|ja|見瀬丸山古墳|1=Maruyama Kofun (Kashihara)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mise Maruyama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Maruyama Kofun (Kashihara)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 丸山古墳 (橿原市)}}, located approximately 800 meters northwest and the largest tumulus in Nara Prefecture, might be a more likely candidate. The tumulus is part of a group of Kofuns, including {{ill|Kanazuka Kofun|lt=Kanazuka Kofun|lt=Hirata Iwaya Kofun|ja|平田岩屋古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirata Iwaya Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to カナヅカ古墳}}, {{ill|Oni-no-Manaita and Oni-no-Secchin|lt=Oni-no-Manaita and Oni-no-Secchin|lt=Oni no Manaita and Oni no Senkan|ja|鬼の俎・鬼の雪隠|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oni no Manaita and Oni no Senkan|qq=}}, and {{ill|Noguchi-ōsaka Kofun|lt=Noguchi-ōsaka Kofun|lt=Noguchi Royal Tomb|ja|野口王墓|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Noguchi Royal Tomb|qq=}} (the joint tomb of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|1=Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|1=Emperor Tenmu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q335181}} and [[:en:Empress Jitō|Empress Jitō]]), all situated along the southern slope of the same hill.

== built ==

While believed to have been built during the late [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]], the exact construction date is uncertain due to the lack of archaeological excavations.

== Interred Individual ==

Although designated by the Imperial Household Agency as the tomb of [[:en:Emperor Kinmei|Emperor Kinmei]], many scholars suggest that the nearby {{ill|Mise Maruyama Kofun|ja|見瀬丸山古墳|1=Maruyama Kofun (Kashihara)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mise Maruyama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Maruyama Kofun (Kashihara)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 丸山古墳 (橿原市)}}, located approximately 800 meters northwest and the largest tumulus in Nara Prefecture, might be a more likely candidate. Other theories propose that [[:en:Soga no Iname|Soga no Iname]] may be the interred individual.

== Saruishi Statues ==

Adjacent to the {{ill|Tomb of Princess Kibi|ja|吉備姫王墓|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomb of Princess Kibi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, stone statues known as {{ill|Saruishi|ja|猿石|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saruishi|qq=}} ("monkey stones") are placed nearby. These statues were excavated during the Edo period from a field just south of Umeyama Kofun and relocated to their current location in the early [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji period]].

The tumulus is part of a group of Kofuns, including {{ill|Kanazuka Kofun|lt=Kanazuka Kofun|lt=Hirata Iwaya Kofun|ja|平田岩屋古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirata Iwaya Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to カナヅカ古墳}}, {{ill|Oni-no-Manaita and Oni-no-Secchin|lt=Oni-no-Manaita and Oni-no-Secchin|lt=Oni no Manaita and Oni no Senkan|ja|鬼の俎・鬼の雪隠|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oni no Manaita and Oni no Senkan|qq=}}, and {{ill|Noguchi-ōsaka Kofun|lt=Noguchi-ōsaka Kofun|lt=Noguchi Royal Tomb|ja|野口王墓|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Noguchi Royal Tomb|qq=}} (the joint tomb of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|1=Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|1=Emperor Tenmu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q335181}} and [[:en:Empress Jitō|Empress Jitō]]), all situated along the southern slope of the same hill.

== built ==

While believed to have been built during the late [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]], the exact construction date is uncertain due to the lack of archaeological excavations.

== Interred Individual ==

Although designated by the Imperial Household Agency as the tomb of [[:en:Emperor Kinmei|Emperor Kinmei]], many scholars suggest that the nearby {{ill|Mise Maruyama Kofun|ja|見瀬丸山古墳|1=Maruyama Kofun (Kashihara)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mise Maruyama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Maruyama Kofun (Kashihara)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 丸山古墳 (橿原市)}}, located approximately 800 meters northwest and the largest tumulus in Nara Prefecture, might be a more likely candidate. Other theories propose that [[:en:Soga no Iname|Soga no Iname]] may be the interred individual.

== Saruishi Statues ==

Adjacent to the {{ill|Tomb of Princess Kibi|ja|吉備姫王墓|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomb of Princess Kibi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, stone statues known as {{ill|Saruishi|ja|猿石|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saruishi|qq=}} ("monkey stones") are placed nearby. These statues were excavated during the Edo period from a field just south of Umeyama Kofun and relocated to their current location in the early [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji period]].

The actual interred individual is unknown, but it has been designated by the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] as the "Hinokuma Sakaai no misasagi" (檜隈坂合陵), attributed to [[:en:Emperor Kinmei|Emperor Kinmei]].

== Overview ==

The tumulus features a keyhole shape, with the front mound oriented to the west along an east-west axis. Measuring approximately 140 meters in total length and 72 meters in diameter for the rear mound, it is the largest tumulus in Asuka Village. The mound is notable for its extensive use of [[:en:Fukiishi|fukiishi]] (covering stones). Surrounding the tumulus is a water-filled moat, which was extensively remodeled during the [[:en:Bunkyū|Bunkyū era]] restoration. Originally, the surrounding area was agricultural land. Some theories suggest that the restoration converted the tumulus from a double circular mound to its current keyhole shape.<ref>Theory proposed by {{ill|Kōichi Mori|ja|森浩一|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōichi Mori|qq=}}</ref>

The tumulus is part of a group of Kofuns, including {{ill|Kanazuka Kofun|lt=Kanazuka Kofun|lt=Hirata Iwaya Kofun|ja|平田岩屋古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirata Iwaya Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to カナヅカ古墳}}, {{ill|Oni-no-Manaita and Oni-no-Secchin|lt=Oni-no-Manaita and Oni-no-Secchin|lt=Oni no Manaita and Oni no Senkan|ja|鬼の俎・鬼の雪隠|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oni no Manaita and Oni no Senkan|qq=}}, and {{ill|Noguchi-ōsaka Kofun|lt=Noguchi-ōsaka Kofun|lt=Noguchi Royal Tomb|ja|野口王墓|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Noguchi Royal Tomb|qq=}} (the joint tomb of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|1=Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|1=Emperor Tenmu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q335181}} and [[:en:Empress Jitō|Empress Jitō]]), all situated along the southern slope of the same hill.

== built ==

While believed to have been built during the late [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]], the exact construction date is uncertain due to the lack of archaeological excavations.

== Interred Individual ==

Although designated by the Imperial Household Agency as the tomb of [[:en:Emperor Kinmei|Emperor Kinmei]], many scholars suggest that the nearby {{ill|Mise Maruyama Kofun|ja|見瀬丸山古墳|1=Maruyama Kofun (Kashihara)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mise Maruyama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Maruyama Kofun (Kashihara)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 丸山古墳 (橿原市)}}, located approximately 800 meters northwest and the largest tumulus in Nara Prefecture, might be a more likely candidate. Other theories propose that [[:en:Soga no Iname|Soga no Iname]] may be the interred individual.

== Saruishi Statues ==

Adjacent to the {{ill|Tomb of Princess Kibi|ja|吉備姫王墓|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomb of Princess Kibi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, stone statues known as {{ill|Saruishi|ja|猿石|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saruishi|qq=}} ("monkey stones") are placed nearby. These statues were excavated during the Edo period from a field just south of Umeyama Kofun and relocated to their current location in the early [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji period]].

Originally, the surrounding area was agricultural land. Some theories suggest that the restoration converted the tumulus from a double circular mound to its current keyhole shape.<ref>Theory proposed by {{ill|Kōichi Mori|ja|森浩一|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōichi Mori|qq=}}</ref>

The tumulus is part of a group of Kofuns, including {{ill|Kanazuka Kofun|lt=Kanazuka Kofun|lt=Hirata Iwaya Kofun|ja|平田岩屋古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirata Iwaya Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to カナヅカ古墳}}, {{ill|Oni-no-Manaita and Oni-no-Secchin|lt=Oni-no-Manaita and Oni-no-Secchin|lt=Oni no Manaita and Oni no Senkan|ja|鬼の俎・鬼の雪隠|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oni no Manaita and Oni no Senkan|qq=}}, and {{ill|Noguchi-ōsaka Kofun|lt=Noguchi-ōsaka Kofun|lt=Noguchi Royal Tomb|ja|野口王墓|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Noguchi Royal Tomb|qq=}} (the joint tomb of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|1=Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|1=Emperor Tenmu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q335181}} and {{ill|Empress Jitō|en|Empress Jitō|WD=Q232026|lt=Empress Jitō}}), all situated along the southern slope of the same hill.

== built ==

While believed to have been built during the late {{ill|Kofun period|en|Kofun period|WD=Q459939|lt=Kofun period}}, the exact construction date is uncertain due to the lack of archaeological excavations.

== Interred Individual ==

Although designated by the Imperial Household Agency as the tomb of {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|en|Emperor Kinmei|WD=Q329792|lt=Emperor Kinmei}}, many scholars suggest that the nearby {{ill|Mise Maruyama Kofun|ja|見瀬丸山古墳|1=Maruyama Kofun (Kashihara)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mise Maruyama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Maruyama Kofun (Kashihara)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 丸山古墳 (橿原市)}}, located approximately 800 meters northwest and the largest tumulus in Nara Prefecture, might be a more likely candidate. Other theories propose that {{ill|Soga no Iname|en|Soga no Iname|WD=Q595897|lt=Soga no Iname}} may be the interred individual.

== Saruishi Statues ==

Adjacent to the {{ill|Tomb of Princess Kibi|ja|吉備姫王墓|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomb of Princess Kibi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, stone statues known as {{ill|Saruishi|ja|猿石|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saruishi|qq=}} ("monkey stones") are placed nearby. These statues were excavated during the Edo period from a field just south of Umeyama Kofun and relocated to their current location in the early {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji period}}.
