The actual interred individual is unknown, but it has been designated by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|en|Imperial Household Agency|WD=Q1143602|lt=Imperial Household Agency}} as the "Hinokuma Sakaai no misasagi" (檜隈坂合陵), attributed to {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|en|Emperor Kinmei|WD=Q329792|lt=Emperor Kinmei}}.

== Overview ==

The tumulus features a keyhole shape, with the front mound oriented to the west along an east-west axis. Measuring approximately 140 meters in total length and 72 meters in diameter for the rear mound, it is the largest tumulus in Asuka Village. The mound is notable for its extensive use of {{ill|Fukiishi|en|Fukiishi|WD=Q11621213|lt=fukiishi}} (covering stones). Surrounding the tumulus is a water-filled moat, which was extensively remodeled during the {{ill|Bunkyū|en|Bunkyū|WD=Q730266|lt=Bunkyū era}} restoration. Originally, the surrounding area was agricultural land. Some theories suggest that the restoration converted the tumulus from a double circular mound to its current keyhole shape.<ref>Theory proposed by {{ill|Kōichi Mori|ja|森浩一|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōichi Mori|qq=}}</ref>

The tumulus is part of a group of Kofuns, including {{ill|Kanazuka Kofun|lt=Kanazuka Kofun|lt=Hirata Iwaya Kofun|ja|平田岩屋古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirata Iwaya Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to カナヅカ古墳}}, {{ill|Oni-no-Manaita and Oni-no-Secchin|lt=Oni-no-Manaita and Oni-no-Secchin|lt=Oni no Manaita and Oni no Senkan|ja|鬼の俎・鬼の雪隠|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oni no Manaita and Oni no Senkan|qq=}}, and {{ill|Noguchi-ōsaka Kofun|lt=Noguchi-ōsaka Kofun|lt=Noguchi Royal Tomb|ja|野口王墓|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Noguchi Royal Tomb|qq=}} (the joint tomb of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|1=Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|1=Emperor Tenmu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q335181}} and {{ill|Empress Jitō|en|Empress Jitō|WD=Q232026|lt=Empress Jitō}}), all situated along the southern slope of the same hill.

== built ==

While believed to have been built during the late {{ill|Kofun period|en|Kofun period|WD=Q459939|lt=Kofun period}}, the exact construction date is uncertain due to the lack of archaeological excavations.

== Interred Individual ==

Although designated by the Imperial Household Agency as the tomb of {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|en|Emperor Kinmei|WD=Q329792|lt=Emperor Kinmei}}, many scholars suggest that the nearby {{ill|Mise Maruyama Kofun|ja|見瀬丸山古墳|1=Maruyama Kofun (Kashihara)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mise Maruyama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Maruyama Kofun (Kashihara)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 丸山古墳 (橿原市)}}, located approximately 800 meters northwest and the largest tumulus in Nara Prefecture, might be a more likely candidate. Other theories propose that {{ill|Soga no Iname|en|Soga no Iname|WD=Q595897|lt=Soga no Iname}} may be the interred individual.

== Saruishi Statues ==

Adjacent to the {{ill|Tomb of Princess Kibi|ja|吉備姫王墓|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomb of Princess Kibi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, stone statues known as {{ill|Saruishi|ja|猿石|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saruishi|qq=}} ("monkey stones") are placed nearby. These statues were excavated during the Edo period from a field just south of Umeyama Kofun and relocated to their current location in the early {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji period}}.

Surrounding the tumulus is a water-filled moat, which was extensively remodeled during the {{ill|Bunkyū|en|Bunkyū|WD=Q730266|lt=Bunkyū era|1=Bunkyū}} restoration. Originally, the surrounding area was agricultural land. Some theories suggest that the restoration converted the tumulus from a double circular mound to its current keyhole shape.<ref>Theory proposed by {{ill|Kōichi Mori|ja|森浩一|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōichi Mori|qq=}}</ref>

The tumulus is part of a group of Kofuns, including {{ill|Kanazuka Kofun|lt=Kanazuka Kofun|lt=Hirata Iwaya Kofun|ja|平田岩屋古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirata Iwaya Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to カナヅカ古墳}}, {{ill|Oni-no-Manaita and Oni-no-Secchin|lt=Oni-no-Manaita and Oni-no-Secchin|lt=Oni no Manaita and Oni no Senkan|ja|鬼の俎・鬼の雪隠|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oni no Manaita and Oni no Senkan|qq=}}, and {{ill|Noguchi-ōsaka Kofun|lt=Noguchi-ōsaka Kofun|lt=Noguchi Royal Tomb|ja|野口王墓|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Noguchi Royal Tomb|qq=}} (the joint tomb of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|1=Emperor Tenmu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q335181}} and {{ill|Empress Jitō|en|Empress Jitō|WD=Q232026|lt=Empress Jitō|1=Empress Jitō}}), all situated along the southern slope of the same hill.

== built ==

While believed to have been built during the late {{ill|Kofun period|en|Kofun period|WD=Q459939|lt=Kofun period|1=Kofun period}}, the exact construction date is uncertain due to the lack of archaeological excavations.

== Interred Individual ==

Although designated by the Imperial Household Agency as the tomb of {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|en|Emperor Kinmei|WD=Q329792|lt=Emperor Kinmei|1=Emperor Kinmei}}, many scholars suggest that the nearby {{ill|Mise Maruyama Kofun|ja|見瀬丸山古墳|1=Maruyama Kofun (Kashihara)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mise Maruyama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Maruyama Kofun (Kashihara)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 丸山古墳 (橿原市)}}, located approximately 800 meters northwest and the largest tumulus in Nara Prefecture, might be a more likely candidate. Other theories propose that {{ill|Soga no Iname|en|Soga no Iname|WD=Q595897|lt=Soga no Iname|1=Soga no Iname}} may be the interred individual.

== Saruishi Statues ==

Adjacent to the {{ill|Tomb of Princess Kibi|ja|吉備姫王墓|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomb of Princess Kibi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, stone statues known as {{ill|Saruishi|ja|猿石|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saruishi|qq=}} ("monkey stones") are placed nearby. These statues were excavated during the Edo period from a field just south of Umeyama Kofun and relocated to their current location in the early {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji period|1=Meiji era}}.

Some theories suggest that the restoration converted the tumulus from a double circular mound to its current keyhole shape.<ref>Theory proposed by {{ill|Kōichi Mori|ja|森浩一|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōichi Mori|qq=}}</ref>

The tumulus is part of a group of Kofuns, including {{ill|Kanazuka Kofun|lt=Kanazuka Kofun|lt=Hirata Iwaya Kofun|ja|平田岩屋古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirata Iwaya Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to カナヅカ古墳}}, {{ill|Oni-no-Manaita and Oni-no-Secchin|lt=Oni-no-Manaita and Oni-no-Secchin|lt=Oni no Manaita and Oni no Senkan|ja|鬼の俎・鬼の雪隠|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oni no Manaita and Oni no Senkan|qq=}}, and {{ill|Noguchi-ōsaka Kofun|lt=Noguchi-ōsaka Kofun|lt=Noguchi Royal Tomb|ja|野口王墓|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Noguchi Royal Tomb|qq=}} (the joint tomb of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|1=Emperor Tenmu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q335181}} and {{ill|Empress Jitō|en|Empress Jitō|WD=Q232026|lt=Empress Jitō|1=Empress Jitō}}), all situated along the southern slope of the same hill.

== built ==

While believed to have been built during the late {{ill|Kofun period|en|Kofun period|WD=Q459939|lt=Kofun period|1=Kofun period}}, the exact construction date is uncertain due to the lack of archaeological excavations.

== Interred Individual ==

Although designated by the Imperial Household Agency as the tomb of {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|en|Emperor Kinmei|WD=Q329792|lt=Emperor Kinmei|1=Emperor Kinmei}}, many scholars suggest that the nearby {{ill|Mise Maruyama Kofun|ja|見瀬丸山古墳|1=Maruyama Kofun (Kashihara)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mise Maruyama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Maruyama Kofun (Kashihara)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 丸山古墳 (橿原市)}}, located approximately 800 meters northwest and the largest tumulus in Nara Prefecture, might be a more likely candidate. Other theories propose that {{ill|Soga no Iname|en|Soga no Iname|WD=Q595897|lt=Soga no Iname|1=Soga no Iname}} may be the interred individual.

== Saruishi Statues ==

Adjacent to the {{ill|Tomb of Princess Kibi|ja|吉備姫王墓|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomb of Princess Kibi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, stone statues known as {{ill|Saruishi|ja|猿石|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saruishi|qq=}} ("monkey stones") are placed nearby. These statues were excavated during the Edo period from a field just south of Umeyama Kofun and relocated to their current location in the early {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji period|1=Meiji era}}.

The tumulus is part of a group of Kofuns, including {{ill|Kanazuka Kofun|lt=Kanazuka Kofun|lt=Hirata Iwaya Kofun|ja|平田岩屋古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirata Iwaya Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to カナヅカ古墳}}, {{ill|Oni-no-Manaita and Oni-no-Secchin|lt=Oni-no-Manaita and Oni-no-Secchin|lt=Oni no Manaita and Oni no Senkan|ja|鬼の俎・鬼の雪隠|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oni no Manaita and Oni no Senkan|qq=}}, and {{ill|Noguchi-ōsaka Kofun|lt=Noguchi-ōsaka Kofun|lt=Noguchi Royal Tomb|ja|野口王墓|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Noguchi Royal Tomb|qq=}} (the joint tomb of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|1=Emperor Tenmu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q335181}} and {{ill|Empress Jitō|en|Empress Jitō|WD=Q232026|lt=Empress Jitō|1=Empress Jitō}}), all situated along the southern slope of the same hill.

== built ==

While believed to have been built during the late {{ill|Kofun period|en|Kofun period|WD=Q459939|lt=Kofun period|1=Kofun period}}, the exact construction date is uncertain due to the lack of archaeological excavations.

== Interred Individual ==

Although designated by the Imperial Household Agency as the tomb of {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|en|Emperor Kinmei|WD=Q329792|lt=Emperor Kinmei|1=Emperor Kinmei}}, many scholars suggest that the nearby {{ill|Mise Maruyama Kofun|ja|見瀬丸山古墳|1=Maruyama Kofun (Kashihara)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mise Maruyama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Maruyama Kofun (Kashihara)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 丸山古墳 (橿原市)}}, located approximately 800 meters northwest and the largest tumulus in Nara Prefecture, might be a more likely candidate. Other theories propose that {{ill|Soga no Iname|en|Soga no Iname|WD=Q595897|lt=Soga no Iname|1=Soga no Iname}} may be the interred individual.

== Saruishi Statues ==

Adjacent to the {{ill|Tomb of Princess Kibi|ja|吉備姫王墓|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomb of Princess Kibi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, stone statues known as {{ill|Saruishi|ja|猿石|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saruishi|qq=}} ("monkey stones") are placed nearby. These statues were excavated during the Edo period from a field just south of Umeyama Kofun and relocated to their current location in the early {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji period|1=Meiji era}}.

[[File:Umeyama kohun aerial.jpg|thumb|220px|Bottom left: Kibi-hime Royal Tomb and Saruishi statues ({{National Land Aerial Photography}})]]
[[File:Umeyama Kofun, haisho.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Mausoleum of {{ill|Zenpō-kōen-fun|en|Zenpō-kōen-fun|WD=Q11268718|lt=Zenpō-kōen-fun|1=Zenpō-kōen-fun}} located in {{ill|Asuka, Nara|en|Asuka, Nara|WD=Q752397|lt=Asuka, Nara|1=Asuka, Nara}}, Nara Prefecture.

The actual interred individual is unknown, but it has been designated by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|en|Imperial Household Agency|WD=Q1143602|lt=Imperial Household Agency|1=Imperial Household Agency}} as the "Hinokuma Sakaai no misasagi" (檜隈坂合陵), attributed to {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|en|Emperor Kinmei|WD=Q329792|lt=Emperor Kinmei|1=Emperor Kinmei}}.

== Overview ==

The tumulus features a keyhole shape, with the front mound oriented to the west along an east-west axis. Measuring approximately 140 meters in total length and 72 meters in diameter for the rear mound, it is the largest tumulus in Asuka Village. The mound is notable for its extensive use of {{ill|Fukiishi|en|Fukiishi|WD=Q11621213|lt=fukiishi|1=Fukiishi}} (covering stones). Surrounding the tumulus is a water-filled moat, which was extensively remodeled during the {{ill|Bunkyū|en|Bunkyū|WD=Q730266|lt=Bunkyū era|1=Bunkyū}} restoration. Originally, the surrounding area was agricultural land. Some theories suggest that the restoration converted the tumulus from a double circular mound to its current keyhole shape.<ref>Theory proposed by {{ill|Kōichi Mori|ja|森浩一|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōichi Mori|qq=}}</ref>

The tumulus is part of a group of Kofuns, including {{ill|Kanazuka Kofun|lt=Kanazuka Kofun|lt=Hirata Iwaya Kofun|ja|平田岩屋古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirata Iwaya Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to カナヅカ古墳}}, {{ill|Oni-no-Manaita and Oni-no-Secchin|lt=Oni-no-Manaita and Oni-no-Secchin|lt=Oni no Manaita and Oni no Senkan|ja|鬼の俎・鬼の雪隠|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oni no Manaita and Oni no Senkan|qq=}}, and {{ill|Noguchi-ōsaka Kofun|lt=Noguchi-ōsaka Kofun|lt=Noguchi Royal Tomb|ja|野口王墓|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Noguchi Royal Tomb|qq=}} (the joint tomb of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|1=Emperor Tenmu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q335181}} and {{ill|Empress Jitō|en|Empress Jitō|WD=Q232026|lt=Empress Jitō|1=Empress Jitō}}), all situated along the southern slope of the same hill.

== built ==

While believed to have been built during the late {{ill|Kofun period|en|Kofun period|WD=Q459939|lt=Kofun period|1=Kofun period}}, the exact construction date is uncertain due to the lack of archaeological excavations.

== Interred Individual ==

Although designated by the Imperial Household Agency as the tomb of {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|en|Emperor Kinmei|WD=Q329792|lt=Emperor Kinmei|1=Emperor Kinmei}}, many scholars suggest that the nearby {{ill|Mise Maruyama Kofun|ja|見瀬丸山古墳|1=Maruyama Kofun (Kashihara)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mise Maruyama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Maruyama Kofun (Kashihara)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 丸山古墳 (橿原市)}}, located approximately 800 meters northwest and the largest tumulus in Nara Prefecture, might be a more likely candidate. Other theories propose that {{ill|Soga no Iname|en|Soga no Iname|WD=Q595897|lt=Soga no Iname|1=Soga no Iname}} may be the interred individual.

== Saruishi Statues ==

Adjacent to the {{ill|Tomb of Princess Kibi|ja|吉備姫王墓|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomb of Princess Kibi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, stone statues known as {{ill|Saruishi|ja|猿石|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saruishi|qq=}} ("monkey stones") are placed nearby. These statues were excavated during the Edo period from a field just south of Umeyama Kofun and relocated to their current location in the early {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji period|1=Meiji era}}.
